DOWSE, THOMAS ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; educ. at Epsom; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P., (Lond.); D.P.H. (Camb.); mem. R. Inst. of Pub. Health; formerly hse.-surg. and res. obstetrical offr., Charing Cross hosp., London; civ. surg., Roy. Mil. hosp., Colchester; surg. to Roy. Nat. Miss. to Deep Sea Fishermen; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1902-8; port. med. offr., Levuka, Fiji; dist. comsrr., and govt. med. offr., Cockburn Harbour, Turks Island, 1909.

DOWER, EDWARD EBENEZER.—Ck., ch. mag.'s off., Kokstad, C. Colony, Oct., 1891; Matatiele, Jan., 1896; nat. affairs offr., Aug., 1890; prin. clk., 1900; ch. clk., July, 1901; also acctng. offr., Prime Minister's dept., July, 1904; ast. sec. to nat. affairs dept., July, 1906; sec. to ditto, Sept., 1907; head of dept. of native affairs, Union of South Africa, 1910.

DOWNER, THE HON. SIR J. W., K.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.—B. 1844; att.-gen., S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884, and att. gen. and premier, 1885-7; del. to col. conf., 1887; ch. sec. and premier, 1892-3; mem. of senate, Australian parl., 1901-1903; mem. leg. coun., S. Aust., 1905.

DOWNES, MAJOR-GEN. M. FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1885), late R.A.—B. 1834; served in the Crimea till June, 1856, medal and clasp and Turkish medal; instructor of fortif., R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery in Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instructor of gunnery at Woolwich five years; col.-comdt., S. Australia, June, 1877; mem. of roy. coun. on defences, Sydney, 1881; sec.of defence, Victoria, Apr., 1885, to Mar., 1888; comdt., S. Australian forces, Apr., 1888-92; ret. 1902.

DOYLE, EDWARD ANGEL GAYNES.—B. 1859; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.; obstet. soc., Lond.; mem. Br. med. assoc.; late astt. house surg., Westminster hosp.; joined med. service, Trinidad, 1884; ag. dist. med. offr., 1886; res. surg., San Fernando hosp., Nov., 1892; ag. res. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1893; res. surg., ditto, 1897; D.M.O., S. Nararima, 1906.

DRAKE, THE HON. JAS. GEORGE.—Mem. leg. ass., Queensland, 1888, 1893, 1896, 1899; M.L.C., 12th Dec., 1899; sec. for pub. instr. and P.M.G., Queensland, Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; senator for Queensland, Commonwealth parl., Mar., 1901; P.M.G., Commonwealth, Feb., 1901, to Aug., 1903; min. of def., Aug.-Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., Sept., 1903; vice-pres. of ex. coun., 1904; ret., 1906.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, RALPH EVELYN.—B. 1875; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, Dulwich Coll. and St. George's hosp.: open scholarship, St. George's hosp., 1st year's proficiency prize and Pollock prize; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); apptd. surg.-capt., attached to railway pioneer regt., S.A.F.F., Dec., 1899 (medal and 2 clasps); med. offr., Uganda Prot., 26th Sept., 1900; Nandi expedit., 1900 (medal and clasp); Suk-Turkana expedit., 1901 (ment. in despatch, spec. ment. by Sir H. Johnston, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., recd. appreciation of serv. from S. of S. for Foreign Affairs); med. offr., Somaliland Prot., May, 1904; recd. appreciation from S. of S. for the Colonies for serv. during smallpox epidemic in Berbera, Somaliland Prot., 1904-5; passed in Somal language, Dec., 1905.

DRAYSON, MATTHEW JOHN.—B. 1865; Apptd. after compet. exam., a clerk in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1883; ast. regisr., May, 1892; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., July, 1896; seconded for service as chief clerk, col. sec.'s off., Hong Kong 11th Jan., 1902; acted as clk. of coun., Aug., 1902; retd. to C.O. as dep. ch. regisr., 22nd July, 1906.

DRAYTON, EDWARD RAWLE, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; clk. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; 2nd clk. in office of gov.-in-chief of the Windward Is., July, 1879; ag. ch. clk., May, 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apr., 1883; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Browne, July to Oct., 1884; provost-marshal, Grenada, Apr., 1885; ag. col. regisr., Sept., 1885; regist. sup. ct., J.P., July, 1886; mem. legis. coun., May, 1887; inspr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1888; treas., May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; col. sec. and regist-gen., June, 1892; admstd. govt. of Grenada whenever gov. absent, 1890-1907; govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1893, Feb. to Sept., 1897, and Feb., 1900, to Apr., 1901; and of Windward Is., Jan., 1897, and Oct., 1907; delegate to W. Indian quarantine conf., Apr., 1904; deleg. to Canadian trade conf., Jan., 1908.
DREW, DESMOND, B.A.—Trin. Coll., Dublin, 1908; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., Dublin Univ., 1909; med. offr., Nyasaland Prot., Feb., 1911.
DRUMMOND, ROBERT, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1887; ed. at Glasgow Univ. and Univ. Coll. Hosp., London; med. offr., Nyasaland Prot., Feb., 1911.

DRURY, EDWARD HERBERT MERIVALE.—Trinity Hall, Camb., 1890; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1896; priv. sec. to Hon. Sir R. G. W. Herbert, G.C.B., Mar., 1899, to June, 1900; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir C. A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., Windward Is., July, 1900; ditto, Trinidad, Dec., 1900, to July, 1902; admitted to practice at Trinidad bar, July, 1902; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, St. Helena, Oct., 1902; emigrn. agt., Oct., 1902; clk. to ex. coun., Apr., 1903; J.P., Nov., 1902; schl. attndce. offr., Aug., 1904; collr. of govt. rents and taxes, Jan., 1903, to Dec., 1906; ast. stip. mag., Nov., 1904; clk. to res. comsrr., Bech. Prot., Jan., 1907; ag. ch. clk. and regist., June, 1907; transfd. temporarily as 2nd clk. to astt. comsrr., Southern dist., Bech. Prot., 15th Feb., 1910; J.P., S. dist., Bech. Prot., 26th Aug., 1910; ag. astt. res. mag., Mar., 1911.

DRYSDALE, ARTHUR, K.C.—B.1857; ed. public schools and priv. acad., Colchester and Hants counties, Nova Scotia; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1895; elec. to Nova Scotia legis., 1891; re-elec., 1894, 1897, 1901, 1906; comsrr. of pub. works and mines, 1901 to 1905; attorney-general, Nova Scotia, 1905 to 1907; just. of the supreme ct., N.S., 1907.

DU BOULAY, ED.—Ag. clk. to audr. and treas., St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1890; 4th clk., treas., Mar. to May, 1892; 3rd clk., May to Dec., 1892; 2nd clk., treas., and acctn., Jan., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., treas., Feb. to Nov., 1893; audit. clk., Apr., 1894; ag. audr., St. Lucia, Mar. to Sept., 1901; attached to Somerset house for service in W. African colonies, Dec., 1901; local auditor, S. Leone, 24th Apr., 1903.

DUDER, T. C.—J.P., Newfoundland, 1874; M.H.A., Fogo, since 1893; financial sec., 1894; chmn. bd. of wks., 1894; survr.-gen. and chmn. bd. of agricul., 1897; min. agricul. and mines, 1898; stip. mag., Bonne Bay, 1901.

DUDLEY, RT. HON. EARL OF, P.C. (1902); G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1908); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1807; L.C.C., a D.L. and co. ald. for Worcester; Lord High Steward of Kidderminster; major, Worcester, I.Y.; served in S. Africa, 1900; parly. sec. to B. of T., 1895-1902; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1905; gov.-gen. and comdr.-in-chief of Commonwealth of Australia, 1908 to 1911.

DUFF, HECTOR LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1872; apptd. by F.O., asst. collr., B.C. Africa, 1897; ag. collr., dist. of W. Nyasa, 1896; Zomba, 1899; resig. from serv., 1903; re-apptd., Apr., 1904; collr., Manji dist., May, 1904; ag. collr., Blantyre dist., 1905; res. of Manji, Apr., 1906; ag. res., Blantyre, Feb., 1907; seconded, Feb., 1909-10; admitted mem. of Inner Temple, Jan., 1909; 1st cls. hons. and spec. prize, constitutional law, 1909; ag. asst. dep.-gov., Nyasaland, June, 1910; ag. dep.-gov., July, 1910; 1st grade res., Jan., 1911; ag. dep.-gov., Apr. to July, 1911; ag. asst. dep.-gov., July to Oct., 1911; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; author of "Nyasaland under the F.O.", 2nd edition; and a history of Nyasaland in the native dialect, published by the Prot. govt.
DUFF, HON. JAMES STODDART.—B. 1856; ed. pub. sch. and Collingwood Coll. Inst.; elec. to Ontario legis. for West Simcoe, 1898, 1902, 1905 1908 and 1911; min. of agric., Ontario, 1908.
DUFF, LYMAN POORE, B.A., LL.B. Toronto Univ.—B. 1865; apptd. judge, sup. ct., British Columbia, 1904; judge, sup. ct. of Canada, 1906.

DUFF, GRANT, THE RT. HON. SIR M. E., P.C., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S. (1880).—B. 1829; ed. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1850; under-sec. of state for India, 1868 to 1874; lord rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1866 to 1872; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1880, to Aug., 1881; M.P. for Elgin dist., 1857 to 1881; gov. of Madras, 1881-6.

DUFFIELD, S.—Clk. to acengt. offr., Cape govt. rlys., 1897; bkpr. to ch. acctn., O.F.S. (afterwards Imp. military) rlys., Oct., 1897, to 11th June, 1900; 1st clk. and bkpr. to orphan master, O.R.C., 11th June, 1900, to 1st Apr., 1903; asst. mast. of High Ct., O.R.C., 1st Apr., 1903; ag. mast. of High Ct., Jan. to June, 1906.

DUFFIELD, THOMAS.—Entered crown lands office, S. Australia, Mar., 1867; ch. clk. and immigrn. offr., crown lands and immigrn. dept., 1877; capt. mil. force, 1881; sec. crown lands dept.; mem. of central land and pastoral bds., 1896-1905; sec., crown lands dept., and ch. inspr. of fisheries, Nov., 1901.

DUFFY, CHAS. GAVAN, C.M.G. (1904). LL.B.,—B. 1855; 3rd son of late Sir Chas. Gavan Duffy, K.C.M.G.; ed. at Stonyhurst Coll., St. Patrick's Coll., Melbourne, and Melbourne Univ.; clk. in chief sec's. off., Victoria, 1871-8; transf. to Victoria legia. assem. staff, 1878; called to the bar, 1880; apptd. clk. asst. to legis. assem., 1891; has been clk. to H. of R., C. of A., since 1901; was assist. sec. to Fed. Convin, Melbourne, 1897.

DUFFY, THE HON. JOHN GAVAN.—B. 1849; eldest son of the late Sir Charles Gavan Duffy, at Stonyhurst; vice-chancellor's prize, Engls. essay, Melbourne Univ.; succeeded his father representation of Dalhousie in legis. assem. Victoria, 1874; pres. of bd. of land and vis. of Victoria, 1880; postmr.-gen. of Victoria, 1880; also atty.-gen., 1892; mem. of cabinet with portfolio, 1892; resig. Jan., 1893; again postmr.-gen., 1894 to 1899; rep. Victoria at prem. confee. at Sydney, 1896; rep. Victoria at Prem. Coun. of Aust., Hobart, 1893, when he was chmn. of standing commte.; rep. Victoria at postal confees. 1891, 1892-5-7-8; rep. Aust. at Fiji at postal congress at Washington, 1897.

DUGDALE, CAPTAIN G. FRANK.—Ed. Winchester Coll.; cadet in the mercantile navy, 1892 to 1896; offr., 1897 to 1904, serving in Shire and White Star Lines; sub.-lieut. R.N., 1905, serving in H.M.S. "Daedalus", "Defiance"; ag. lieut., R.N.R., 1906, serving H.M.S. "Cambridge" and "Cornwall"; promoted to lieut., R.N.R., 1907, and served H.M.S. "Diadem" and "Mars"; apptd. offr., Uganda Railway Lake Steamers, 1908; chief offr., 1909; transf. to the Uganda Rail. and apptd. capt. of the protectorate vessel, "William MacKinnon", 1910; ag. sub-marine, 1911.

DUKE, MANSERGH PACE, M.R.C.P., L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (1875).—B. 1852; ed. Acad. inst., and Meath hosp., Dub.; med. offr., Dominica, 1875 to 1880; asst. surg., Gold Coast, 1880-1; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1886; offr., mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1888; offr., dist. 1, and med. offr. of health, Oct.; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1895; mem. pct. commte., Oct., 1896; ex officio mem. bd. of Jan., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. escheator, July, 1898; holds dormant comm., July, 1899; act as commnr., and has so acted on num. occasions; resig. dormant comm., July, 1900.

DUKES, HUGH ROBSON.—Sern. asst. Model Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1st July, 1893; headmaster, Stanger schl., 1st July, 1893; ag. inspr. of schls., 15th May, 1902 to 30th June, 1903; headmaster, Richmond schl., 1st Oct., 1903; inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1903.

DUNBAR, ARTHUR DUNCAN, M.A. (Aberd.)—Served during S. African war in S. Africa; Horse (Queen's and King's medals and cl. clk. to dir. of educn., Transvaal and Orange, 1902-3; priv. sec. to educn. adviser to high. for S. Africa, 1904-5; clk., educn. dept., 1905; sec. to civ. serv. comm., O.R.C., 1905.

DUNCAN, ANDREW, H. F. (late lieut. R.N.).—Joined R.N., July, 1868; served in Mediter. S. America, Cape, India, Australia, and W. of Africa; resig. Nov., 1883; passed ext. theoretical survey, July, 1883, and practice, 1884, at the Cape Univ., in honour of his year on both occasions; from Apr., employed by the survr.-gen. of the Cape in priv. practice; mem. land comm. of Br. of Analand, Sept., 1885; survr.-gen. of the Cape, July, 1886; survr.-gen. to Br. S. Africs.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—Ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball Coll.; 1st cls. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1895; scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner, clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; co. 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906.

DUNCOMBE, HENRY FEVERSHAM.—Entd. col. serv. as an acctnt., treas., Bahamas, 1891; ag. mag. and collr. in various dists., 1894-5-6; rec. mag., Abaco, 1897; dist. commr. Lagos, 1898; has acted on various occasions as pol. mag., collr. of cust., and treas.; passed govt. exam. in Yoruba language, 1900.

DUNCOMBE, W. KELLSALL.—B. 1878; 2nd clk., tel. dept., Bahamas, Dec., 1895; rev. offr., Inagua, Mar., 1897; asst. res. just., Watlings Island, May, 1900; res. just., Cat Island, July, 1900; supervisor of cust., Lagos, Jan., 1905; ag. collr. of cust. from Aug. to Nov., 1906; and from Apr. to May, 1907; 1st grade supervisor of cust., 1st May, 1907; ag. prov. collr. of cust., Lagos, May to Dec., 1908; prov. collr. of cust., 1st Jan., 1909; ag. compt. of cust., S. Nigeria, June to Nov., 1910; temp. M.L.C., 1910.
DUNDAS, HON. C. C. F.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 4th Sept., 1908.
DUNDAS, HON. KENNETH ROBERT.—Ed. in Germany, studied engnrng in Norway; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1904; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1908.

DUNEDIN, 2ND BISHOP OF (founded 1864), Rt. REV. SAMUEL TARRATT NEVILL, D.D.—B. 1837; coh. 1871; Magdal. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1865; curate of Scarisbrick, Lancs., 1860-64; rector of Skelton, Staffs., 1864-71; is also primate of New Zealand.

DUNLOP, J. M. M., B.A., LL.D., D.C.L.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; barr.-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, and Inner Temple; dist. commr., Lagos, 1900; ag. pol. mag., 1901-2-3; ag. atty.-gen., 1904; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1905; ag. atty.-gen., 1906; ditto, S. Nigeria, 1906-7; ag. puisne judge, 1906-7-8-9; ag. solr.-gen., 1909-10.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL R.A., C.M.G. (1884)—B. 1838; ag. commr. of pol., S. Sttlnts., Nov., 1870; ag. pol. mag. and commr. of ct. of requests, P.W., Apr., 1871; ag. dep. commr. of pol. and coroner, Apr., 1871; ag. dep. commr. of pol., Singapore, fire commr. and coroner, Aug., 1871; ag. imprt.-gen. of pol., Dec., 1872; commr. to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, commr. with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungai Ujong; imprt.-gen., Straits pol., May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, apptd. special commr. (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organised the expdn. which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; commr. to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied Gen. Colborne's force up the Perak river, and across country to Kinta; ag. res. coun., Penang, 1884 to 1885; pres. of Singapore mun. comm., 1889; ret., 1890.

DUNLOP, WALTER RONALD.—B. 1887; ed. Univ. Coll. sch., London, and at South-east Agric. coll., Wye, Kent; diploma with honours of Wye coll., 1908; asst. lec., staff, Wye coll.; agric. and science master, St. Kitt's-Nevis gram. sch., Feb., 1910; ag. head master, Aug., 1910; prov. insp. cattle in Nevis, Sept., 1910, in connection with outbreak of disease; writer of many contributions to British agric. journals.

DUNN, EDWARD JOHN, F.G.S.—B. 1844; dir. of geological survey of Victoria, Sept., 1904; awarded Murchison medal by R. Geological Soc. for scientific services in S. Africa and Australia, 1905; author of a work on "Pebbles," 1911.

DUNN, ROBERT ERNST.—Clerk and interp. to the R.M., Umgeni div., Natal 1880; to R.M., Lions River, Mar., 1883; to R.M., Alfred, Oct., 1887; to R.M., Upper Umkumani, 1894; mag. for Colony, mag., Weenen div., 1901.

DUNSTAN, WINDHAM ROWLAND.—M.A. (Oxon.); LL.D. (Aberdeen); F.R.S.; ed. Bedford schl.; lec. and demonstr. of chem., Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lec. on chem., St. Thomas' Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres., section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem. of adv. comittee on crop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; mem. comm. intell. comittee, Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., since 1903; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlmt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyasaland, (reports presented to parlmt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlmt.); "Report on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-8 (presented to Parlmt."); "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910.

DUNWILLE, WILLIAM.—B. 1855; ed. Trinity coll., Kandy, and Royal coll. Colombo; local div., Ceylon civ. ser.; commr. of request and pol. mag., Kurunegala, Feb., 1891; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, March, 1894; ag. fiscal, Cent. Prov., April, 1897; pol. mag., Galagedera, and pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1898; pol. mag., Matale and Panwila, July, 1899.

DUPIGNY, E. G. MORGAN.—Asst. govt. off., Dominica, Feb., 1889; clk. commrs. of valuation, 1890; clk. mag., dist. F., 1890; do., presdt.'s off., Dom., 1890-92; do. registr. off., 1892; do. treas., Antigua, 1896; stud. Mid. Temp., 1900; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 1903; 3rd class res., 1906; 2nd class res., 1911.
DURMAN, F. J.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 15th April, 1901.

DURRANT, F. C. WELLS, M.A.—K.C., 1911; B. 1864; ed. in St. Vincent, Univ. Coll. schl., Lond., and Univ. of Edin.; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1886; practised as barr. and solr. in St. Vincent; ag. pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1898; stip. mag. and ag. atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1902; resig., 1904; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1909; ag. chief just., July, 1910, and from May to Aug., 1911.

DU TOIT, PIETER JOHANNES.—Clerk, dep. of agric., Jan., 1890; 2nd cls. clk., May, 1896; 1st cls. clk., June, 1900; ag. chief clk. to ag. dir. of agric., Aug., 1905; ag. under-sec. for agric., Mar., 1908; apptnt. confirmed, Jan., 1909; ag. under-sec. for agric. for Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910.
DUTTON, BERNARD JOSEPH.—B. 1876; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. asst., Badulla-Kacheri, Aug., 1900; Anuradhapura Kacheri, Nov., 1902; dist. judge, Kegalla, Apr., 1903;

DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KORTRIGHT.—B. 1862; served in prea.'s office, Virgin Is., 1879 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; clk., registr. office, Nevis, 1886, and registr. of pub. cemeteries there, Apr., 1886; 2nd clk., registr.'s office, Antigua, 1887; clk. to trustees, supt. ct. lib. July, 1888; ag. 1st clk., registr.'s office, Mar., 1889, to Aug., 1890; comsnr. to admnrs. oaths, 1889; marshal V.-A. ct., L.I., Dec., 1889; clk. to mag. dist. A., 1890; dep. cor., dist. A., 1892; ag. postmtr., Antigua, May to Sept., 1894; 1st clk., registr.'s off., Jan., 1895; dep. cor., dist. B., Oct., 1896; ag. mag., dist. A., Jan., Feb., Apr. to Nov., 1897; sec. to H.M. Diamond Jubilee comttee, June, 1897; ag. mag., dist. B., Apr., 1898, to Jan., 1899, during which period, May-June, 1898, acted also as mag., dist. A., and from May to Sept., 1899, and 25th Oct., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; during which period (Jan. to Apr.) acted also as mag., dist. B.; J.P., Sept., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., June, 1900; to Mar., 1901; and Apr. to Nov., 1901; also in Mar., 1902; mem. of "Coronation" comttee., and hon. sec. to same, May, 1902; ag. mag., dist. A., Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903; registr. and prov.-marshal and registr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, 11th June, 1903; mem. of Basseterre town bd., and deputy chmn. of same, June, 1903; chmn. of same, Jan., 1905; J.P., St. Kitts, 1904; registr. and prov.-marshal, Antigua, and ch. registr., Leeward Is., July, 1905; trustee, sup. ct. library, Aug., 1905; registr. of joint stock companies, Leeward Is., Nov., 1906; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1907; chmn., St. John's City comsnrs., May, 1907, to 31st Dec., 1909; chmn., trustees of pub. library, July, 1907, to 30th June, 1910; ag. mag., dists. L and C, Sept. to Oct., 1909.

DYSON, CECIL VENN, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; supt. of posts and telegraphs, Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1896; mag., Seremban, Feb., 1900; sec. central census comttee., Nov., 1900; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kinta, Jan., 1903; sheriff and dep. registr., Penang, Aug., 1906; registr., sup. ct., dist. judge and mag., Malacca, Apr., 1906.

EAGLESOME, JOHN EGAN, C.M.G. (1905).—M. Inst. C.E.; served in P.W.D., rly., India, 1890; recd. thanks of Indian govt., Khojak tunnel, 1892, Godavari bridge, 1900; services lent to C.O., 1st Sept., 1900, as D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; in charge of Baro-Kano rly. from 1907.
EAKIN, JAS. W.—B.A., M.D., M. Ch. Univ., Dublin; govt. med. offr., Trinidad; dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, S., and consulting surg. Col. hosp., San Fernando; surg.-major, local force.

EARL, ROBT. STEPHEN, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab., 1891).—B. 1865; med. offr. Lasove dist., Dominica, July, 1892; med. offr., No. 5 dist., Antigua, 1896; comsnr. Virgin Is.; col. surg., Falkland Is., 1909.

EARLE, LIONEL, C.B. (1911); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. at Marl., Univ. of Göttingen and Paris, and Merton Coll., Oxford; ast. sec. to royal comsn. on Paris exhib., 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, 1900; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1903; priv. sec. to lord prea. of the coun. (Earl of Crewe), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), Nov., 1910.

EARNSHAW, ALBERT.—B. 1865; ed. at Univ. Coll., Durham, and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; held foundtn.; Newby and Univ. class. schlrships., Durham, 1885-1887; 1st cls. classics, 1st year exam., 1886; 1st cls. class., final, 1887; B.A., 1887; M.A., 1890; elected fellow of Durham.

EDWARDS, CHAS. HENRY.—Ed. St. Bees gram. schlr. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; exhibtnr. of Queen's Coll., B.A., 1894; B.C.L., 1901; called to the bar, Mid. Term., 1898; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1904.

EDWARDS, HERBERT ARCHER, F.R.A.S.—Cadet, schoolship H.M.S. "Conway," Jan., 1886; passed out 1st on list, Dec., 1887; midshipman, R.N.R., 1889; sub-lieut., 1895; lieut., 1900; served in Royal Navy in several ships; 1st cls. certif. in torpedo and gunnery; extra master's certif.; naval instr., school ship H.M.S. "Conway," 1902-5; marine offr., Lagos, Aug., 1905; lent to govt. of G. Coast for special service, Feb., 1906; diploma in surveying, R.G.S., 1907; comdr., S.Y. "Ivy," S. Nigeria, Dec., 1908.
EDWARDS, W. C.—Aast. dist. comsnr., E.A.F., 1910.

EDWARDS, CAPT. WM. FREDERICK SAVERY, D.S.O.—4th Batt. Devonshire regt.; B. 1872; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; served in ranks of Duke of Cornwall's L.I., 1892; 2nd lieut. 4th Devon regt., Jan., 1899; served in Ashanti, 1900; aast. inspr. S. Leone frontier pol., 1899; in command of S. Leone frontier pol. during Ashanti rising (twice wounded, mentioned in despatches, D.S.O., medal with two clasps); promoted for services, inspr. S. Leone pol.; capt., 1901; seconded to S.A.C., 1901; inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons in Uganda, 1906; inspr.-gen. of pol., E. Africa and Uganda, 22nd June, 1908; awarded King's Police Medal, 1911.

EGAN, JAMES DAVIS O'DOWN, L.R.C.S. Ireland (1886); LL.M.; K.Q.C.P. Ireland (1887); B. 1863; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; res. surg. asst. to Sir T. Stoker, Richmond hosp., 1885; res. clin. clk. to Sir J. Banks, K.C.B., Whitworth hosp., 1886; res. surg., Richmond, Whitworth, and Hardwick hosps., 1886; govt. med. offr., B. Guiana, 1890; aast. surg., pub. hosp., Georgetown, 1890-1896; ag. med. offr., Buxton dist., 1897; Tuschen dist., 1899; Belfield dist., 1901; med. offr., Buxton dist., 1903; mem. of medical bd., B. Guiana, since 1896; is also mem. of bd. of agric., and a J.P. for the colony.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1880; aast. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); ag. sen. dist. offr., Butterworth, P.W., Apr., 1890; a.J.P. and coroner for S. Sttlmts., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungai Ujong, July, 1893; ag. res., Pahang, May to July, 1894; offl. assignee and regiar. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comansrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Jan., 1898; ag. rec. councillor, Malacca, Mar., 1898, and Mar., 1899; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; ag. col. treas., S.S., Nov.-Dec., 1898; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apr., 1900; ag. res. councilr., Penang, Feb. to Apr., 1901; ag. col. treas., S.S., Aug.-Oct., 1902; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high. comsnr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, 30th July, 1904; govr. and comdr.-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mch., assumed govt., 1st May, 1906; represented W. African Colonies and Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

EGGETT, W. H.—B. 1862; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1890; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., Apr., 1896; asst. acctnt., 6th Apr., 1899.

EHRHARDT, ALBERT K.C.—B. 1862; ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham., and Worcr. Coll., Oxford; graduated with honours in mods. and lit. hum., 1886; Inner Tem. schlrship, common law, 1888; called to bar, 1889; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1896; travelling comsrr. on the E. bindry., Aug., 1897; ag. res. of Ibadan, 1898; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., June, 1900; ag. att.-gen., Sept., 1900; rlyw. comsrr. at Aro, June, 1901; ag. treas., Aug., 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Mar. to June, 1902; apptd. res., Ibadan, Feb., 1902; apptd. col. treas., May, 1902; atty.-gen., Fiji, mem. ex. and leg. couns., May, 1903; apptd. as comsrr. to produce revised edition of ordinances of the colony, 1903; ag. ch. just., May to Oct., 1904; produced revised edition of ordinances, 1906; ag. ch. jus. and ch. jud. comsrr. of W. Pacific High Comn., 1907; chmn. of comen. to inquire into customs duties and admin., 1907.

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., Oxon.—Ed. Highgate Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. Stltmsts., Oct., 1884; passed in Malay, 1886; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1890; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag., Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. land revenue, Singapore, Dec., 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. inspr. of schl., S. Stltmsts., June, 1895; also ag. official assignee and registr. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., July, 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1898; inspr. of schl., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as first mag. till Feb., 1899; dir. pub. instr., Aug., 1901; inspr. of prisons, July, 1904; fin. comsrr., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; dir. of educn., S. Stltmsts., and F.M.S., 1906.
ELDER, J. H. C.—B. 1870; ed. at Marlborough, served in South African war, 1900-1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 22nd Sept., 1906.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—Ck., P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clk. to pres. and isld. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., 1882; postmr. of Antigua, Aug., 1891; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. B., 1894, 1896, and 1897; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. H., Nevis, Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; mem. St. Kitts-Nevis legis. coun., and gen. legis. coun., 1899; ag. comsrr. of Virgin Is., and ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1903; ag. treas., Antigua, June, 1906; treas. and shipping mast., Apr., 1907.

ELFFERS, PIETER.—T. ii. (Holland), T. i. (O.F.S.); ed. at Normal Training Coll., Nymeyer, Holland; Dutch mast., pub. schl., Stellenbosch, 1882-84; ditto, Grey Coll. Schl., 1884-93; prin., gov't. schl., Fauresmith, 1893-1901; Dutch mast., boys' high schl., Wynberg, Cape Colony, 1901-03; ditto, pub. schl., Bethulie, 1903-04; ditto, pub. schl., Winburg, 1904-05; inspr. of schl., O.R.C., 1st July 1905.

ELGEE, CAPT. C. H.—B. 1871; ed. Lancing and Sandhurst; 1st comsrr., 16th Foot, Feb., 1892; capt., Oct., 1899; active service, Chitral, 1895 (medal with clasp); Ashanti campaign, relief of Kumasi, 1900 (medal); adjutant, Lagos Haussa Force, 1899; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, gov. of Lagos, 1900 to 1902; and clk. of ex. coun.; ag. insp.-gen., 1902; trav. comsrr., Lagos, 1902; ag. col. treas., mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1902; resident, Ibadan, 1st Jan., 1903; mem. of S. Nigeria liquor trade inquiry com., 1909; author of "Memo. on Negro Education."

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE, 9th Earl (creat. 1633), VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.C. (creat. 1899); G.C.S.I., G.C.L.E., P.C., LL.D.C.L.; Baron Bruce, 1603; Earl of Kincardine and Baron Bruce of Torry, 1647; Baron Elgin (U.K.), 1849.—B. 1849; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, M.A.; treas. of Household and comsrr. of wks., 1886; Lord-Lieut. of Fifeshire since 1885; Viceroy of India, 1894-99; chmn. Roy. Coman. on S. African War, 1902; chmn. Roy. Coman. on Free Churches Controversy, Scotland, 1904; chmn. Roy. Coman. to administer Scottish Churches Act, 1905; sec. of state for Colonies, Dec., 1905, to Apr., 1908.

ELIOT, EDWARD CARLYON.—B. 1870; Bradfield Coll. and Uppingham; apptd. class clk., govt. secretariat, B. Guiana, 1892; seconded ag. clk. to govt. agent, N.W. dist. B. Guiana, for periods 1896 to 1899; 4th class clk., govt. secretariat, 1899; pay and quartering Hausa force, Gold Coast Col., 1900; apptd. asst. treas., Oct., 1902; dist. comsrr., G. (G.) 5th May, 1905; with powers of travelling com., 1906; provincial comsrr., 18th Oct., 1907; transferred to Tobago as warden and stipendiary J.P., 1911.

ELIOT, JOHN ALFRED ROY.—Ed. at Harrow House, Winchfield and Marlborough College, collr., E. Africa Prot., 4th May, 1903; 1st Aug., 1903; Machakos, 15th June; resigned, 20th Dec., 1905; asst. treas., 22nd Dec., 1907.

ELIOT, LAURENCE S., I.S.O. (1903).—B. clk. to mage., W. Australia; landing and tide clk. at Bunbury, 1863; also postmr. and ass. registrar; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1872; sec. to bd. of educn., and ass. clk. in legis. coun. reg. gen., registr. of deeds, and regist. of wills, 1876; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1880; 1st clk. and registr. gen., col. sec., Jan., 1880; apptd. to take the census, April, and to superintend its compilation; ch. exec. acctnt., treasy., Apr., 1881; ag. asst. com., Dec., 1889, to Oct., 1890; under treas., 1891; is a J.P.

ELLARD, JOHN BRANDON.—Ed. at Northampton Gram. Schl.; Thorneycroft's M.I., 1900; transf. comsrr., Driscoll's Scouts; medal, 5 bars, King's medal; sec. and ass. Repatn. Coman., Hoopstad, O.R.C., to 1903; Coman.; asst. to loc. auditor, B. C. Africa, 1903-04; treasy. asst., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1906; asst. pay and qrtmr., pol., E. Oct., 1908.

ELLENBERGER, JULES.—Clk. to asst. Bech. Prot., 1890; interpreter to comsrr., Bech. Prot., 1893; ditto to Sir Pard's boundy. coman., 1894; J.P., 1895; mag., 1898; served in S. African war, relief of Mafeking; spec. comsrr. for allegiance in So. dist. of Bech. Prot. to late S.A.R. and O.F.S.; asst. comsrr., So. Bech. Prot., 11th July, 1902.
ELLIOT, MAJOR SIR HENRY GEORGE, K.C.B. (1899), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1852; mag. of Tembuland, C. of Good Hope command of Tembu levies during Basotho ret., 1904.

ELLIOT, JOHN AUGUSTUS GILBERT.—Lieut. reserve of officers; ed. privately Edin. Univ.; enlisted in C.I.V. (M.I.), African war, Jan., 1900; 2nd lieut. R. Sec., Aug., 1900; dangerously wounded at... Frederikstad, Oct. 1900; 1st lieut., May, 1903; served with 8th and 4th regts. of M.I., S. Africa, 1903-4; intell. dept., S. Africa, 1904-1906; rejoined 2nd R. Scots Fus., Salisbury Plain, June, 1906; M.I. certif.; musk. certif., Hythe, 1907; pub. health certif., Edin. Univ., 1908; asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., 14th Apr., 1909; passed Swahili exam., 1910.

ELLIOTT, COULTON.—Lieut. R.N.R. (retired); Assoc. Inst. Naval Architects; B. 1871; ed. at Plympton gram. schl. and H.M.S. Worcester; in merchant service to 1898, ex-master (Lond.), passed in steam; Royal Navy, 1896 to 1898; hydrographer, Great Pacific Cable survey, 1899-1900; deputy marine supt., N. Nigeria marine, Mech. 1902; marine supt., 1st Jan., 1905.
ELLIOTT, F.—Asst. dist. supt. of pol., E.A.P., Aug., 1907.

ELLIS, FRANCIS ROBT., C.M.G.—B. 1849; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1871; additional pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1874; office ast. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., 1876; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, 1878; ag. ast. agt., Matale, 1879; ditto, Mullaitivu, 1880; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1st Apr., 1883; pol. mag., Colombo, 1885; dir. and inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1891-6; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., May to Nov., 1893; ag. prim. collr. of cust., Jan., 1896; gov't. agt., S. Prov., Jan. to Mar., 1897; gov't. agt., W. Prov., Sept., 1897; ag. gen., Mar., 1902; ag. col. sec., 19th Nov., 1903; gov', N. Borneo, 1911.

ELLIS, FRANK THOMAS.—B. 1881; cadet, S. Stiltns., 27th Nov., 1903; attached to Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1904; sent to China to study Hokkien, Aug., 1904; passed final in Chinese, Aug., 1906; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Sept., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Oct., 1906; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Xmas. Is., Apr., 1907; ag. 2nd dist. offr., Tapah, Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Island, May, 1909; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Nov. 1909.

ELLIS, CAPT. R. FAIRFAX.—B. 1876; ed. at Beaumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 5th Batt. Roy. Fus., 1897; seconded for service under Roy. Niger Co., May, 1899; served with 2nd N. Nigeria Rifles, Jan., 1900, to Nov., 1901; ast. commnr., N. Nigeria police, 21st Jan., 1905.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—B. 1871; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxon.; scholar, 1890-1895; proc. acc. Hertford Univ. scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical moda., 1892; Univ. jun. Greek testament prize, 1893; 1st class literae humaniores, 1894; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1897; chancellor's prize, Latin essay, 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., Apr., 1895; priv. sec. to Lord Selborne, June, 1897; 1st class clk., 1899; prin. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; mem. of Indian emigtn. comtee., 1909.

ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMORE, M.D., M.R.C.S.—B. 1860; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr., Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892, again 1894; ag. col. res. surg. in 1901, 1902 and 1903; priv. civ. med. offr., S.S., Jan., 1910; police surg. since 1897; J.P. for Singapore.

ELY, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1871; clk. in dept. of agt.-gen. for Tasmania; ch. clk., 1895; sec., 1899; acted as priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Edward Braddon, P.C., K.C.M.G., ex-premier of Tasmania, during his visit to England for the Diamond Jubilee celebration, 1897.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1864; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and Le Poile 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1909.

EMERSON, GEO. HT., K.C.—Speaker, house of assmr., Newfoundland, 1890-94; mem. of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; mem. exec. coun., 1895; asst. judge, sup. ct., Nov., 1896.
EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901.

EMMOTT, 1ST BARON (U.K.) (creat. 1911), RT. HON. ALFRED EMMOTT.—P.C. (1908); B. 1858; ed. at Grove House, Tottenham; B.A., Lond.; J.P., Lancs. and Oldham; mem. of town council, Oldham, for 12 years; mayor, 1891; M.P., Oldham, 1899-1911; chrmn. of ways and means and deputy-speaker of H. of C., 1906-1911; partly, under sec. of state for the colonies, 26th Oct., 1911; chrmn. of West African currency comtee., 1911-12.

EMTAGE, O. DE C., M.A.—Ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; Barbados scholar, 1886; scholar, Worc. Coll., Oxon, 1887; 1st class math. mods., 2nd cls. finals, 1890; hon. nat. sc. schools, 1891; ast. mast., Harr. Coll., 1891; examiner, 2nd grade schools, Barbados, 1891-1902; ditto, gram. school, St. Lucia, 1894-95; ditto, Grenada, 1897-98; headmaster, The Lodge School, 1899.

EMTAGE, WM. THOS. ALLER.—B. 1862; M.A., Oxford; Fell. of Phys. Soc., Lond.; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; scholar, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, 1880; 1st cls. math. mods., 1881; 1st cls. final maths., 1883; 1st cls. nat. sci., 1885; University Coll., Nottingham, math. lecturer, 1885; ast. prof. math. and phys., 1889; prof. maths. and phys., 1891; principal, tech. inst., Wandsworth (L.C.C.), 1895; director of pub. instr., Mauritius, 1900; examnr., Oxford Locals, maths., 1888, 1889; examnr., final hon. school nat. sci., Oxford, 1890, 1891; author of "Mathematics of Electricity" (Oxford Press), and other works.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1850; apptd., 29th Dec., 1869, after passing an exam. before the civ. ser. commsrs., clk. in the accts. branch in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1868; 1st class ast. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; bookkeeper and sen. ast. to the financial clk., 1884; acted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1896.

ENGLAND, WILLIAM WENTWORTH.—Entd. gov't. ser., Antigua, 1st Feb., 1907; clk. to regisr.-gen. of births and deaths; ag. treasy. offr., Montserrat, 11th Jan. to 30th Dec., 1908; ag. clk. to commr., Virgin Is., 5th Mar. to 25th Aug., 1909.

ENNIS, GEORGE FRANCIS MACDANIEL.—B. 1868; ed. at King's Coll. school and privately; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1892; sec. to gov', B.N. Borneo, 13th Nov., 1894; sessions judge, B.N. Borneo and Labuan, 24th Dec., 1894; judge of genl. ct., Labuan, 1895; judge of ch. ct., B.N. Borneo, 14th Aug., 1895; ag. res., Province Alcock, B.N. Borneo, 13th Dec., 1895; resig., Oct., 1897; ag. ast. judge, Zanzibar, 18th May to 7th July, and 1st Aug. to 24th Oct., 1899; regisr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1899; town mag., Mombasa, 20th July, 1900; ag. judge, E. Africa Prot., 6th Sept., 1900; ag. ast. judge, Zanzibar, 25th Jan., 1901; legal vice-consul, Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; judge of high ct. of Uganda, 11th Aug., 1902, and judge of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; joint author of "The Registration of Transfers."

ENRAGHT-MOONY, F.—Sub-inspr., Basuto-land police, Mar., 1884; acted as govt. sec., Basutoland, 1893-1895; commanded Bech. Prot. native police with rank of capt., 1896; asst. commr., Basutoland, 1897; acted as govt. sec. 1900-1901; mentioned in com.-in-chief's desps. 1901; native commr., Zoutpansberg, Aug., 1901; commanded Ft. Edward and Spelonken dist., Northern Transvaal, and acted as intell. offr. until close of war; special commr., Swaziland, June, 1902; res. mag. and native commr., Swaziland, 1905.

ESNOUF, E. AMAND.—Dist. mag., Seychelles, 1870; Grand Port, Mauritius, 15 May, 1878; dist. mag., Moka, 1883; jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, Aug., 1884; ag. master sup. ct., May, 1886; and Dec., 1887, to Jan., 1888; ag. sen. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, Mar., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to Aug., 1896; ag. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Feb., 1898; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Riv. du Rempart, Mar., 1898; dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Mar., 1899; ag. substitute mast., sup. ct., Aug., 1901.

ESPIE, HENRY PATTULLO.—Ed. at Glasgow Acad.; associate, Chartered Institute of Acctnts. and Actuaries, Glasgow, Apr., 1891; asst. chief acctnt., Imperial Brit. East Africa Compy., 1891; 1st asst. treas., July, 1895; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1903.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VYVIAN ARMIT.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl., London, and Crystal Palace schl. of engineering; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, June, 1894 to 1900; Lagos govt. rly., 1900-1901; dist. engrnr., Gold Coast govt. rly., 1901 to 1904; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 9th June, 1905; ag. asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1906 and 1909.
EVANS, E. P.—Educ. at Radley Coll. and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons., Mod. Hist.), 1906; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 7th Feb., 1908; 2nd asst. sec., 1910; clk. to exec. coun., 1911.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transf'd. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug., to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. govt., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. govt., G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admnstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1895; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901.
EVANS, H. A.—Balliff mag.'s ct., Virgin Is., Jan., 1885; bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1890; clk. post office, 1890; govt. offr. and cashier, 1901.

EVANS, HON. JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, 11th July, 1904, to 10th Oct., 1905; premier and treas., 11th Oct., 1905, to 1st May, 1906; premier and ch. sec. 1st May, 1906 to June, 1909.
EVANS, R. N.—Asst. to chief of customs, E.A.P., Moh., 1911.

EVANS, WILLIAM.—B. 1860; cadet, S. Stltmtn., Nov., 1882; ed. King's Coll., Camb.; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Dec., 1886; collr. of land rev., Malacca, May, 1886; 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, May, 1887; ag. ast. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1888; passed exams. in Chinese and Malay, J.F., ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1888; asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1890; ag. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1890; ditto, S. Stltmtn., Nov. and Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; ast. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; prot. of Chinese, S. Stltmtn., Apr., 1895; ag. audr.-gen., S.S., May, 1901, to July, 1902; pres., municipal comm., Singapore, Sept., 1903; seconded for spec. set. in Transvaal as prot. of Chinese, 25th Dec., 1904; ag. rec. coun., Malacca, Mar., 1906; confirmed ditto, Feb., 1907; ag. col. sec., 29th May to 26 June, 1908, Feb. to May, 1909, and from 4th, 1910; res. councillor, Penang, July, 1916; continued to act as col. sec.

EVANS, W. SCOTT.—Apptd. clk. col. sec., off., Jamaica, 16th Sept., 1896; sec., retrench. comte., 1900; sec., Falmouth water supply, 1901; ag. sec., Montego Bay riot comm., 1902; ast. priv. sec. to Sir S. Olivier when admin. govt., 1904; ag. sec., Jamaica schls. comm. comte., selection Rhodes schlhp., 1904; ast. N. Nigeria, June, 1905.

EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to pres. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apr., 1899; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1899; clk., regar.'s off., May, 1906; ag. ch. clk., govt. offr., Antigua.

EVELYN, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1864; Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. col. sec., Barbados, 1883; ast. clk., govt. savings, 1883; ch. clk., 1884; rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1885, to Nov., 1890; acnt., P.W.D., Nov., to Jan., 1893; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to 1894; ch. clk., govt. off., and clk. of comm., 1894; ast. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1900; as col. sec. for several periods since 1901.

EVELYN, MERRIT.—2nd clk. to pres. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treasy., Nevis, 1878-1883; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. of int. charge, Nevis treasy., 1883; prot. of int. Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comm., ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6; water comm., Nevis, 1901; mem. quarantine bd., Nevis.

EVERARD, W.—Supernum. col. sec., Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, Antigua, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of int. rev. offrs., 1884, to Dec., 1885; ch. clk., P.O., Mar., 1886; sen. landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EWAKT, DAVID, I.S.O.—B. 1843; ent. ser., Canada, 1871; chief architect, dist. pub. wks., 1897.

EWART, RAYMOND MARMAJUKE.—Ed. David's, Glas., 1891; Manipur field force, chief transport offr., 1892 to 1894, 1st clk. vice-consulate, Arabia; ag. Brit. vice-cons., Hodeida and Cameron, 1894-95; Aden pol. supt. of Zanzibar pol.; ast. supt. of pol., E. Prot., 8th Nov., 1895; Mbaruk rebellion, ast. supt. of pol., 1906.

EWING, Hon. SIR THOMAS TEAL, K.C.M.G. (1908).—Min. for defence, Aust.; M.P. for Richmond (N.S.W.), vice-pres. of exec. coun., 6th July to 1st Oct., 1906; ag. postm.-gen., 1906; min. for affairs, 13th Oct., 1906, to 24th Jan., 1907; profession a licensed surveyor; M.L.A. Richmond, 1885-94; and for Liamore, 1894-95; was sometime chrmn. of partly. pub. wks., N.S.W.; chrmn. of royal comm. on rly. into city of Sydney; ret. to 1st house of... of Aust., Mar., 1901, re-elected Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1906; joint author of "Progress of Australia during the Nineteenth Century," and of various publications on Australian subjects, retired from politics, 1910.

EZECHIEL, P. H.—B. 1875; B.A., Bombay, 1889 (et. 14); first in Woolwich competn., 1893; B.A., London, 1894 (2nd exhib. at matric., 1892); major schlr., Trim. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; passed final exam. for Bar, 1902; sec. to Crown agts. for the Colonies, May, 1905.
FACEY, LUTHER HERBERT.—3rd cl. clk., prisons dept., Jamaica, June, 1877; 2nd cl. clk., June, 1879; 1st cl. clk., police and prisons dept., Nov., 1892.

FAFUNWA, W. K.—Cadet, Lagos civ. ser., 1896; 5th cls. clk., Jan., 1898; 4th cls. clk., Dec., 1898; 3rd cls. clk., audit off., Nov., 1902; pay master, P.W.D., May, 1903; sg. acctnt., P.W.D., Mar.-May, 1904; 2nd cls. clk., treasry., Sept., 1906; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907; asst. ch. clk., Jan., 1910.

FAGAN, JOSEPH PATRICK, L.R.C.S.I., L.R.C.P., L.M., D.P.H. (Dublin), Dip. Trop. Med.—B. 1867; med. offr. in Niger coast prot. under F.O., 13th Apr., 1894; in medical charge in operations against Chief Nana, Benin River (spec. ment. in desp. Queen's medal and clasp) 1894; in operations against the King of Benin, with the Aro field force (medal and clasp) 1901 and 1902; sen. med. offr., May, 1903; dep. P.M.O., N. Nigeria, 3rd Aug., 1907.

FAIR, LIEUT.-COL. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; entd. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1885; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff off. to Gen. Gatacre during Atbara campaign, 1898 (despa., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls. Medjidieh); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (despa., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 clasps); commanded div. S.A.C., Eastern Transvaal, 1901-4; ditto, O.R.C., 1904-8; nom. mem. of I.C.C., 1906; res. comsnr. and comdt.-gen., Southern Rhodesia, 1908.

FAIRBAIRN, JOHN.—B. 1863; tempor. clk. in legia. coun. off., Cape Town, Apr., 1881, to 30th Apr., 1889; crown lands off., 31st May, 1890, to 31st July, 1891; clk. to regis. of mines, Prince Albert, 1st Aug., 1891, to 31st May, 1892; legis. coun. off., 1st June, 1892, to 30th Sept., 1893, and 1st Jan. to 31st May, 1899; clk. of the papers and comtee. clk., 1st July, 1899; clk. asst. and taxing offr., 1st Jan., 1904; hon. sec., Central Good Hope Red Cross comtee., Victoria League for concentration camp relief, etc., etc.; is an esquire of the order of St. John of Jerusalem, in England, 1901; S. African War medal and clasp, 1899-1901.

FAIRBAIRN, RICHARD PURDOM, C.E.—B. 1855; ed. at high schl., London, Ontario; served in engnr.'s off., London, 1872; P.L.S., 1876; practising engnr., 1877; P.W.D., Ontario, 1879; chief engnr., pub. wks., Ontario, 1903; dep-min. of pub. wks., 1910.

FAIRBAIRN, ROBERT, I.S.O. (1906).—Second master, boys' schl., Perth, W. Australia, 1859; clk. to bench of mags., landing waiter, and postmnr., Vasse, 1862; res. mag., mag. of local ct., and dist. registr., 1875; Newcastle, 1875; Vasse, 1880; Kimberley, 1883; Fremantle, 1886.

FAIRBAIRN, THOMAS.—Cler. asst., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 19th May, 1890; 6th cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1892; 5th cls., 28th May, 1895; ag. 3rd cls., 17th July, 1898, to 6th Apr., 1899; 4th cls., 1st Mar., 1901; ag. 4th cls., govt. secretariat, 7th Aug., 1903; 4th cls., govt. secretariat, 1st Dec., 1903; 3rd cls., customs, 1st June, 1906; ag. 3rd cls., govt. secretariat, 29th Oct., 106, to 31st May, 1907; 3rd cls., immgr. off., 13th June, 1907; ag. 1st cls., 1st Nov., 1907, to 18th Jan., 1908, and 23rd July, 1908, to 24th Mar., 1909; ag. immgr. agt., 9th Dec., 1909, to 30th Sept., 1910; passed Hindi exam., May, 1911.

FALCK, DAVID GEORGE ANOSI.—Joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Nov., 1881; postmnr. and telegraphist, Winburg, Nov., 1881; landdrost's clk., Bethlehem, Aug., 1889; ag. landdrost, Heilbron, Apr., 1890; sec., G.P.O., O.F.S., Aug., 1892; postmnr.-gen., O.F.S., 1st Jan., 1895; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900; M.L.C., 27th June, 1904; retired.

FALCONBRIDGE, SIR WILLIAM GLENHOLME, KT. (1908), M.A.—B. 1846; ed. model grammar schl. for Upper Canada; Univ. of Toronto, B.A., 1866; M.A., 1870; lecturer in Univ. Coll., Toronto, 1867-68; called to the bar, 1871; mem., comsn. to revise statutes of Ontario, 1897; mem., comsn. to revise Imperial statutes affecting Ontario, 1901; a judge of the King's Bench Div., High Ct. of Just. for Ontario, 1887; ch. just., King's Bench, 1900.
FALSHAW, PERCY SCOTT.—M.R.C.V.S., Govt. vet. surgeon, Singapore, S. Sttlnts., 15th June, 1893.

FARFAN, JOSEPH FRANCIS AMBROSE.—Ed. at Ushaw Coll., Durham; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised at Trinidad bar from 1877 to 1904; mem. of comsn. to inquire into difficulties existing between agricultural contractors and proprietors, and in collaboration with late Mr. Justice Lewis drafted "Agricultural Contracts Orde." 1889; retained on several occasions by the Crown to prosecute in criminal cases; mag., Arima, Trinidad, 16th Nov., 1904; ag. S.J.P. of Port-of-Spain on several occasions, 1904 to 1908; chrmn. of comsn. to inquire into frauds of Diego Martin Local Road Bd., 1906.

FARQUHARSON, NORMAN DOUGLAS.—Chief acctnt., refugee camps dept., O.R.C., 1st Sept., 1901; chief asstnt., repatriation (subsequently govt. relief) dept., O.R.C., 13th June, 1902; civil comn., Bloemfontein, 1st Sept., 1904.
FARRAR, NICHOLAS.—Supernum., P.W.dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 3rd cls. clk., G.P.O., 1893; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., 1900; postmnr.-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Jan., 1905.

FARRER, ROLAND J.—Ed. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlnts., Nov., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Mar., 1897, to Sept., 1898, Dec., 1898, to Mar., 1900; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Mar., 1900; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1900; ag. supt. educ., Penang, June to Aug., 1900; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, May, 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; ag. dep. pub. proser., July, 1904; resumed duty as dist. offr., B. Mertajam, Feb., 1905; ag. collr., land revenue, Singapore, June, 1908.
FARRINGTON, R. J. A.—B. 1872; entd. Bahamas civ. ser., 1891; Out Island comn. (2nd div.), 1909.

FARROW, ROBINSON RUSSELL.—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Huron Co., Ontario; sessional clk., H. of C., Canada, Jan., 1881; clk. dept. of agric., June, 1881; transfd. to customs dept., June, 1884; acctnt., customs dept., July, 1895; asst. comn., customs dept., May, 1907; mem. of bd. of customs, Sept., 1907.
FAULKNER, E.—Ed. at the gram. schl., Freetown; brigade office clk., S. Leone, 1876 to 1883; ch. clk. col. secretariat, 1883; asst. col. sec., 1889.
FAULKNER, S. H.—Asst. auditor, E.A.P., 8th Jan., 1909.

FAURE, HON. SIR P. H., K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1848; sec. for native affairs, Cape, July, 1890; col. sec., May, 1893; sec. for agricult., Jan., 1896, to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1902-04; M.L.A. for Namaqualand, 1904; col. sec., June, 1907, to Feb., 1908; M.L.C. for W. Prov., 1908.
FAWCETT, J. F. ST. A.—Deputy regis., high ct., E.A.P., 1910.

FAWCETT, WILLIAM, B.SC. (LOND.), F.L.S.—B. 1851; asst. botanical dept., Br. Museum, 1880; dir. pub. gardens and plantations, Jamaica, 1886; mem. bd. of govs., Jamaica institute, 1887; chrmn., ditto, 1888-89 and 1906-07; nom. M.L.C., 1896; mem. bd. of agric., 1900; dep. chrmn. of agric. soc., 1901.

FAWKES, ARCHIBALD WALTER, K.C.—Ed. Repton schl.; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1879; regis., sup. ct., comsnr. of ct. of requests and of stamps, Malacca, 1884; ag. regis., sup. ct., Penang, 1885; pol. mag. and coroner, Gibraltar, 1886; acted as atty.-gen., 1890 and 1891; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 1892; puisne judge, O.R.C., 1901.
FEILDEN, CAPT. R. B.—Capt., R.A.; ed. Wellington Coll.; lieut. R.A., July, 1884; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, May, 1890; to high comsnr., Cyprus, 1892; and to gov., Br. Guiana, 1898.

FELL, T. E., B.A., Cantab., 1897—Apptd. oh. clk., col. sec.'s off., G. Coast, 2nd Oct., 1897; ag. asst. col. sec., 14th Sept., 1899; insp't., preventive services, 4th Dec., 1899; ag. trav. comsnr., 1900-1901; trav. comsnr., 16th June, 1902; ag. comsnr. for nat. affairs, 26th June, 1902.

FENN, JOHN CYRIL DOUGLAS.—B. 1879; ed. at Marlborough Coll.; asst. treas., Gold Coast, Nov., 1903; resigned, Dec., 1906; re-apptd., Jan., 1908; sen. asst. treas., June, 1908; retired, Apr., 1909; acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, May, 1910.
FERNANDEZ, HENRY.—Clerical asst. treas., Br. Guiana, 1887; 5th class clk., 1889; 4th class, 1891.
FERNLEIGH, THOMAS ERNEST.—Sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. police, June, 1908; passed lower civ. serv. law exam.; asst. res. mag., 1911.
FERREIRA, P. J., C.M.G. (1880).—Was comdt. of local cavalry in the Transvaal against Sikukuni.

FESTING, RICHARD ARTHUR GRINDALL.—B. 1875; ed. Clifton, and scholar of Queen's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., June, 1901; Oct., 1902; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, July, 1902; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1903; Matale, Feb., 1906; Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1909.
FFRENCH-MULLEN, VINCENT, L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. med. offr., Port Maria, Jamaica, Apr., 1881.

FIDDIES, SIR GEORGE VANDELEUR, K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acmntt. to Sir A. Milne, high comsnr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; polit. sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; acmntt. offr., 1907; asst. under-sec. of state, 20th Jul., 1909.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; ed. Tr. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke, 1893; 1st class classical honours, 1896; Goldsm. co. exhibn., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam, 2nd cls. C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; asst. priv. sec. to R. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; cls. clk., Jan., 1907; mem. of deptmtl. comm., W. African med. staff, 1909.
FIELDING, HENRY.—Sub-insp., Bech. Prot. police, 1896; paymaster, 1903; bec. local major, 1910; is also chief customs off.

FIELDING, HON. WILLIAM STEVENS, LL.D.—B. 1848; mem. for Halifax, Nova Assem., 1882; declined premiership, but govt. without office in that year, and continued in that position till May, 1884, when he became premier, provincial sec., and treas., Aug., min. of finance in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; deleg. to Col. Conf., London, one of H.M.'s plenipotentiaries for negotiating Franco-Canadian treaty, 1907; one of the Comrnrs. to inquire into trade relations between Canada and Brit. W. Indies, 1909; del. to Washington to discuss proposed reciprocity, 1911; defeated at g. e. 1911 and retired.
FIGG, FREDERICK GEORGE.—Ast. observatory, 1872; magnetic observer, 1873; asst., Hong Kong Observatory, 1883; agt., 1897, 1900, 1902 and 1903; director, 1904-1912.
FILBEE, F. W.—B. 1887; apptd. after exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. assignd. to Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1908; to Agric., July, 1908; to C.O., 1909.
FILLEUL, P. R.—Asst. dist. comm., 1910.
FINLAYSON, GEORGE ALEXANDER, logist, Singapore, May, 1903; pathol., 1906.

FIRMSTONE, HAROLD WILLIAM.—ed. at Rugby and Hertford Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. Sttltns., 1890; passed final... Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. dist. offr., South Malacca, May, 1894; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1896; dist. offr. P.W., Oct., 1896; asst. protec. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1897; ag. asst. protec. Chinese, supt. Indian immigr., and mun. comsrr. for Penang, Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1902, and from Aug., 1902; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Feb. to July, 1902; ag. asst. ditto, Penang, Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and from Aug., 1903; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Jan., 1905; mun. comsrr. for Malacca, Apr., 1905; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Sept., 1907, to May, 1908, and from 29th May to 28th June, 1908; ag. inspr. of prisons, Oct., 1909; sen. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, July, 1910; ag. dist. judge, and 1st mag., Sing., July, 1910.
FIRR, TOM FREEMAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Crystal Palace Engng. Schl.; 1st asst. survr., Nyassaland Prot., June, 1903; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1909.

FISCHER, RT. HON. ABRAHAM, P.C. (1911).—Attorney, Cape Colony, 7th Dec., 1871; notary, 14th Dec., 1871; attorney and notary, Griqualand West, 29th May, 1872; advocate and attorney, O.F.S., 27th July, 1875; notary, 2nd Mar., 1878; advocate, attorney, etc., O.R.C., 17th Mar., 1903; formerly mem. of Orange Free State Volkraad, with seat on exec. coun.; chrmn. of cent. comte. of Oranga Union, 1906; prime min. and col. sec., O.R.C., 1907; deleg. to S. Africa Nat. Convention, 1908; deleg. to England in connection with passing of S. Africa Act through Impl. Parl., 1909; mem. of Impl. Conf. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; min. of lands, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

FISET, COLONEL EUGENE, D.S.O., G.G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; apptd. staff adjt., med. corps., Canada, 1902; dir.-gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min. of militia and defence, vice-pres. of the militia coun., 1906; recd. his D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches.

FISHER, RT. HON. ANDREW, P.C. (1911).—B. 1862; M.L.A. for Gympie, Queensland, 1893 and 1899; sec. for rlys. and min. of pub. wks., 1899; returned to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected 1903 and 1906; min. for trade and cust., 1904; deputy leader of Federal Labour Party; chrmn. of Labour party, 1907; prime min. and treas., C. of A., Apr., 1910; represented C. of A. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., and at Imp. Conf., 1911.
FISHER, HENRY GEORGE CURRAL, A.R.I.B.A.—Asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1900; exec. engrnr., 1903.

FISHER, STANLEY.—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrenia, Cyprus, May, 1902; acted as King's advocate, May to Oct., 1902, from May to Oct., 1905, and from Mar. to July, 1907; ag. puisne judge, Apr., 1906, to Feb., 1907; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec., 1903; joint comsrr. for compiling revised edition of Statue Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which recd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.

FISHER, HON. SYDNEY, B.A., P.C. (Brome).—B. 1850; ed. at High Schl. and McGill Univ., Montreal, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; unsuccessfully contested Brome for seat in H. of Commons, Canada, 1880; elected for Brome at general elections, 1882 and 1887; defeated, 1891; again returned for Brome, 1896, and became min. of agric. in Sir Wilfrid Laurier's govt.; re-elected by acclamation at general elections, 1900 and 1904; organised and directed management of Canadian section of Paris Exhibition, 1900; visited Japan to inquire into openings for Canadian trade, 1903; jt. comsrr. for Canada at Washington Conf., to consider the conservation of the natural resources of the continent, 1909; defeated at g.e. 1911, and retired.

FISHER, THOMAS.—B. 1864; served in R.N. from 1868 to 1880; transferred to col. prison ser. as keeper of Antigua prison and head of the Leeward Is. prison ser., Feb. 18th, 1891; supt. of pauper cemetery, 1901; J.P., Antigua, 1902; ag. mag. and manager of Barbuda, Dec., 1903, to May, 1904; ag. supt. of Skerrett's schl., 1903; supt. of St. John's training schl., 1905; dep. chmn. or city comsrr., Antigua, 1907.

FISHER, WILLIAM WOODHOUSE.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; ag. crown counsel, N. circuit, Ceylon, Jan., 1885, to Feb., 1886; ag. dist. judge, Matara, June, 1886, to Feb., 1887; ag. crown counsel, Kandy, Feb., 1887; crown counsel, N.W. prov., Ceylon, Mar., 1887; sec. to comte., for drafting code of civ. procedure, June, 1887, to May, 1888; additional crown counsel, N. circuit, Jan., 1890; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1891; ag. puisne judge, 1894; mag. Jamaica, 1895; puisne judge, S. Sttlmtns., Apr., 1905.
FISHLOCK, WALTER CHARLES.—Trained at Royal Gardens, Kew; agric. instructor, Virgin Is., Apr., 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1903; mem. quarantine bd., 1906; mem. bd. of health, 1908.; J.P., 1908.
FITCHETT, FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1911), M.A., LL.D., New Zealand.—B. 1851; solr.-gen. of New Zealand, 1900-11; is now public trustee.

FITCHETT, WILLIAM HERBERT.—Cllk., agric. dept., Cape Colony, 22nd Feb., 1895; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Wodehouse, 27th Sept., 1898; Port Elizabeth, 23rd Oct., 1899; Coleaburg, 16th Mar., 1900; Britstown, 16th Aug., 1901; acted as C.C. and R.M., Britstown, 1902; asst. regiar., high ct., and chief clk. to the sheriff of the O.R.C., 29th July, 1902; acted as regiar. and sheriff in 1903 and 1904.

FITZGERALD, BRYAN.—B. 1878; 3rd clerical asst., chief commissary's off., B. Guiana, Aug., 1893; 6th cl. clk., audit dept., May, 1895; 5th cls. clk., Nov., 1896; ag. clk. of ct. and gold offr., dept. of mines, Bartica, and navigation offr., for river Essequibo and its tributaries, Nov., 1900, to Feb., 1901, and May to July, 1901; clk. of ct., Corentyne Coast judicial dist., Nov., 1901; J.P., Sept., 1902; 4th cls. clk., G.P.O., Feb., 1904; clk. of ct., Berbice judi. dist., Apr., 1904; J.P., 1904; transf. to G. Coast, Dec., 1907.

FITZGERALD, THE HON. GEORGE PARKER.—B. 1843; mem. of cabinet, Tasmania, without portfolio, 1888-92; while mem. of Tasmanian cabinet was mainly instrumental in obtaining construction of Strahan-Zeehan rly. connecting silver fields with deep water port; was also principal mover in the cause of technical educn.; also chrmn. of tech. educn. comte.; was (with Hon. Wm. Hart) apptd. liquidator of the Bank of Van Diemen's Land in Oct., 1891.

FITZGERALD, HON. ROWAN ROBERT, K.C., D.C.L.—B. 1847; ed. Prince of Wales' Coll., P.E.I.; called to the bar, 1870; K.C., 1880; recorder, City of Charlottetown, P.E.I., 1876; judge, supreme ct. of judicature, P.E.I., and vice-chancellor, ct. of equity, 1894.
FITZGERALD, WILLIAM, M.A., Barr.-at-law.—B. 1845; apptd. asst. deputy min. of finance of Canada and supt. of insurance, 1885.

FITZPATRICK, Right Hon. Sir Charles, P.C. (1908); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr.-gen., Canada, 1896; min. of just., 1902; ch. just. of Canada, 1906; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, 1908.
FITZ-PATRICK, G. R.—Aast. dist. supt. of police, E.A.P., 1909.

FLEMING, Sir Francis, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1842, called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Nov., 1866; crown solr. for Mauritius, 1869; dist. and stip. mag., 1872; acted as dist. judge, Seychelles, 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Barbados, 1878; acted as ch. just., Sept., 1878, to Mar., 1879; acted as ch. just., St. Lucia, July, 1879; priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Strahan, administering the govt. of the Cape, in 1880; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1881; acted as atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1882; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1883; atty.-gen., 1884; acted as ch. just., Mar. to Dec., 1885; col. sec., Natal, July, 1886, but did not take up apptmt., being apptd. col. sec. of Mauritius, Dec., 1886; adminstld. govt., July, 1887, to Dec., 1888; col. sec., Hong Kong, 1889; adminstld. the govt., Feb. to Dec., 1890; gov., S. Leone, 1892; ditto, Leeward Is., 1895; ret. 1901; mem. of distressed colonial seamen comtce., 1909.

FLEMING, Hon. James Kidd.—B. 1868; ed. at common schls., New Brunswick; elec. to legls. New Brunswick, Jan., 1900; re-elec. 1903 and 1908; sworn of the exec. coun., as prov. sec., Mar., 1908; re-elec. by acclamation, 7th Apr., 1908.

FLEMING, Sir Sanford, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.E., C.M.G. (1877), LL.D. (St. Andrews), 1884—B. 1827; engrn.-in-ch., Northern rly., Canada, 1856 to 1863; deleg. to Canadian and Br. govt. from Red River settlers, 1863, in reference to Pacific rly.; nominated by provs. of Canada, N. Brunswick and N. Scotia; govt. engrn.-in-ch. inter-col. rly., 1863; engrn.-in-ch., Canadian Pacific rly., 1871 to 1880; chancellor, Queen's Univ., since 1880; deleg. from Canadian inst. and Amer. meteorological soc., N. York, to internat. geograph. congress at Venice, 1881; representing the Dominion of Canada at internat. prime meridian confce., Washington, 1884; dir. Hudson Bay Co., Canadian Pacific rly., etc.; author of "The Intercol., an Historical Sketch," 1876, "Old to New Westminster," 1884, memoirs on "A Prime Meridian for all Nations," "Universal Time," and various other wks.; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; representative of Canada, col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii, 1894, with Mr. Mercer of the C.O., negotiating for landing place for Pacific cable; mem. of Ottawa improvement coman., 1907.

FLETCHER, A. G. M.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. astt. regis. gen., 1903-4; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1906-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offl. recvr., 1909; astt. to atty.-gen., 1909; regisr. of trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911.
FLETCHER, Geo. Wm.—Temporarily employed in Imp. commissariat, Hobart, 1851; clk. col. treasry., Hobart, July, 1852; receiver and paymr., Jan., 1856.

FLETCHER, Wm., D.S.O.—Surgeon, capt. militia med. staff corps; 6 mos. in 1898 attached to regulars at R.A.M.C. depot, Aldershot; med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1898; served with Ashanti exped., 1900 (medal and clasp); Aro expedn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp).

FLINT, Thomas Barnard, M.A., LL.B. D.C.L.—B. 1847; ed. at Mt. Allison (co. Sackville, New Brunswick; B.A., 1867; M.A., 1872, and Harvard Univ., Boston; LL.B., 1875; barrister-at-law, 1872; sheriff, Yarmouth (co. 1883-7; astt. clk., House of Assembly, Nova Scotia, 1887 to 1891; elected to H. of C., Canada 1891; re-elected, 1896 and 1900; clk. of H. of C. Canada, 11th Nov., 1902; recd. degree of D.C.L. from Mt. Allison Univ., 1903.
FLOOD, J. E. W.—B. 1886; ed. at Port Royal Schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (scholar univ. student); apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910.
FLOWERS, Hon. F.—M.L.C., New South Wales, since 1900; vice-chmn. of party, sub-comtce. on pub. wks., 1904; chmn., 1907; pres. of exec. coun., Oct., 1910.

FOAKER, Frederic George.—Ed. at Service Coll., Westward Ho, and King's London; Impl. Brit. E. Africa Co., Oct., 1894 to Mar., 1894; 2nd cls. astt., Uganda Feb. 26th Aug., 1894; collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Nov., 1902; Impl. Brit. E. Africa Co., medal, Uganda, 1897-1898.
FORAN, William—B. 1871; entered civil service, Canada, 1890; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; sec., bd. ser. examns., 1895; 1st cls. clk., 1906; sec., civil ser. coman., 1908.

FORD, Henry Francis.—Entered civil service, Cape, 1868; promoted to deeds office, 1872; to inaugurate deeds office at Kokstad, and as clk. to ch. mag., Transkei; in charge of office, King William's Town, 1884; ch. clk. conveyance branch, master's office, Cape town, 1888; registr. and mstr. high ct., Grahamstown, July, 1890.

FORD, Joseph Charles.—B. 1832; educated in France (Académie de Douai), and by tuition by Dr. Langley at Wolverhampton; instr. of Jamaica, 1889; one of the comtce. Jamaica internat. exhib., 1891, and spec. comtce. for Bahamas at that exhib.; apptd. sup't. of printing off., Feb., 1891; co-ed. and comp. "Handbook of Jamaica."

FORD, Sir Theodore Thomas, Kt., Bt. (1888).—B. 1829; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1860; puisne judge, S. Sttlms., Mar., 1870; judge of Penang, 1874, to Apr., 1876; resigned, returned to England; re-apptd. senior puisne judge, 1876; ch. justice, 1886; ret., 1889.

FORDE, Robert Michael.—B. 1861; col. surg., Gold Coast, Nov., 1891; astt. coman., Amik, 1892; on special service on French bndy. coman., Feb. to July, 1892; offr. special mission to Kumasi, Dec., 1892-Jan., 1895; col. surg., Gambia, Feb., 1895; and comanr. of ct. of requests, chmn. health and quarantine bd., health of Bathurst and med. offr., Gambia field force, 1895; received thanks of medal and clasp; received thanks of Queen for "sleeping sickness" research, 1901; numerary M.L.C. on several occasions; chmn. mag. and collr. of cust. in 1906; prin. med. S. Leone, Feb., 1907.

FOREMAN, John.—Mont., L.K.Q.C.P.I. and L.M.; med. offr., Virginia, 1878; ag. mag., Virgin Is., 1890; M.L.C. and mem. ex. coun., 1885; ag. med. Dominica, 1882-1884; med. offr., Diego St. Kitts, 1889-1893; med. supt. leper and Pogson hoop., and med. offr., dist. Point, St. Kitts, 1893; surg.-capt., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force.
FORREER, HENRY Augustus.—Cadet, S. Sttlms., Nov., 1909.

FORREST, GEORGE FITZGERALD.—B. 1881; ed. Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; cadet, civ. ser., F.M.S., Nov., 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Jan., 1906; Tangalla, Aug., 1906; supt. of pol., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1907; N. Prov., Sept., 1909; pol. mag., Galle, Nov., 1909; Kandy, 1910.

FORREST, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1891), LL.D. (Camb., 1897), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1847; Hon. Fellow of the Italian geog. socy.; Hon. Fellow of the imp. geog. societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey dept. of W. Australia, 1865; commanded exploring expeds. in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichhardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the S. Coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the W. Coast; to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received thanks of gov. and legis. coun., and gold medal of the Roy. Geog. Socy. of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; dep. survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay district, and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in N.W. Australia, 1878; acted as comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen.; comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1883 to Dec., 1890, during which time was mem. of the exec. and legis. couns.; in Mar., 1883 and 1886, reported on the Kimberley dist., N. Australia, and selected the towns, etc., of Wyndham in Cambridge Gulf; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible govt., in which he took the position of premier and treas., and which he continuously held for over 10 yrs., resigning on 13th Feb., 1902, to join the 1st Commonwealth Govt. of Australia; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian federation convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1875, "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; deleg. to col. confe., 1887; represented W. Australia at the federal convention at Adelaide, Mar., 1897, and in June of the same year was present as rep. of the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; again rep. W. Australia at fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; Melbourne, 1898; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as postmaster-general, Jan., 1901; defence minister, 1901 to 1903; and min. for home affairs until Apr., 1904; treas. of Commonwealth, July, 1906; resig., July, 1907; again treas., Commonwealth of Australia, 2nd June, 1909.
FORSYTH, L. E.—B. 1850; entered Bahamas civ. serv., 1892; Out. Is. comsnr. (1st div.), 1909.

FOSBERY, WIDENHAM FRANCIS WIDENHAM, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1869; cons. agt., Niger Coast Prot., 1893; ag. vice-cons., 1894-5; dist. comsnr., 1896; ag. div. comsnr., 1897-8; pol. offr., central division expndn., 1898 (severely wounded); res., Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Benin Terr. expndn., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comsnr., 1900; pol. offr., Ishan expndn., 1901 (medal with clasp); div. comsnr., 1902; pol. offr., Asaba Hint. expndn., 1902; senior div. comsnr., 1903; pol. offr., Igarra expndn., 1903 (clasp); ag. high comsnr., 1903-4; ag. sec. to adminstr., 1904; dep. high comsnr., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and again in Sept., 1905; prov. comsnr., 1906; ag. col. sec., S. Nigeria, May to June, 1906; ag. gov., June to Aug., 1906; dep. gov. and ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1906.

FOSTER, HON. GEORGE E., B.A., D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1847; ed. Superior Schls. and Univ. of New Brunswick, Edinburgh and Heidelberg; prof. of classics, Univ., N.B., 1873-1877; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1882, for electoral dist. of Kings, N.B.; min. of marine and fisheries, 1886; re-elec., 1887 and 1891 for same constituency; 1896 for York Co., N.B.; 1904 for North Toronto, Ontario; re-elec. 1908 and 1911 for same constituency; min. of finance in Sir John A. Macdonald's govt., 1888 to 1891, and in govt.s. of Sir John Thompson, Sir John Abbot, Sir Mackenzie Bowell and Sir Charles Tupper from 1891-1896; min. of trade and commerce in Mr. Borden's govt., 1911.

FOSTER, HON. RICHARD WITTY.—Mem. of H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1893 to 1906; Comsnr. of pub. wks., 1899; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904; ditto, and min. of agric., 1st March to 26th July, 1906; mem. of H. of B., C. of A., 1909.
FOSTON, EDMUND CHRISTOPHER.—L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); col. surg., Prov.Wellesley (South), S. Sttlms., 24th Aug., 1891; supt. S. S. emigr. depot, Negapatam, 17th Nov., 1900.

FOWLER, FRANK.—B. 1864; asst. clk., pub. wks. dept., Brit. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clk., Jan., 1881; 2nd clk., Apr., 1884; 1st clk. and draftsman, govt. ld. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd govt. survr., May, 1886; senr. govt. survr., Apr., 1896; apptd. J.P., Nov., 1900; asst. comsnr. of lands and mines, Apr., 1902; comsnr. of lands and mines, 1st Apr., 1903; chmn., Shanks Canal comsnr., Mar., 1907; deputy chmn., local govt. bd., Dec., 1907; mem. of local govt. bd.; mem. of bd. of examrs. for land survrs.; mem. of bd. of agric.; has held numerous acting appointments.

FOWLER, GEORGE MERRICK, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1852; ed. at Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., 5th Jan., 1874; ag. asst. collr. of cust., Jaffna, 1st July, 1876; offr. of cls. V., 1st Sept., 1876; ag. landing and tide survr., customa, Galle, 25th Sept., 1877; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, 1st Feb., 1878; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Feb., 1878; ag. off. asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., 4th Nov., 1879; ditto, N. Cent. Prov., 9th Feb., 1880; ditto, N. Prov., 10th Mar., 1882; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, 1st Apr., 1883; ag. dist. judge, Chilau, 21st Oct., 1883; ag. pol. mag., Galle, 10th Oct., 1884; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, 8th Jan., 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya-Vilankulam and Mullaitivu, 1st Feb., 1886; offr. of cls. III., 1st May, 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, 15th Apr., 1891; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 7th Dec., 1894; offr. of cls. II., 16th Dec., 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1897; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., 5th July, 1897; ag. dist. judge, Negombo, 23rd Oct., 1897; govt. agt., Ratnapura, 23rd June, 1898; ag. govt. agt., W. prov., 1st June, 1899; offr. of cls. I., 1st Jan., 1900; govt. agt., W. prov., 10th Feb., 1902; ag. aud.-gen., 14th Apr., 1906; ag. col. sec., 8th Sept., 1906; ag. aud.-gen., 3rd Dec., 1906; ag. col. sec., 5th Dec., 1906; controller of rev., 18th May, 1907; ag. col. sec., 11th July, 1907; ret., Sept., 1907.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; govt.'s off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-06; ch. clk. and acctnt., F.W.D., 1906.

FOX, HENRY WILSON, B.A.—Ed. Charterhouse, Marlborough Coll., Univ. Coll., Lond., Trin. Coll., Cam.; exhibtnr., Trin. Coll., 1882; foundation scholar, 1883; natural sci. trip.; exhibtnr., Lincoln's Inn, 1888; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir C. Mills, K.C.M.G., 1887 to 1889; admitted to practise in sup. ct., Cape Colony, and high ct., S. Rhodesia, 1894; pub. prosecutor, S. Rhodesia, 1894-1897; man. B.S.A. Co., head office, London, 1898; served during 1896-97 in the Matabeleland and Mashonaland rebellion (medal and clasp and ment. in desps.).

FOX, HOWARD ORME.—B. 1865; ed. Marlborough Coll.; King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1889; landing survr., customs, Colombo, Mar., 1896; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, July, 1896; Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Oct., 1896; Matale, Sept., 1896; dist. judge, Tangalla, Jan., 1900; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1902; dist. judge, Batticaloa, May, 1902; asst. land sttlmt. offr., July, 1903; acted as chief sttlmt. offr., 1906, and Apr., 1910.

FOXON, CUTHBERT COLENSO.—B. 1867; elk. and Zulu interp. to asst. comntr. and res. mag., Ndawande dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transf. to Entonjaneni dist., Apr., 1891; to Lower Umfolosi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z'land pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apr., 1893; transf. as elk. and Zulu interp. to Nkandhla dist., Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z'land pol., Apr., 1893; commanded detachment Z'land pol. with the special comn. annexation of Sambanans and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy comntr., Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z'land, Dec., 1897; mag., Nkandhla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904; mag., Umlalazi, 1906.

FOXON, FRANK ERNEST.—3rd class elk. civ. serv., Natal, and 2nd elk. and Zulu interp., Umgeni div., Nov., 1880; ag. elk. of ct., regis. circuit ct., and sub-distributor of stamps, Weenen country, 1886; elk. of ct., dep.-clk. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, Upper Umkomangi div., May, 1887; admstr. native law, Impendlele, Mar., 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. mag., Lower Tugela div., Apr. to June, 1890; ag.
