(1886), K.C., Queensland.—B. 1846; ad. at Univ., Sydney, N.S.W.; B.A., 1863; Mort Travelling Fellowship, 1865; M.A., 1870; called to the Queensland bar, 1867; Q.C., 1876; entered the legis. assem., Queensland, 1872; atty.-gen., 1874 to 1878; sec. for pub. instruction, 1876 to 1878; sec. for pub. wks., 1878 to 1879; led the opposition in the assem., 1879 to 1883; premier, col. sec., and sec. for pub. instruction, Nov., 1883; deleg. to the inter-col. convention held at Sydney, Dec., 1883; resig. office of sec. for pub. instruction, Jan., 1889; mem. of the federal coun. of Australasia, and chmn. of the standing comttee. of the fed. coun., 1886; resig. office of col. sec., Apr., 1886, and accepted newly-created office of ch. sec.; deleg. to col. confec., 1887; ch. sec. and treas., Aug., 1887; pres. of fed. coun., 1888, 1891 and 1893; resig. office after gen. election, June, 1888, and became leader of opposition in assem.; again premier, ch. sec. and atty.-gen., 1890; vice-pres. and chmn. of constitutional comttee. of National Austrn. convention, 1891; ch. just. of Queensland, 1893; lieut.-gov. of Queensland, Sept., 1899; chief just. of high ct. of Australia, Oct., 1903; author of Queensland Criminal Code.
GRIFITH, W. L.—Agt. of the Canadian govt. in Wales for some years; sec. to high comnr., Mar., 1903.

GRIFITH, SIR WM. BRANDFORD, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1858; Gilchrist scholar, 1877; B.A. (Lond.), 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., June, 1881; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast Col., Apr. to Oct., 1884; dist. comnr., 1885; ag. puisne judge, Lagos, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1885; ag. Queen's advoc., 1886 and 1887; compiled ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1887; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888; R.M., St. Catherine, Feb., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., May to Dec., 1893; ch. just., G. Coast Col., May, 1895; admstr. govt. of Lagos, July to Aug., 1896; dep. for gov. G. Coast, Aug. to Nov., 1897; revised ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1898 and 1903; retired, 1911.

GRINDLE, G. E. A.—B. 1869; scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schl., 1891; B.Eng., 1891; elected fellow of C.C.C., 1891; Chancellors English Essay, 1892; M.A., 1894; 2nd class cl. in local govt. board, Feb., 1893; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1893; 2nd class clk. in compet. exam., in office of S. of S. for Cola., Feb., 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Nov., 1898; 1st class clk., 30th June, 1900; priv. clk., 30th June, 1909.

GRINLINTON, F. H., C.M.G. (1903), B.I.E., F.R.G.S., A.I.C.E., F.R. Met., S.-Apptd. Ceylon survey dept., Apr., 1870; dist. clk., Jan., 1877; ch. survr., Nov., 1881; ag. survr., Aug., 1894; survr.-gen., Dec., 1896; ret. in 1904; proceeded to F.M.S. to advise govt. reorganization of survey dept., Oct., 1904.

GRINLINTON, SIR J. J., KT. BACH. F.R.G.S., and A.I.C.E.—B. 1828; was engaged for seven years on the Ordnance Survey of England; gazetted ensign in the 66th Regt.; appd. adjt.; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855 as lieut. 4th King's Own Regt., and asst. during the siege of Sebastopol; was also making a mili. survey of the allied positions; was mentioned in despatches on the capture of Sebastopol, 8th Sept., 1855; Crimean medal clasps; 5th class of the Order of Medjidieh; Turkish Medal; asst. survr.-gen. of India, Sept., 1857; ret'd from the army by the then commn. Oct., 1858; appd. an offl. councillor of municipality of Colombo at its formation, and served for six years; resig. the public service, Oct., 1872, and subsequently followed mili. pursuits; mem. of the central irr. in Ceylon, 1887; a councillor of municipality of Colombo, and mem. legis. coun., Ceylon (to represent gen. European community), Nov., 1888; sec. for Ceylon, Chicago Exhib., 1893.

GROOM, HON. LITTLETON ERNEST, M.A.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, 1901; elected to 1st hse. of rep., Ceylon, 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of commn. on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Nuttallia, 1904-5; several times Crown prizewinner; twice ag. judge of dist. ct., Queensland; for home affairs, C. of Aust., 5th July, 1906; atty.-gen., 13th Oct., 1906; min. of home affairs, June, 1909, to Apr., 1910.

GROSS, D.—Apptd. after compet. exam. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and temp. employed in the bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1906, to Apr., 1907; in the bd. of educ., June, 1907; in the Scotch educn. dept., Aug., 1907; at general post office, Sept. to Jan., 1908; assigned to the C.O., 1908.

GRUBER, JASPER WM.—Employed in the vestry of St. Ann, Jamaica, in 1854; 1st class customs and internal rev., Feb.; parochial treas., May, 1870; harbmr., St. Mary's Otc., 1875; is also treas. parish of St. James.
GUINNESS, SIR ARTHUR BOBT., K.C.M.G. (1911)—B. 1846; called to the bar, N.S.W., 1887; mem. of H. of Representatives, N.S.W., since 1884; chmn. of comttees, speaker of H. of R. since 1903.

GUNN, HUGH.—B. 1870; ed. at Aberdeen, and Aberdeen and Edin. M.A., Edin.; sub-inspr. of schla. under educn. dept., June, 1899; inspr. of O.R.C., 29th Mar., 1902; ch. ditt., 1903; ag. asst. dir. of educn., 17th Aug.; dir. of educn., 1st Feb., 1904; M.L.A., 1904.

GUPPY, ROBERT.—Imperial postal serv., 31st Mar., 1892; oh. clk. and acctnt., post and tel. dept., G. Coast, 1900; ag. dir. of telegraphs, 2nd June to 26th July, 1901; acctnt., 1902; prepared system of accounting passed in Council and brought into force 1st Sept., 1902.
GURNER, JOHN AUGUSTUS.—B. 1855; ed. at Chelt. Coll. and Jesus Coll. (Camb.), LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1877; parly. draughtsman, Victoria, May, 1882; Crown prosecutor, Mar., 1889.

GURNEY, WALTER EDWIN.—Temporarily employed in audit and control off., Cape Colony, June, 1880; apptd. book-keeper, Feb., 1886; inspr. and acctnt., Dec., 1889; ast. cont. and auditor-gen., July, 1890; ag. cont. and auditor-gen., June, 1903; cont. and auditor-gen. and chmn. of tender bd., Nov., 1903; cont. and auditor-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
GUSHUE, GEO. WILLIAM.—B. 1854; M.H.A. for Trinity Dist., Newfoundland, 1894; again elected, 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of pub. wks., 1900.
HAANEL, EUGENE EMIL FELIX RICHARD, Ph.D.(Bral.), Mem. A.L.C.E., A.Ph.S., A.E.C.S., etc.—B. 1841; entered civ. ser., Canada, 1901; apptd. dir. of mines, 1907.

HADDON-SMITH, GEO. BASIL, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1861; lieut. Royal Guernsey militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regt., 1880; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regt., 1881; ast. inspr., Lagos constab., 1886; adjt., 1887; priv. sec. to ag. gov. and clk. of couns., 1887, 1889, and 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. and sheriff of Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; ag. dist. comsnr., 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jebu expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political off. on Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; ast. col. sec., Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expedit., 1896; ch. ass. col. sec., G. Coast, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., G. Coast coll.; political off. to Col. Sir J. Willoocks in the Ashanti expedit., 1900; took part in relief of Kumasi, spec. ment. in desps. (Ashanti medal and clasp); ag. gov. Gambia, July to Oct., 1901; col. sec., S. Leone, Oct., 1901; ag. gov., S. Leone, 1905 and 1907.

HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1850; ed. at Westminster schl. and at Chr. Ch., Oxford; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-gov. of Natal, Mar., 1877; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1880; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1881; acted as col. treas. and also as col. sec., 1885; col. sec., 1887; was chmn. of Natal trade comsn., 1885-6; deleg. to customs and rly. confe., Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov., Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; ret., 1893, on introduction of responsible gov.
HAGGART, THE HON. JOHN G.—Mem. of H. of C., Canada, since 1872; postm.-gen., 1883; min. of rlys. and canals, 1892-6.

HAHN, D. M., M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, Realschool, Hamburg, and Technological Inst., Hamburg; in charge of govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1887; foreman, P.W.D., Jan., 1888; ag. ast. engrnr., 1891; ast. engrnr., in charge of wks. and bldgs., Feb., 1894; ast. engrnr., office and wks., Nov., 1899; engrnr., office and wks., Apr., 1905; ag. div. engrnr., N. Div., 1903 and 1907, in addition to substantive duties; ag. inspr. of mines, 1907 to June 1908; and dep. inspr. of mines, July, 1908, in addition to substantive duties; mem. bd. of industrial training.

HAINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor and chaplain, Selangor, 1890; inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1891; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1899; also ag. headmnr., High schl., Malacca, 1899-1900; col. chap., Penang, Apr., 1901; (Surrogate), hon. chaplain, Penang volunteer corps.

HALCOMB, FRED., M.A. (Wadham Coll., Oxon).—B. 1836; parly. librarian, S. Australia, Jan., 1870; clk., ast. and sergt-at-arms, legis. coun., Apr., 1874; clk. house of assem., May, 1887; clk., legis. coun., and clk. of parliaments, 1901.

HALKETT, J. B.—B. 1845; apptd. 3rd cls. clk., dept. of marine and fisheries, Canada, 1873; junr. 2nd cls., 1880; senr., 2nd cls., 1888; lat. cls., 1902; chief clk., 1905; chief, correspondence, 1st div., 1908.

HALL, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1848; volr. col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, Nov., 1866; clk., gen. bd. of health, June, 1867; clk. med. dept., Nov., 1868; acctnt., immgr. dept., June, 1872; senior clk., Oct., 1880; and ag. ch. clk. several times from 1880 to 1886; inspr. of imigrts., 1886; ag. storekr.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1898; do., Sept., 1901; ditto, 12th Aug., 1903, to 19th Aug., 1904; confirmed storekr.-gen., 1st June, 1907.

HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Ed. at Harrow schl.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1891; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1891; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; aupt. of educn., Penang, Oct., 1894; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Nov., 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1897; sheriff and dep. reglar., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1897; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, Mar., 1898; 2nd ast. col. sec., S. Sttlmnts., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as collr. ld. rev., Penang, till Mar., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treasry, Malacca, Sept., 1900, and confirmed, Aug., 1902; sen. dist. offr., July, 1904; ag. P.M.G., Aug., 1904; ag. inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmnts., Oct., 1905; ag. aud.-gen., S. Sttlmnts., Aug. to Dec., 1907; ag. dist. judge, and 1st mag., Penang, 1909.

HALL, CAPT. W. DURHAM.—Ast. comsnr. of pol., G. Coast, 1894; served in Ashanti expedit., 1895-6 (received the "Ashanti Star"); attached to London Met. Pol. for course of instr., and to L.C.C. for instr. in weights and measures (received certif. from Council), 1896; ag. comsnr. of pol., sheriff, mag. and gov. of pris., G. Coast, 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., 1896; attached to 1st batt. Royal Sussex regt., 1898-99; passed school of instr., 1900; served with Ashanti expedit., 1900 (specially selected to raise levies, ment. in desps., medal and clasp); attached to 1st batt. Royal Rifle Reserve regt., 1900-01; offr.'s musketry certif., Hythe, 1901; apptd. to a company, W. African regt., May, 1901; ag. adjut., June to Dec., 1901; garrison adjut. and intell. offr., S. Leone, Jan., 1902, to May, 1903; A.D.C. to ag. govrr., S. Leone, 1902-3; ast. comanr., N. Nigeria constab., 1903, and assisted in raising and organising the force; served in Bassa expedit., N. Nigeria, 1904 (W. African general service medal and clasp); attached to Brighton pol. for course of instr., 1906; local commdt. of pol. and ast. comanr., Cyprus, Dec., 1906.

HALLETT, WM. CARDY.—B. 1859; ed. at Mt. Allison Univ., New Brunswick; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. to col. survr., Bermuda, 1st Aug., 1881; ast. supt. of pub. wks., 1st Jan., 1887; aupt. of pub. wks., 1st July, 1887; col. survr., 1st Oct., 1887; has also served as suptdg. engrnr. of channel improvement wks., 1894-96, for which received thanks of gov. and exec. coun.; mem. of parish boundaries coman., 1899; spec. employed on survey of Jenkins boiler channel, 1902; visited London in connection with St. George's harbour improvements, Feb. to Apr., 1903; mem. of St. George's harbour coman., 1903; seconded for spec. work preparing plans and documents for St. George's channel wks., 1st Dec., 1903, to 31st Mar., 1904; mem. of Sandys parish road coman., 1904.

HALLEY, JOHN.—B. 1873; ed. Gordon's Coll., Marischal Coll., and Univ. of Aberdeen, M.B., C.M., D.Ph.; civ. surg. attached to R.A.M.C., Sierra Leone, 1900-2; ditto, S. Africa, 1902; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1903; health offr. and local authority, Suva, 1903; supt. of Muana leper asylum, Beqa, 1904; dist. med. offr., Navua, 1905; stip. mag. of the colony, 1905; govt. med. offr., Levuka, 1906; res. comanr., Rotumah, 1908.
HALLEY, ROBT. HENRY.—B. 1878; ed. Dollar Academy, Scotland; clk., central repatriation bd., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1902, to May, 1903; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., May, 1903.

HALLIFAX, EDWIN RICHARD.—Ed. Blundell's schl.; Blundell's scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; passed cadet, 1900; ag. police mag., New Territory, 1899; J.P., 1900; pol. mag., Hong Kong, 1899; asst. supt. of pol., and pol. mag., N.T., 1900; ag. regar.-gen., Mar.-June, 1902; acted as dep. supt. of pol., asst. supt. of fire brigade, capt. supt. of pol., etc., on several occasions, 1902-04; seconded as Transvaal emigrn. agt. at Chingwataong, Mar., 1905; dist. offr., N.T., Sept., 1907; in addition, acted as asst. land offr., N.T., May-Aug., 1907, and Aug.-Sept., 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., Oct., 1909, to May, 1911; ag. dep. supt. of police and asst. supt. of fire brig., May, 1911.

HALLIFAX, F. J.—Cadet, S. Sttltns., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; ag. dist. offr. Penang, Apr., 1896, and Malacca, Nov., 1896; supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1897; dist. offr., June, 1898; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1901; ag. coll. of land revenue, Penang, Apr., 1902; also ag. regar. of deeds and regar. of bills of sale, Penang, May, 1902; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1902, but continued to act as coll. of land rev.; ag. coll. of land rev., and offr. in charge of Treasury, Malacca, Apr., 1903; 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1905; ag. sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1906; pres. mun. comans., Penang, 1st Sept., 1906.

HALL-JONES, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1851; ed. at public schls.; arrived in New Zealand, 1875; mem. of house of representatives for Timaru, New Zealand, since 1890; min. of pub. wks. and marine, 1896; premier col. treas., min. for educn. and labour, June, 1906; resig. premiership, and assumed portfolios of pub. wks. and rlya. in Ward ministry, Aug., 1906; high comanr. in Lond., 1908.

HAMBLIN, R.A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1889; assigned to C.O. July, 1889.

HAMILTON, CHARLES BOUGHTON, C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1850; entered the service, 1865; 2nd clk. rec-gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1873, 1877; col. book-keeper, 1879; ag. rec. gen. 1881; and ag. audr.-gen., 1883 to 1886; received thanks of coun. on leaving for post of rec.-gen., &c., Trinidad, 1886; J.P.; ag. audr.-gen., M.E.C., and M.L.C., 1886; mem. of bd. of educn., 1887; of bd. of health, 1888; M.L.C., 1889; chmn. of comte. for widows' and orphans' fund, 1889; ag. col. sec., 1890; chmn. of improvement comte., and ag. audr.-gen. and sec., 1891; mem. col. coun., 1891; rec. gen. Guiana, Dec., 1892; ag. audr.-gen. and mem. of policy, May, 1893, to May, 1893, and Oct., 1895; sen. comanr., Vlissingen estate; spirits coman., "Trotman," trust comanr., widows' and orphans' fund, &c.; mem. exec. Dec., 1895; off. mem. et. of policy, Jul., 1896; chmn., excise bd., 1901; chmn., educ. com., 1902; vote of thanks of comb. et. for spec. gratuitously rendered in public roads and adminstr., 1894 to 1905; chmn. of comte. sub-dividing colony into districts, 1907; rec. thanks of S. of S. for "valuable services to the Crown and Colony during long and able term of office," Aug., 1908, gort. com. Demerara Rly., July, 1910.
HAMILTON, FREDERICK GRABY.—Ed. Paul's schl., Lond., and Magdalen Coll., Oxf.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 17th Dec., 1910.
HAMILTON, R.—Medical offr., E.A.I., 1911.

HAMILTON, ROBERT WILLIAM.—Ed. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; clas. B.A., 1889 (honours); M.A., 1892; Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1893; comanr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; registr. L Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; asst. admstr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court, Africa, 11th Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve comanr., 31st Oct., 1904; prim. judge, L Prot., 1st Apr., 1905.

HAMILTON, SIR W. A. B., K.C.M.G., C.B. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1844; Harrow; apptd. a jun. clk. in the C.O., after compet. exam.; sent on secret serv. America, 1867; 2nd cls. clk., 30th Sep., 1870; priv. sec., July, 1879; and priv. sec. to Mr. Bull-Hugessen (afterwards Lord Brabourne), 9th Oct., 1872; called to the bar (Inner Temple), Nov., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. James M.P., under sec. of state for the cols., 1874, to 14th Feb., 1878; priv. sec. to Hon. James Lowther, M.P., ch. sec. for 15th Feb., 1878; capt. in the E. Lothian cavalry, 5th June, 1878; major, 18th Jun., hon. lieut.-col. and comdg. regt., 1894; 12th yeomanry brigade, 1895; author Montenello: a Romance of the Civil War; priv. sec. to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., to Sir H. Holland (now Lord Knutsford), 1887, to Aug., 1892; sec. to col. conf. priv. clk., 1st Jan., 1894; ch. clk., Mr. ret., Jan., 1909; offr. of arms of the Order Michael and St. George, 1901-1911; Gr. Usher of the Blue Rod, 1911.

HANCOCK, HERBERT HENRY, M.A.—St. John's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classics, 1876; 2nd class classical finals, 1878; R.A. M.A., 1883; asst. master Cheltenham Col., 2nd master Bosworth schl., 1880; prof. and moral philos. Codrington Coll., 1883; ag. prin., 1889-91; 2nd master, Ques. Coll., Trinidad, 1892; ag. prin., Apr., 1894, June to Aug., 1898, Sept. to Dec., Jan. to Dec., 1908.
HANCOCK, WM. JOHN, M.I.E.E.—M.I.E.E.—B. 1865; hon. loc. sec., I.E.E.; ed. at Univ. of Glasgow; supt. of G.W. Aust., 1885; supt. of telegraphs, 1886; electrical engr., 1894.

HAND, JOHN ST. VINCENT.—1st cls. of s.'s off., Lagos, Oct., 1898; ch. clk., etc. OFF., Lagos govt. rlwy., Apr., 1901; ast. sec., sec's off., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1904; priv. sec. to J. J. Thorburn, Esq., ag. gov. of Lagos, July, 1905; and clk. to exec. coun.
HANDS, ARTHUR L.C.—B. 1880; clk. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1898; ch. clk. and clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1907; J.P., 1910.
HANITSCH, KARL RICHARD, PH.D. (Jena).—B. 1860; demonstrator of Zoology, Univ. Coll., Liverpool, 1887; curator and librarian, Raffles library and museum, Singapore, 1895.

HANLEY, ALLAN HASTINGS, C.M.G. (1903); F.R.C.S.—Med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now S. Nigeria), May, 1893; sen. med. offr., Apr., 1901; dep. prin. med. offr., May, 1903; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1903, to Apr., 1904.

HANN, HUGH.—B. 1862; joined R.A. 1881; served in India, 1882 to 1890; ret. from serv., 1890; joined home prison serv., 1891; promoted clk. and schoolmaster, 1896; supt. of pris., S. Leone, 1891; sheriff and ag. supt. of pol., S. Leone, July to Sept., 1902; J.P. for colony, 1902; formed S. Leone fire brigade and first supt., 1893; 1st ast. supt. of pris., Ceylon, 1906; supt. of pris., Kandy, 1st Nov., 1906, to Sept., 1908; ast. supt., convict establishment, Colombo, Oct., 1908, to Dec., 1909; ag. insp.-gen. of prisons, Ceylon, and supt. of convict establishment, Sept. to Dec., 1909; supt., Mutwal and Hulftsdorp prisons, Jan. to Aug., 1910; supt., Mahara prison, Sept., 1910; ag. insp.-gen. of prisons, Sept. to Dec., 1911; supt., Fremantle prison, W. Aust., 1st Jan., 1912.

HANNA, HON. WM. JOHN, K.C.—Prov. sec. and regiar.-gen. of Prov. of Ontario, Canada; B. 1862; ed. at pub. schl., Brooke Tp., Lambton Co., Ontario; barrister-at-law; an unsuccessful candidate for H. of C. for W. Lambton at g.e., 1896 and 1900; first elected to legislature at g.e., 1902; re-elec. at g.e., 1905; prov. sec. in Whitney adminstr., 8th Feb., 1905; re-elec. by acclam. at bye-elec., 21st Feb., 1905; re-elec. at g.e., 1908 and 1911.
HANNAY, HENRY DESMOND.—Sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. police, 1901; ag. mag., N'Gami, 1907-08.

HANNYNGTON, JOHN ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1911).—Capt., Indian army; temp. maj., King's African Rifles; ed. at Unit. Serv. Coll., Westward Ho!; passed in Hindustani, higher standard, Pushtu, higher standard, Kiswahili, govt. test exam. for promotion to field offr. and staff employ; gazetted 8th June, 1889; apptd. comdt., Jubaland Camel Corps, 15th Dec., 1901; comdt., 6th King's African Rifles, Somaliland Prot., 28th Feb., 1905.

HANSON, B. E.—Educ. Clifton Coll.; clk., exchequer and audit dept., 10th Apr., 1899; clk., audit off., Hong Kong, 1st June, 1900; ag. local aud., 31st Mar., 1901, to 1st Aug., 1902; returned to head office, July, 1903; ast. aud., Uganda, 9th June, 1904; ag. local aud., May, to Nov., 1904, and July to Nov., 1906; local aud., N. Nigeria, 3rd Mar., 1908; local aud., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1909.
HARAN, JAMES AUGUSTINE, C.M.G. (1909); M.D., M.A.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Feb., 1898; med. offr. of health, Mombasa, Nov., 1908; sen. med. offr., 1910.

HARBIN, JOHN ALPHONSE.—Ch. clk. to regiar., Grenada, July, 1884; ditto to regiar. sup. ct., July, 1886; marshal V.-A. ct., Mar., 1887; ag. regiar. sup. ct., and ag. regiar. in V.-A. ct., May, 1888, to Apr., 1890; acctnt., treas., July, 1890; sec. to coman. pub. wks. dept. inquiry, Dec., 1890; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1892, to Feb., 1893; ag. inspr. of schools Feb., 1893, to Mar., 1894; inspr. of schools, Mar., 1894; ag. col. postmaster, Aug.-Sept., 1894; mem. bd. of ed. Apr., 1895; ag. regiar. sup. ct., Aug.-Sept., 1901; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. and assts. treas., Carriacou, Dec., 1901, to Jan., 1902.
HARBOUR, E. W. H.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to bd. of trade, 22nd Jan., 1901; transfd. to C.O., 22nd July, 1901.

HARCOURT, ALEXANDER BERNARD.—B. 1863; apptd. cons. agt. in Oil R. Protect., Oct., 1892; ag. v.-cons., Bonny dist., June, 1894, to July, 1895; dist. comsnr., Bonny, 1st Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. cent. div. on various occasions; transf. to Cross Riv. div., Dec., 1899; travllng. comsnr., S. Nig., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. dival. comsnr., Cross Riv. div., May-Dec., 1900; ag. res. Benin City Territories, 1901 and 1902; divl. comsnr., Cross River, 17th Jan., 1903; polit. offr., M'Kpani expdn., 1903 (medal and clasp); ag. sec. and dep. high comsnr., 24th Feb. to 3rd Apr., 1905; ast. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., 1st May, 1906; ag. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., June to Dec., 1906; ditto, W. Prov., 1st Jan. to 31st Mar., 1907.

HARCOURT, Rt. Hon. LEWIS, P.C. (1905).—B. 1863; ed. at Eton; Hon. D.C.L., Oxford; a trustee of the Wallace Collection and London Museum; M.P. for Rossendale, Lancashire, since 1904; first comsnr. of wks., 1905; sec. of state for the colonies, Nov., 1910.

HARDING, ALFRED J.—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam., as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comte, to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ast. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal comsn. on Mauritius, 1909; ag. 1st cl. clk., Sept., 1910, to Jan., 1911.

HARDING, COLONEL COLIN, C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1863; formerly ch. inspr. of native police, Mashonaland; served in Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-7; comdt. of Barotse native pol., 1901-1906; dist. comsnr., Northern territories, G. Coast, 1910.

HARDING, EDWARD J., M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comte., 1909.

HARE, FREDERICK ARTHUR.—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Wicklow; clk. to mags., Vasse, W. Australia, 1880; clk. and asst. priv. sec. to Sir William Robinson, during various periods from 1880 to 1882; inspr. of pol. for the S. dists. of the col., 1882; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, Cambridge Gulf, 1886-7; ag. govt. res., Albany, 1887-8; res. mag. York, 1888-97; warden and res. mag. East Coolgardie Goldfield, 1897-1900; comsnr. of police, 1900.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES.—B. 1853; clk. treasy., W. Australia, Apr., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dists., June, 1886; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; sec., W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1891; ag. agent-gen., 1909.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; and to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.

HARPER, CHARLES HENRY.—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 5th May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, 24th Jan., 1900; attached to Ashanti field force, 1st June to 1st Sept., 1900; asst. dist. commnr., 6th Sept., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec., 26th Oct., 1904; asst. col. sec., 8th Feb., 1906; ag. chief asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 14th Apr. to 25th June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, 16th Oct., 1908; prov. commnr., 23rd July, 1909; census offr., 1911.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY.—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900.

HARRIGAN, SAMUEL SIMONS.—Head teacher, Wesleyan schls., Virgin Is., 1878; resig., 1903; clerical asst., govt. offices, Road Town, 1903; ag. govt. offr., West End, Nov., 1904, Feb., 1905, and Feb. to Apr., 1907; govt. offr., Road Town, 1906.

HARRIOTT, ALEXIS WYNNS.—Ed. at Yale; B.A. 1856; M.A. and B.Ph., 1859; ag. col. surr. and inspr., P.W. Turks and Caicos Islds., Feb. to Apr., 1864; J.F., 1879; unoffl. mem. legis. bd., 1881 to 1888, when apptd. asst. commnr. Grand Turk; ag. commnr., Apr. to July, 1888; manager savings bk., 1889; mem. bd. of educn., 1891; coroner, 1893; ag. auditor on various occasions, 1888-1906; ag. offl. mem. of legis. bd., 1904 and 1905; asst. commnr., Salt Cay, July, 1907.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—B. 1865; clk. to commnr., and to legis. bd., Turks and Caicos Islds., Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891, and 1902; ag. regisr. sup. ct., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. asst. commnr. (dist. mag., regisr. sup. ct. and regisr.-gen.), man. savings bank, and inspr. of pol. and prisons), G. Turk, May to July, 1896, Aug. to Nov., 1901, and May to Aug., 1906; J.P., 1901; ag. aud., Aug. and Sept., 1901, and June to Aug., 1906; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag.acctnt. and clk. (coll. of cust., postmr., cashier and regisr. shipping); commnr.'s office, Apr. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; ag. offl. mem., legis. bd., 1906; int. cls. clk., rev. dept., Jamaica, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. commnr., Salt Cay, May to July, 1909; ag. deputy stamp commnr., Aug. to Nov., 1909.

HARRIS, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1856; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancred student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Comfee., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1896; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bndry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bndry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana bndry. arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comttee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; represents C.O. on advisory comttee. of comml. intell. B. of T.; mem. of managing comttee. of Imp. Inst.; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govrs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRISON, CUTHBERT WOODVILLE—B. 1874; ed. at Sherborne Schl., Clifton Col., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wa Eq.; 3rd cl. class. mods., 1894; cadet, F.M., Nov., 1897; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, 1898; also ag. harb. mast., Klang, Feb. to passed cadet, Apr., 1901; asst. dist. offr., Langat, continued also as ag. asst. dist. of Klang, and harb. mast. at Port Swettenham, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., ditto, Tampin, Mar., 1904; also asst. dir. of Matang, Oct., 1904; ag. asst. sec. to res. Bd., 1906; ag. sec. to res., Aug., 1906; ag. asst. sec. res., Dec., 1906; ag. regisr., aug., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, Feb., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Larut, and regisr. of titles, North, Aug., 1908; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 1909; continued ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut.

HARRISON, JAMES HERBERT—Ed. Regent's Park Coll., and New College, and at University Coll. and Hospital; F.R.C.S. (London); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); dist. mag., surg., Cayo, Br. Honduras, 1890; ag. dir., Orange Walk, 1891, 1892; dist. mag., surg., Orange Walk, June, 1892; asst. col. Belize, 12th May, 1897; col. surg., 1st Sept., 1897.

HARRISON, JOHN BURCHMORE—(1901).—B. 1856; Nat. Sc. scholar, Christ Camb., B.A. (Nat. So. Tripos), 1878; M.A. prof. of chemistry and agricult. science, 1879; govt. analyst and prof. of Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; director of science and agric., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1890; F.I.C., F.G.S., F.C.S., and F.G.S. of author of numerous papers relating to the geology of the W. Indies and B. Guiana; spec. serv. to U.S.A. in connection with sugar, relating to sugar analysis, recd. thank. govt.; specially thanked on several occas. S. of S. for services in connection with agriculture, etc.

HARRISSON, SYDNEY THIRWALL—(1908).—Ed. Blackheath; apptd. asst. P.W.D., G. C. Col., 9th Nov., 1894; ed. 2nd June, 1897; asst. acct., W.A.F.F., 1898; ch. acct., 8th Nov., 1899; treas. 1st Jan., 1900; comptroller of customs, 1908.
HART, WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1847; public library and museum, Mauritius, 1903.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY.—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford; served in 13th I.Y. in South Africa, 1900; medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Smith, Cyprus, 1901; asst. insp. of Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in French and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Geo., 1906; dist. commnr., 1906 to 1910; sec. commnr. for the Western Pacific, 1910.

HARTE, LOVELACE FRED. EDWARD—First class certif. at I.C. Coll. in Dearticled law student, 3rd Jan., 1876; peace the same year; Jan., 1877, clk., dept., Trinidad; clk. in audit office, Sep., 7th clk., Jan., 1883, June, 1887; 2nd clk., Oct., 1887; ch. clk., 1897; also commnr. whole isld.; ag. dep. registr. on occasion.
HARTLEY, CHAS.—M.A., Cantab.; English and modern languages, Royal Col., 11th Dec., 1896; principal, Royal Coll., 1903.
HARTLEY, R. B., C.M.G. (1901).—Lieut.-Col., C.M.R.; created V.C. for in the operations against the strong

HARTLEY, JOHN WHITTAM.—Ed. at Owens Coll., Manchester; M.B., Ch.B., Mar., 1904; Kaye (buttleworth schlr., Victoria Univ., 1898-1901; emp. astt., medical dept., Hong Kong, Aug., 1906; surg.-ltnt., Hong Kong vol. corps., Aug., 1907; rly. med. offr., Oct. to Dec., 1906; astt. supt., G.C.H., Dec., 1906, to Oct., 1907; rly. med. offr., Oct., 1907; consulting med. offr., New Territories; J.P., 1910; lect. on fevers, Hong Kong coll. of med., 1909; ag. astt. med. offr. of health, Apl.-Nov., 1911.

HARVEY, GEORGE LEONARD, C.E.—Astt. dir. of wks., So. Nigeria, June, 1900; astt. dir. of pub. wks., 1901; deputy dir. of pub. wks., 1901; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar. to Nov., 1902, and Apr. to Nov., 1904.

HARVEY, HON. HOBACK, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll., Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1889; admitted to practice in N.W.T., 1893; regiar. of land titles for South Alberta land regist., dist., 1896; dep. atty.-gen. of N.W.T., 1900; puisane judge, sup. ct., N.W.T., June, 1904; puisane judge, sup. ct. of Alberta, 1907; ch. just. of Alberta, Oct., 1910.
HARWARD, JOHN.—Principal, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 1st Apr., 1892; dir. of pub. instrn., 23rd June, 1903.

HASKETT-SMITH, W. J. J. S.—B. 1881; ed. R. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst, 1899-1901; 2nd lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1901; lieut., 1905; served in S. Africa with R. Irish Rifles and Moun. Inf., 1901-2 (Queen's medal and 3 clasps); lieut. 2nd batt., W.A.F.F., N. Nigeria, 1904-6; Gambia Company, 1907.
HASKINS, WILFRED J., A.M.C.E.A.—Astt. supt. of wks., S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1906.

HASTINGS, A. C. G.—Served in 43rd Co. I.Y. in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1901; attached to vice-consulate, Fez, Dec., 1901, to Sept., 1905; ag., vice-consul, Nov., 1903, to May, 1904; astt. res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1911.

HASTINGS, W. C. N.—D.S.O. (1902); 2nd lieut., 4th batt. South Wales Bord., 1891; capt., 1895; astt. inspr., S. Leone Frontier pol., 1898; inspr., 1899; ag. dist. comsrr., Koinadugu dist., 1899; S. Leone expdn., 1898-1899 (medal with clasps); 2nd lieut., Manchester regt., 1899; capt., 1901; S. Africa, 1900-1902 (ment. in desps., two medals and five clasps); lieut., N. Nigeria regt., 1903; captain, 1903; Munshi expdn., 1906; in command of Gambia company, W.A.F.F., 1906.

HASZARD, HON. FRANCIS LONGWORTH.—B. 1849; ed. at Prince of Wales's Coll., Prince Edward Is.; called to the bar, 1872; K.C., 1894; elected to legis., P.E. Is., 1904 and 1908; mem. exec. coun., 1905; prem. and atty.-gen., P.E. Is., 1906; judge, supreme ct., and master of the rolls, P.E.I., 1911.
HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transfd. to C.O., 18th Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902.
HATHERTON, BARON.—COL. THE HON. EDWARD GEORGE PERCY LITTLETON, C.M.G. (1880); B. 1842; late mil. sec. to the gov.-gen. of Canada.
HATTON, EDWIN FULLERTON, M.D., M.R.C.S.—Ed. at Upper Canada Coll. and Toronto Univ.; entd. col. serv., 1882; is now med. supt. of lunatic asylum and poor asylum, and dist. med. offr., Grenada; M.L.C.

HAWKINS, ISAAC THOMAS, M.I.C.E.—B. 1858; mem. of incorp. assoc. of mun. and county engrns.; diplomas in engineering science and construction, sanitary science and pub. health law; articled and served through shops, 1875-1881; held two chief mun. apptmts., 1882-1883, and 1888-1897 (including the designing and carrying out of an extensive scheme of water supply); col. survr., Lagos, 19th June, 1897; dep. dir. of pub. wks., 1st Jan., 1898; prov. engnr., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; ag. D.P.W. for an aggregate period of 4½ years, between 1897 and 1906.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot batt. at Dum Din in 1859; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volrs. in 1868 at their formation; astt. inspr., G. Coast armed native pol., 1875; dist. comsrr. of Accra, Oct., 1875; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Apr., 1877; astt. col. sec. G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius; admstr. of the Gambia, 1885; adminstd. the govt. of the W. Africa Sttlmts., from July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; gov., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yonnie expdn., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admstr., Barbados, 1891; gov., Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

HAYCRAFT, THOS. WAGSTAFFE.—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; exam. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. cham. of arbitration, 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larncas, Cyprus, 1899; ag. Queen's advocate, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."
HAYES-WILLIAMS, WILLIAM GORDON.—B. 1862; regiar.-gen., New South Wales, 1898.

HAYNES, THOS. BENJAMIN.—Apptd. dispenser, penal settlement, Massaruni, B. Guiana, 6th June, 1870; 1st cls. prison warder, 12th July, 1877; schlmtr. to penal settmts., 1st Sept., 1878; dispenser in charge of leper asylum, Kaow Is., 27th Oct., 1879; clk. to penal settmts., 27th Oct., 1883; keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 5th July, 1887; supt., Georgetown prison, 19th June, 1894.
HAYNES, W. LINDSAY H.—Clk. to ch. just., Barbados, 1874-8; clk. to comsrr., apptd. to investigate offences against rioters, 1876; inl. rev. offr., dist. B, 1878; now inspr. of inl. rev. offrs. regir.

HAYWOOD, CECIL WALTER INGLEFIELD WIGHTWICK.—Ed. in Germany and Belgium; E. Tel. Co., 1895-1900; 3rd astt., Uganda Prot., 15th Apr., 1901; astt. collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902; collr., 1st Apr., 1905.

HAZELAND, FRANCIS ARTHUR.—B. 1861; clk. to puisane judge, Hong Kong, Nov., 1878; ag. dep. regir., Mar. to Nov., 1882; clk. to ch. just., Sept., 1883; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. office, Mar. to Oct., 1890; ag. clk. of coun., Mar. to Dec., 1890; dep. regir. and acctnt., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1894; ag. regir., sup. ct., offl. trustee, regir. of companies and land offr., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; has passed in Chinese (higher standard); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Hilary, 1900; ag. pol. mag., June, 1900, to June, 1901; 2nd pol.mag., 7th June, 1901; ag. 1st pol. mag. and coroner on several occasions, 1902-7; 1st pol. mag. and coroner, 21st Mar., 1909; ag. atty.-gen., 28th Oct., 1909, to 1st May, 1910; ag. puisne judge, from 1st May, 1910, to 13th May, 1911.

HAZEN, JOHN DOUGLAR, K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis., for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers' soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and mem. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911.

HEAN, HON. ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1912).—B. 1859; warden and coun. of Sorrell mun., Tasmania, since 1886; M.L.A. for Sorrell since 1903; min. of lands and wks., agric. and rlyws., and comnr. of main roads, July, 1904; ag. premier, 1911.
HEARSEY, HERBERT HYDE YOUNG.—M.B., C.M. (Edin.), 1890; med. offr., Nyassaland Prot., June, 1896; prin. med. offr., Apr. 1902.

HEATH, ASHTON MARLER, A.M.I.C.E. (1893); M.I.M.E. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Manchester gram. schl.; served at Messrs. Sharp, Stewart & Co.'s loco. wks., Manchester, 1877-1882; asst. under loco. supt., L. & S. W. Rlyw., 1882-1887; inspr. under Sir A. M. Rendel, K.C.I.E., 1887-1897; chief asst. engr. to J. Carruthers, Esq., M.I.C.E., consulting engr. to W. Aust. and N. Zealand govt., 1897-1900; entd. Crown Agents' office as head of engineering and wks. dept., 1900; head of engineering insptn. dept., and chief inspecting engr. since 1904; mem. of engineering standards comm., 1905; assoc. mem. of mech. transport comm., War Office, 1907; mem. of deptl. conference on leadless glaze, 1908.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A," Dominica, 1904.

HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. at King's Schl., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1897; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrenia div., gov. of prison and dep.coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as comnr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high comnr., Sir C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sec.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in comm. of pol., Kyrenia div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; asst. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in comm. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907.

HELLIER, MAURICE.—Mast. of govt. English schl., S. Stiltna, Dec., 1887; ag. sub-inspr. of schl., Malacca, Nov., 1904; ag. head, Malay Coll., Malacca, Sept., 1905; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, May, 1906; ag. inspr. of schl., Singapore and Malacca, Dec., 1906; confirmed, Jan., 1907; ag. dir. of educn., S. Stiltna, and F.M.S., Mar. to July, 1908.

HELLINGS, ROBERT BAILEY.—B. 1863; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1885; addl. pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1888; asst. collr., etc., esa Trincomalee, Nov., 1888; asst. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1889; dist. judge, Kegalle, 1893, Dec., 1893; pol. mag., Galle, July, 1895; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1895; dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1899; comnr. of rlyw., Colombo, Aug., 1900, May, 1904; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1901; asst. gov't. agt., Nov., 1904; gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1906; ag. gov't. agt., Cent. Prov., 1910.

HELY-HUTCHINSON, THE RIGHT HON. WALTER FRANCIS, P.C. (1909), G.C.M.G., K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883), B. 1878; ed. of Trin. Coll., Camb., called to the bar; mem. of the Inner Temp.; accompanied Sir T. Robinson to Fiji as special attache on the occasion of the cession of the islands to Gr. Brit., Sept.-Oct., 1874; priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, 10th Oct., 1874, for Fiji affairs; priv. sec. N.S. Wales affairs, Jan., 1875; col. sec. ados., Nov., 1877; ch. sec. to gov't. of Nga. and liqut.-gov't. and ch. sec. to gov't., 1878; Windward Is., 1889; gov. of Natal, 1890; Cape, 1901; ret., 1910.
HEMMANT, E. V.—Asst. dist.comnr., 4th Sept., 1908.

HEMSTED, CHARLES SELWOOD.—Asst., E. Africa Prot., 20th Apr., 1900; 1st Apr., 1903; dist. comnr., 1st Apr., 1903; asst., E. Africa Prot., 19th Sept., 1903; acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901; acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1901; collr., 17th Dec., 1906.
HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS—Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. L.R.C.P. (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 18th Oct., 1904.
HENDERSON, ALEXANDER.—B. 1878; clk. refugee camps, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1902; attached to audit dept., Apr., 1902; enctts., treas., 20th Aug., 1902; acctnt., agric., 1st July, 1904.
HENDERSON, J. T.—Shorthand reporter, debates, legiun, Natal, 1883.

HENDRIE, LIEUT.-COL. HON. JOHN HARN, C.V.O. (1907).—B. 1857; ed. schls., Hamilton, and Upper Canada; engr., mayor of Hamilton, 1901-2; ed. legis., 1902, 1905, and 1908; min. with folio, 1902; chmn. rly. comte., Ont.; mem. hydro-electric coman.; mem. board of coman.; mem. Strathcona trust; Canadian Militia; comd. 2nd Bde. Ont. Artill., 1904 to 1909; ex-pres. Can. Artill.; ex-pres. Ontario Artill. Assoc.

HENDY, CHARLES LOWICK.—B. 1878; clerical asst. inspr., prisons office, Br. Guernsey; ag. clk. of supplies and sec. to the bd., July to Oct., 1885; July to Sept., 1888; ag. clk. of supplies, July, 1890, to Jan., 1891; issuer to convicts, Massaruni, Nov., 1890; clk. of supplies, convicts, to bd. of prisons, June, 1892; ag. sup. of asyl., Nov. to Dec., 1895, and Feb. to July, 1896; ag. man. dir. orphan asyl., in addition to ag. clk. of supplies, from July to Nov., 1896; ag. clk. of supplies, from July to Nov., 1896; orphan asyl., July, 1901, to Jan., 1903; almshouse, and sec., poor law coman., 1903, to Apr., 1904; resumed duties as clk. of supplies, prisons dept., Apr., 1904; almshouse, and sec., poor law coman., Dec., 1909; resumed duties as clk. of supplies, prisons dept., Apr., 1904; almshouse, and sec., poor law coman., Dec., 1909.
HENNESSEY, PATRICK H.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), R.C.P. (Lond.); house surg., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1907; supernumerary med. offr., Penang, Feb., 1908.
HENSON, STANLEY B.—Police probationer, S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1907.

HENNIKER-HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. Oxon.; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, hons. hist., 1901; cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec's off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regt.-gen.'s off., Malay, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1903, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. clk., native dept., and ag. ast. native comsnr., July, 1906; apptd. ast. native comsnr., Mar., 1907.
HERAT, PUNCHI BANDA.—B. 1888; ed. at Roy. Coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1910.
HERBERT, CHAS. EDWARD.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1900-05; govt. res. and judge of N. Territory, 1906 to 1910; now deputy chief judicial offr. in Papua.

HERCHENRODER, FURCY ALFRED, K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid.Tem., June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge, Seychelles, 1900; ch. just., 1903; proc. and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1905.

HEREFORD, GEORGE A.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1904; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Mar., 1906; ast. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1907.

HERN, W. STANLEY.—B. 1876; lieut., Duke of Edinburgh's Wiltshire Regt.; served in Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-1897; in S. Africa, 1900-1902; with W.A.F.K., 1904-05; offr. comdg. preventive service, Lagos, 1906; dist. comsnr., June, 1906; offr. comdg. Ijebu Ode, 1906; dist. comsnr., Ikorodu and Sagamu, 1907.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.L.A.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C., min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

HEWBY, WILLIAM PETCH, C.M.G. (1902), F.R.G.S.—B. 1866; ed. privately; entd. service of Royal Niger Co. (then the National African Co.), 1883; awarded the Company's medal; 1st cls. rea., N. Nigeria, 1st Oct., 1901.

HEWITT-FLETCHER, STANLEY.—B. 1866; Mem. Inst. Chartd. Acctnts., 1892; 2nd acctnt., Br. C. Africa Prot., June, 1893; collr. and mag., Zomba dist., Apr., 1900; British vice-consul and admtn. agt., Chinde, Jan., 1901; H.M.'s consul, Chinde, 30th Sept., 1909 (Cent. Africa medal and clasp).

HEYDON, C. G., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and M.L.C., N.S. Wales, 1893; dist. ct. judge, Mar., 1900; pres. of arbitn. ct., and pres. of indust. ct., June, 1905; sole comsnr. for consolidating statutes of N.S. Wales, 1906.

HEYWOOD, ARTHUR WM.—B. 1853; ast. to supt. of woods and forests, Cape Col., Jan., 1884; dist. forest offr., 1888; conservator, Knyasa, Mar., 1895; ditto, Transkeian Territories, May, 1898; ditto, King William's Town, July, 1907.
HICKIE, CLARENCE FREDERIC.—Acctnt., Uganda rly., 1st Apr., 1898; ast. ch. acctnt., 1st Nov., 1899; 3rd treasy. ast., E. Africa Prot., 1st May, 1903; 2nd treasy. ast., 28th Nov., 1903.

HICKSON, ROBERT R. PURDON, I.S.O. (1910), M.I.C.E.—Res. engr., Carlingford harbour wks., Ireland, 1866 to 1872; res. engr., Barrow harbour wks., England, 1872-6; engr.-in-ch. harbours and jetties, S. Australia, 1876 to 1881; ast. engr., harbours and rivers, N.S. Wales, 1881-9; comsnr. and engr.-in-ch. roads, bridges, and sewerage, 1889 to 1895; under sec. for pub. wks., and comsnr. for roads, 1896-1901; vice-pres.of pub. wks. tender bd., and chmn. of deptmtl. bd. of reference, N.S.W.; J.P. for S.A. and N.S.W.; pres. of Sydney harbour trust since 1901.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., 22nd May, 1905; ast. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911.

HIGGINS, HON. HENRY BOURNES.—B. 1851; M.L.A., Victoria, 1894-1900; chmn. of royal coman. on legal procedure; mem. of Austn. Fed. Convention, 1897-98, and of the first Federal parlmt.; judge of High Court, Oct., 1906; and pres. of federal arbitn. ct.

HIGHET, DAVID JOHN, M.I.C.E.—First ast. engr., Ulu Selangor extension, Oct., 1890; ch. ast. engr., Jan., 1894; ag. res. engr. for rlyws., Mar., 1894; dist. rlyw. engr., F.M.S.R., Jan., 1903; ag. divisional engr., Selangor and N. Sembilan, June, 1904; divisional engr., South, Apr., 1906; ag. gen. man., F.M.S.R., May, 1909.

HILL, BERTRAM.—B. 1864; ed. Christ Hosp., and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1888; pol. mag., Kalutara, Sept., 1890; off. ast. to govt. agt., E. Prov., May, 1891; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1895; ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, July, 1897; Matara, May, 1901; dist. judge, Negombo, Dec., 1902; ast. govt. agt., Chilaw, Oct., 1903; pol. mag., Colombo, Apr., 1905; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, May, 1909; Kurunegala, Jan., 1910.

HILL, CHARLES W., F.R.G.S.—Clk., lower div., ch. sec's office, Cyprus, Jan., 1886; passed in Greek, May, 1888; acted as clk., exec. coun., Aug., 1889, to Mar., 1890; and as priv. sec. to admstr., Oct., 1889, to Mar., 1890; govt. clk., shipping master, clk. of couns., and J.P., Falkland Is., 1892; also priv. sec. to gov.; ag. col. treas., collr. of customs, postmr., &c., 1893; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., &c., 1894; special comsnr. on land question, W. Falkland, 1895; ag. inspr. of schools, 1895; special mission to S. Chili, 1897; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1897; col. treas., collr. of cust., and postmr., Falkland Isds., Oct., 1897; ag. col. sec., May, 1899 to June, 1900; senr. ast. treas., G. Coast, 1901; ag. comsnr., Volta dist.; ditto, Winnebah dist., 1901; ag. treas., G. Coast, 1903; postmr., Gibraltar, 1908.
HILL, ERNEST.—M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Lon.; dip. pub. health, Camb.; health offr. for Natal, 1st Sept., 1901; mem. of bd. of health, 21st Nov., 1901; mem. of bd. of trustees, Grey's hosp.; retired.
HILL, G. H.—Jun. clk., P.O., O.R.C., Dec., 1904; typist, audit dept., July, 1905.
HILL, J. K.—Man., govt. farm, agric. dept., Naivasha, E.A.P., 1st Apr., 1907.

HILL, J. K. D.—Entered treasy., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1879; 6th clk., Feb., 1881; 2nd book-keeper, aud. office, Apr., 1881; 1st ditto, Mar., 1882; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1883; 1st ditto, July, 1889; sec. P.W. dept. comm. of inquiry, 1889; ag. ch. clk., 1890-94, and 1895; ag. ch. clk., audit office, 1894-5; apptd., Sept., 1896, acctnt. sup. ct. of civ. justice, and acctnt. regar.'s office; student Line's Inn, July, 1898; passed final exam., Mar., 1899; stip. mag., J.P., and coroner, July, 1899; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1904; sen. mag., Georgetown, Jan., 1907; chmn., regar.'s off. enquiry comm.; mem. of financial regna. comtee., 1909; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1911.
HILLMAN, GEORGE FRANCIS.—Ed. Highbury New Park Coll., Lond.; admitted to the bar of supreme ct., W. Aust., 1898; apptd. clk. of legis. coun. and clk., of parlte., June, 1901.

HILLYER, HERBERT KEYS—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900; shorthand writer and asst. clk., col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, Jan., 1908; attended durbars of native chiefs, Colombo, July, 1908; Kandy, May, 1909; Colombo, May and June, 1909; Kandy, July, 1910; Jaffna, Aug., 1910; Colombo, Aug., 1910; sec. to Colombo Lake development scheme comm., 1909; jt. sec. to agric. banks comtee., 1909; sec. to opium comm., 1909; sec. to legis. coun. constitution comm., 1910; sec. to tuberculosis diseases comm., 1910; secretariat asst., July, 1910; sec. to ankylostomiasis comtee., 1910; sec. to King Edward VII. memorial fund comtee., 1910.

HIME, Rt. Hon. Lieut.-Col. Sir A. H., P.C. (1902), C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1876), (late R.E.)—B. 1842; entered roy. engnr. in 1861; became capt. in 1874; employed in Bermuda under col. govt., from 1869 to 1871 in construction of a causeway and iron swing bridge connecting the island of St. George with the main island, for which he received the thanks of the legislature and the acknowledgements of the S. of S. for the cols.; col. engnr. of Natal, May, 1875; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., 1876; employed on survey of boundary between Orange Free State and Natal, 1884-5; ret., 1893; mem. legis. assm., 1897; min. of canals and works, 1897; premier, Natal, 1899; resig., 18th Aug., 1903.
HIMSORTH, WILLIAM.—B. 1847; ed. priv. schl., Toronto, and old high schl., Quebec; grad. mil. schl., Quebec, 1865; entd. inland rev. dept., Quebec, June, 1868; sec. of dept., 1st July, 1884.

HINE, HUMPHREY ALBERT.—Student Zulu interp., Natal, 29th Dec., 1885; resig., 31st Jan., 1887; 3rd cls. clk., audit office, 14th Jan., 1888; transf. to treasry., 8th Apr., 1889; ag. olk., exec. coun., and clerical asst., gov'r's office, 15th Oct., 1889; ag. clk., gov'r's office, 3rd Feb., 1890, to 29th Sept., 1893; ministerial clk., col. sec.'s office, 1st Nov., 1893; 2nd cls. clk., 1st July, 1894; ag. ch. clk., gov'r's office, 1st Apr., 1895; has held apptmt. as clk., exec. coun., since 6th Dec., 1899; ag. sen. clk., agric. dept., 12th Aug., 1897; sec. to min. of agric., 1st Jan., 1898; asst. under-sec., 1st Nov., 1901; acctg. offr., col. sec.'s dept., 1st July, 1905.

HINDE, SIDNEY LANGFORD.—Chevalier de l'Ordre Royal du Lion; ed. in France and Germany, Clare Coll., Camb., and St. Bart.'s Hosp., London; Congo Free State serv., 1891; Arab campaign, 1892 to 1894, medal and star; med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 27th Sept., 1895; Mbaruk rebellion medal; collr., 1897; sub-commsn., 1st Apr., 1902.
HINDS, CHARLES JOHN CLIFFORD.—Served with S.A.C. in South African war, 1901-2 (Queen's medal and four clasps); acctnt. to land settlmt. dept., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902.

HIRST, GEORGE S. S.—Ed. at Perse Sch., Camb., and Camb. and Edin. Univs.; M.B., C.M. Edin., 1893; asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 1896-1898; asst. commsn. and govt. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks and Caicos Is., 1905-7; commsn., Cayman Is., 1907.

HOAR, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1864; apptd. under prison commrs., England, after civ. sc. exam., 1892; gaoler, St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1892, to Dec., 1898; ag. ch. keeper of prisons, Leeward Is., May to Nov., 1897; ch. warder, Belize, B. Hond., Jan., 1899; ag. keeper of prisons, B. Hond., in 1900-05-06-07-08-09.

HOBLEY, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1904).—Geologist to Impl. Br. E. Africa Co., Mar., 1884; 1st cls. asst. Uganda Prot., Aug., 1894; asst. dep. commsn. E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902; ag. commsn., 27th June to 1st Aug., 1904; assoc. mem. instit. of civ. engnr.; Uganda mutiny medal; Nandi medal, 1900.

HOBBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD.—B. 1865; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the commrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transf. to the C.O., April, 1891; asst. regar., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff-clk., first grade, 1911.

HODGE, LEONARD PERCIVAL, F.S.L., Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Taunton, and King's Coll., Lond.; honors construc., 1886; 2nd cl. supt., P.W., Jamaica, 1891; 1st cl. ditto, and attached to head off., 1892; dir. of roads, G. Coast, 1894, but did not take up apptmt.; col. engnr. and surv-gen., Dominica, 1888; draughtsman, P.W.D., B. Guiana, 20th Mar., 1901; asst. engnr. and draughtsman, Apr., 1901.

HODGES, AUBREY DALLAS PERCIVAL, C.M.G. (1910), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.D., Lond.—Ed. at Epsom Coll. and Lond. Hosp.; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 17th Jan., 1898; in charge of sleeping sickness extended investigations, 7th Dec., 1905; sen. med. offr., 15th Oct., 1906; prin. med. offr., 3rd Sept., 1908.
HODGES, HON. HENRY EDWARD AGINCOURT.—B. 1844; puisne judge, Victoria, since 1889.

HODGSON, SIR FREDERICK MITCHELL K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1898), Knight of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1903).—B. 1851; entered sav. bank dept. of Impl. P.O. and apptd., after compet. exam., Feb., 1869; was employed in the sec.'s office in connection with the transfer of the telegraphs to the state, 1868 to 1870, and apptd. clk. in that office, Aug., 1870; apptd., after further exam., to the 3rd class (gradc I) of the sec.'s office, Aug., 1876; promoted to 2nd class, Aug., 1880; was sen. capt. of the 24th Middlesex (post office) R.V.; postm.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1882; col. sec., G. Coast, Sept., 1888; adminstr. the govt., June, 1889, to Feb., 1890; June to Nov., 1891; Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894; Apr. to Oct., 1896 and Dec., 1897, to March, 1898; major comdg. th... COAST R.V., which he raised in 1892, 1892-300; major comdg. Barbados volr. force, which he raised in 1901, 1901-1904; gov., G. Coast, Mar., 1898; gov. Barbados, Oct., 1900; gov. B. Guiana, 16th July, 1904; ret., 1911.
HODGSON, PAUL M.—B. 1880; solr., attorney and proctor of the Supreme Ct. of England, May, 1905; ditto of the Supreme Court of Hong Kong, April, 1907; asst. crown solr., Sept., 1911.

HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, F.R.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Italy, Felsted, and Mulgrave Castle; in Central Queensland, 1900 to 1902; joined Queensland contingent for S. Africa, 1902; apptd. to Transvaal and O.R.C. repat. dept., 1902; transf. to P.W.D., Transvaal, 1903; sub-inspr. Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; S.J.P., 1907; passed civ. ser. law exam., 1908; ag. A.R.M. and clk. of court, Gaberones, 1910; ag. asst. comsnr., and mag. for South Prot., Mch. to May, 1910, and Mch. to Dec., 1911.

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon div. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; ag. supt. of pol., Galle, Jan., 1909; asst. supt. of pol., Tangalla, May, 1909; Matara, Dec., 1909; off. asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910.
HOFMEYR, G. M.—Under-sec. for educ., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

HOGG, ALAN.—B. 1868; ed. at Winchester and King's Coll., Camb.; 2nd cls. hist. tripos, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripos, 1891; prizeman of the coun. of legal educ. in common law and equity, 1891; Whewell scholar of international law, 1893; called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1892; stip. mag., Entebbe, admstr.-gen., prin. regir. of documents, and regir. of companies, Uganda Prot., 6th Nov., 1908.

HOGUE, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1846; M.L.A. for The Glebe, New S. Wales since 1894; min. of pub. instr. and min. for labour, 1898-9; col. sec., 1904-7; min. of pub. instrn. and min. for labour, 1907-1910.

HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stlitms., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908; ditto, Malacca, May, 1909; exec. engrnr., Malacca, Dec., 1909.

HOLLAND, BERNARD H., C.B., (1904).—B. 1856; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1882; in office of charity comsn., 1884-1890; priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1892-1894; sec. to royal comsn. on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, 1894-1896; to Ld. Rothschild's comte. on old-age pensions, 1896-1898; to Transvaal concessions comsn., 1900-1901; to royal comsn. on Port of London, 1901-2; and to royal comsn. on war in S. Africa, 1902-3; priv. sec. to Right Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1903; ditto to Lord Elgin, 1905-1908; author of "Imperium et Libertas," etc.
HOLLAND, J.—B. 1858; apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 18th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 1904.
HOLLMAN, J. W.—Served in Impl. treaty.; entd. civ. ser., New South Wales, Mar., 1884; served as sec. to pub. ser. board; under-sec. for finance and trade, Jan., 1907.

HOLLINGSWORTH, A. H.—A.M. Inst. C.E. ed. King's Coll., London, engineering dept.; art. pupil, borough and water engr., Croydon; asst. borough and water engr., Croydon; asst.; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Dec., 1896; ag. exec. engrnr. in charge of water and drain. wks., Aug., 1900; ag. consulting sanitary survr., Sept., 1900; exec. engrnr. in charge of water and drain wks., 1st Jan., 1902; J.P., 1907.

HOLLIS, ALFRED CLAUD, C.M.G. (1911).—Ed. Highgate and St. Leonards, and in Switzerland and Germany; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 12th Mar., 1897; collr., 13th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to adminstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. comsnr., from Feb., 1901, to Apr., 1903; sec. to the adminstr., 1st Apr., 1903; sec. for native affairs, 13th June, 1907; African general serv. medal, Jubaland and Nardi (1905-6) clasps; ag. sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911.
HOLMAN, HON. W. A.—Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., New South Wales, since 1898; chmn. of elections qualifications comte., 1904; atty.-gen. and min. of justice, Oct., 1910.

HOLME, HENRY FREDERICK.—4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Leeward Islands, 1898; ag. 3rd clk., 1898 and 1900; ag. priv. sec., 1900 and 1901; ag. 2nd clk., 1900 and 1905; 2nd clk., 1901; asupt., printing office, 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., 1906, 1907, 1908 and 1909; clk., legs. coun., Antigua, 1908; priv. sec. to agt. gov', Apr. to Nov., 1909.
HOLMES, JOHN RICHARD.—S.S.C., 1879; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1889; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1889; commanded detachment in Awoonah expdn., 1889-91; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1899.

HOLROYD, SIR EDWARD DUNDAS, K.T. BACH. (1903).—B. 1828; ed. at Winchester (Queen's gold medal for Latin and English essays), Trin. Coll., Camb. (B.A. 1st cls. tripos, 1851, M.A., 1864), student, Gray's Inn, 1851; called to the bar, 1856; called to Victorian bar, 1859; Tasmanian bar, 1867; Q.C., 1879; raised to bench, Aug., 1881; ex-pres. of Impl. Fed. League of Victoria; sen. puisne judge of sup. ct. of Victoria; ret. 1st Aug., 1906.

HOLT, THOMAS HERBERT.—B. 1861; ed. at Fulneck Schl., Yorks; entd. office of Messrs. J. and B. Freeland, Crown Agents' shipping agents, 1876; senr. partner, 1909; head of Crown Agents shipping office, 1912.
HOLTZIE, MAURICE WM., F.L.S., etc.—Govt. gardener, Port Darwin, 1878; botanic gardener, 1880; dir. botanic garden, Adelaide, 1891.

HOMAGEE, JAMES FRANCIS, I.S.O. (1906).—Court messenger, St. Helena, 1859; clk. to ch. just., 1862; man. govt. savings bk., 1865; clk. of the peace, taxing master, and registr. sup. ct., 1867; legal adviser, crown prosecutor, and registr. V.A. ct., 1870; also supervisor of customs and registr. of shipping, in 1883; admity. advoc., 1890.
HOMBURG, ROBERT.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., since 1884; atty.-gen., 1890-92 and 1902-3; ditto and min. of educn., 1904-5; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1905.

HONEY, JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1908).—Served under Cape govt., 1880 to 1901; transf. to Transvaal when collr. and prin. controller of cust. and prin. regir. of shipping; served in Gaika-Galeka and Basutoland campaigns, holding coman. in latter as lieut. Capetown Rifles; dir. of cust., Transvaal, and cust. adviser to high comsnr., 16th May, 1901; M.L.C., Mem. I.C.C.; chrmn., tender and post office investment boards; ag. sec. for commerce and industries, and ag. comsnr. of customs and excise, Union of S. Africa, 1910; retired.

HONEY, DR SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE.—Served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedit. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B. C. Africa adminstr., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 9th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acting offr., 20th Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admin., 22nd Mar., 1907.
HOOD, Hon. Joseph Henry.—B. 1846; M.A.; puisne judge, Victoria, since 1890.

HOOD, Samuel James.—B. 1868; asst. warehouse keeper, Br. Hond., Oct., 1887; 4th clk., treas. and cust., Mar., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; clk., reg.-gen., June, 1893; superv. of cust., Gold Coast, Feb., 1894; inspg. superv., Nov., 1895; asst. col. of cust., S. Leone, Jan., 1901; ag. collr., Feb. to Aug., 1901; asst. compt. of cust., Gold Coast, Oct., 1901; ag. compt., Feb. to June, and Aug. to Dec., 1902, and Aug., 1903, to Feb., 1904; collr. of cust., Lagos, June, 1904; ag. col. treas., Lagos, Oct., 1905 to Apr., 1906; provl. collr. of cust., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; comptroller of cust., G. Coast, July, 1908.

HOOD, Thos.—Ed. City of Lond. schl., Univ. Coll., and St. Bart's hosp., M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Lond.; asst. col. surg., S. Leone, 1897; served on staff during native rising, 1898 (W. African medal and clasp, 1898); comsnr. of Bandajuma dist., 1899; senior med. offr., 1902; ag. prin. med. offr., Oct., 1903, to Mar., 1904; J.P. for the colony; acted as P.M.O. and M.O.H. on three occasions during 1903 to 1907 for a total period of twenty months; transf. as sen. med. offr., Gambia, Mar., 1907; chmn., bd. of health, 1907.
HOOPER, James—B. 1856; ed. parochial schools, Hatherleigh, Devonshire; journalist, and manager of newspapers in Canada; dep. prov. sec. and King's printer, Manitoba, 1900; is a J.P.

HOOPS, Albert Launcelot.—B. 1876; ed. at King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stip. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M., Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Schol. of Trop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offr., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (severely wounded, ment. in desps., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. capt., Penang vols.; ag. col. surg. rea., Penang, June, 1905; seconded to organise medical and prison depts. in Kedah, Mch., 1906; state surg. and supt. of prisons in Kedah since that date; ag. adviser to govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1906, to Apl., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of Indian immigrts., Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910.

HOPE, James William.—B. 1851; med. offr., convict estabmt., Fremantle; med. supt., invalid depot; med. offr., native penal settlement, Rottntse Is.; and health offr., Port of Fremantle, W. Australia, July, 1894; dist. med. offr. and quarantine offr., Fremantle, 1895; major, Aust. field artillery.
HOPE, John Owen Webley.—3rd treasy. asst., E. Africa Prot., 17th May, 1899; asst. collr., 1st Apr., 1900; collr., 1st Apr., 1903.

HOPE, W. M. S.—Cik. to civ. comsnr., Rouxville, O.R.C., 1900; clk., refugeecamps, 1901; asst. supt., ditto, 1902; sec. repatriation comsnr., Edenburg, 1903; inspr. of acct., govt. relief dept., 1903; examr. of acct., audit dept., 9th May, 1904.

HOPKINS, F. F.—Chief clk., secretariat, N. Nigeria, 16th Oct., 1901; confll. clk. to high comsnr., 1st Apr., 1902; priv. sec., 11th Dec., 1902; title changed to asst., high comsnr.'s office, 1903; chief asst., high comsnr.'s office, 12th Feb., 1904; ag. polit asst. and chief asst. from 1st Apr., 1907; is now titled chief asst. polit. sec.; holds S. Africa medal with 3 clasps, and W. Africa gem. as medal, with clasp "N. Nigeria, 1903."

HOPKINS, Francis Gethin.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A., M.D., etc.; asst. col. surg., Lagos, 1894; sen. asst. col. surg., 1897; sen. med. offr., W.A.M.S., 1902; dep. P.M.O., 1906; has acted as P.M.O. since 1897; M.L.C., Aug., 1903, to Feb., 1904; chmn., infant mortality comsnr., 1901.

HOPKINSON, Emilius, D.S.O., M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; last ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical asst., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., I.Y. (ment. in desps., D.S.O.); late surg. lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903.

HOPELEY, William Musgrove.—B.A. (Cambr.), 1878; b. 1863; called to bar (Inner Temple), 1878; joined Cape sup. ct. bar, 1878; ag. crown prosecutor (Griqualand W.), July, 1885; crown prosecutor (Griqualand W.), Feb., 1886; Q.C., 1890; a judge of the Cape sup. ct. assigned to the high court of Griqualand, Mar., 1892; assigned to sup. ct. temporarily, 1904-1907, and permanently, 1st July, 1907.

HOPTOFF, Wallace Frank.—B. 1872; examr. of acct., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 25th Jan., 1903; acctnt., govt. relief dept., 1st Jul., 1904; acctnt. repatriation recoveries branch, treasy., 1st July, 1905; cashier, treasy., 2nd Oct., 1906.

HOPWOOD, Rt. Hon. Sir Francis John Stephens, P.C. (1912).—G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; asst. law clk., B. of T., 1888-1888; asst. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec. rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congresses in London, 1896; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comte. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic comsn., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions comsn., 1906; mem. of comsnrs. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal comsn. on electoral reform, 1909; regar. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comsn., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912.

HORE, Ernest Henry.—Entered Impl. postal ser., July, 1871; transf. to Cyprus for special service, Oct., 1878; entered col. ser., Aug., 1882; as sub-postmr., Limassol; acted several times as ch. clk.; ch. clk., Feb., 1890; acted several times as Istd. postmr.; apptd. Istd. postmr., Apr., 1893.

HORN, Arthur Edwin.—M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.) D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; served as civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; apptd. to W. African medical staff, 1904; certif. of London schl. of trop. med. (with distinction); awarded Cragg research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.) 1908; recd. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910.

HORNBY-PORTER, CHARLES.—Apptd. dist. commr., Lagos, 1897; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1901; senior asst. col. sec., 1903; ag. col. treas., 1902-1903; ag. col. sec., 1904; holds dormant comm. to sit as pol. mag., and asst such in 1897, 1901, 1902 and 1913; on spec. serv. to Ijebu-Ode, 1901 and 1904, and to Oyo and also to Ife, 1904; pres. of pol. commr., 1901; mem. of rlyw. commn., 1901; pres. of cust. comm., 1904.
HORNE, EDWARD BUTLER.—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schl.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 21st Apr., 1904; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1909.

HORNE, HENRY HASTINGS.—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; Brit. vice-consul, Mexico, 1900-01; S. Africa, field intell. force, S. African medal; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 3rd Apr., 1903; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1907.

HORSBURGH, B.—B. 1868; M.A. Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1889; attached to the Jaffna Kachcheri; ag. office asst., Jaffna, 1891; ag. off. asst., Calle, 1893; ag. asst. gov't. agent, Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, 1895; asst. gov't. agent, Mannar, 1896; asst. gov't. agent, Hambantota, 1898; asst. gov't. agent, Kalutara, 1904; off. of cl. II., 11th Mar., 1905; ag. chmn. mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, 1906; gov't. agent, N. Cent. Prov., 1908; associated with Mr. Thurlay, of the Madras Presidency salt and alkali dept. in introducing an excise system into Ceylon, Sept., 1909; capt. in Ceylon Arty. Vols.; had charge of a camp of Boer prisoners of war at Hambantota, and served on spec. duty at several pearl fisheries since 1891.

HOWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec. 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland gov't. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of gov't. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered gov't., 1902, 1904, 1905, and 1911.
HOSE, CHARLES, D.SC.—Cadet Sarawak service, Mar., 1884; res. 2nd cl., 4th div., Jan., 1891; res. 3rd Div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.
HOUSTON, WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1846; formerly under-sec. for lands, N.S. Wales; comsrr. of lands, appeal ct. of N.S. Wales, and deputy admstr. of Norfolk Island, 1899.

HOWARD, JOSEPH ALBERT.—B. 1862; served in 2nd S. Lancs. Regt., 1882 to 1887; joined S. Settns. pol. force, 1st Dec., 1887; served with Pahang expeditionary force, 1892; inspr. of pol., 1st Dec., 1893; on special serv. in China, 1904; chief inspr. of pol., 1st Feb., 1907; ag. chief detective inspr., 1st Aug., 1907; seconded for serv. as asupt. of Tanjong Pagar Dock Board, 12th Sept., 1907; head of preventive serv., govt. monopolies dept., 16th Nov., 1909.

HOWARTH, SAMUEL EDWARD JAMES.—Ed. at Buxton Coll., London; served in S. African War, Queen's and King's medals with five clasps; asst. Colony manager, field force canteens, O.R.C., Feb., 1902, to Mar., 1903; asst. acctnt., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1905.

HOWDEN, JAMES H.—B. 1860; ed. high schl., Rockwood and St. Catherines; barrister-at-law; elect. to the Manitoba legis. for Beautiful Plains, 1903, 1907 and 1910; min. of telephones for Manitoba, 1907; prov. sec. 1908.

HOWELL, ALLEYN GRAHAM.—B. 1871; 5th clk. corrpdg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. comsrr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1903, and 1907.
HOWELL, C. G.—Sec. and actuary, Barbados savings bank, 1891.
HOWELL, E. H.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911.
HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSE.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909.

HOWELL, HECTOR MANSFIELD.—B. 1842; ed. pub. schl. and Albert Coll., Ontario; called to the bar, Ontario, 1871; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1879; chief just. of ct. of appeal, Manitoba, 1906; chief just. of Manitoba, 1909.

HOWELL, JOHN BRUCE.—B. 1867; 3rd clk., treasy., Barbados, July, 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; ag. ch. clk., record branch, C.S.O., Sept., 1893 to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., provost-marshal's office, Dec., 1897, to Apr., 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comte., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, May to October, 1900; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados, 1st Apr. to 6th July, 1902, and Jan. to Oct., 1904; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., June to Dec., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov., June to Sept., 1906, and from June to Oct., 1907.

HOY, WILLIAM WILSON.—B. 1868; entd. ser. of Cape Govt. rlys., 1889; representative of C.G.R. in Johannesburg, 1896; traffic manager, Bulawayo, 1897-8; Kimberley, 1898; Port Elizabeth, 1899; Bloemfontein (in charge of Imperial mil. rly.), 1900-1902; chief traffic manager, C.S.A.R., 1902; represented C.S.A.R. at rly. confece, at Washington; ag. gen. manager of rlys. and harbours, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

HUDSON, ARTHUR, K.C.—B. 1861; M.A., Balliol Coll., Oxford; called to the bar (Inner Temp.), 1892; of S.E. circuit and W. Ham sessions; dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone Prot., Oct., 1896; solr.-gen. of col. of S. L., Dec., 1897; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1897-1901; atty.-gen., 11th Aug., 1901-1903; ag. ch. just. in 1901, and again in 1903; apptd. puisne judge of col. of S. Leone and circuit judge of Prot., 1st Oct., 1903; atty.-gen., G. Coast, 26th Sept., 1908; K.C., Aug., 1910.
HUDSON, C.—Dist. traffic man., Auckland rlys., N. Zealand, 1884; asst. gen. man., N.Z.

HUGGINS, Henry Daniel.—B. 1866; barrister-at-law, Mid. Temp.; entd. Trinidad service, 1873; clk. of the peace, 1876-80; mag. and warden, Mayaro, 1887; ditto, Cedros, 1894; stip. mag., county of Caroni, 1906.
HUGHES, D.B.B., M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—1st prizeman, senior surgery; medallist, anatomy and pathology; ag. surg. in charge, col. hosp., Grenada, 1900; dist. med. offr., St. Vincent, Dec., 1900.

HUGHES, Edwin Tom.—B. 1883; ed. Ipswich Sch. and Keble Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; off. aast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1908; ag. aast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, May, 1910; addl. pol. mag. and mun. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1910.

HUGHES, Hon. George Edward.—B. 1854; ed. at Prince of Wales and St. Dunstan's Coll., P.E.I.; a town councillor for eight years; prea. maritime B. of T., P.E.I.; elec. to legis., P.E.I., 1900, 1904, 1908; mem. of exec. coun., without portfolio.

HUGHES, George Robert.—Formerly in lands and surveys dept., W. Australia; aast. sec., lands dept., Transvaal, 1903; under-sec., July, 1904; sec., July, 1906; sec. to lands and irrigation dept., July, 1908; acting sec., lands dept., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
HUGHES, J. O.—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1910.

HUGHES, Hon. John.—B. 1857; M.L.C., New S. Wales; appt. to legis. coun., 8th Aug., 1886; vice-pres. of exec. coun. and representative of Reid govt. in Upper House, 22nd Nov., 1898, to 13th Sept., 1899; min. of just., 3rd July to 13th Sept., 1899; mem. of parly. standing comte., on pub. wks., 1901-4; vice-pres. of exec. coun. and representative of govt. in the legis. coun., 1904-1910.

HUGHES, Hon. William Morris.—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to 1st H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elec. 1903 and 1906; min. for external affairs, 1904; representa. of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London-1907; atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910.

HULL, Hon. H. C.—B. 1860; entd. Cape civ. ser., 1879; left the civ. ser. and practised at the bar for many years; served in S. African war; treas., Transvaal; min. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910.

HULL, H. M., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; ed. at Charterhouse; priv. sec. to gov., G. Coast, and clk. of legis. and exec. couns., May, 1888, to Mar., 1891; travelling comanr., G. Coast, Mar., 1891; one of the Br. comans. for the delimitation of bndry. between the G. Coast Col. and the German Protectorate of Togo, Jan., 1892; ag. dir. of telegraphs, July to November, 1892; apptd. Br. comanr. to delimit the bndry. between the G. Coast and the Ivory Coast, 1894, the comanr., however, did not meet; ag. dir. of telegraphs, Aug., 1895, to Feb., 1896; and mentioned in despatches for services during Ashanti expedition, 1896-96; aast. col. sec., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; ag. compr. of customs, Feb. to June, 1900; on sp. service to E. Akim during Ashanti rising, 1900, raised 3,500 native levies (medal); ag. compr. of customs, Nov., 1901; sec. for native affairs, Nov., 1902; ret., May, 1907.

HULSE, Leigh Rodwell.—Ed. Belize, Br.
