Highgate School and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, April, 1897; pol. mag., Calagodara, July, 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1899; N. C. Prov., Mar., 1901; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1901; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1903; asst. coll. of cust., Trincomalie, Dec., 1904; dist. judge, Tangalla, Feb., 1905; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, April, 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1909.

SCHREIBER, COLLINGWOOD, C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1831; emigrated from England, 1852; entered service of Hamilton and Toronto Rly., and became asst. engnr., 1852; suptg. engrnr. of wks., N. Rly. of Canada, 1860-64; div. engrnr., Nova Scotia govt. service, on Truro and Pictou Rly., 1864-5; survey engrnr., Dominion govt. service, on inter-col. rly., 1886; suptg. engrnr., N. Brunswick E. Rly., 1869-71; ditto, inter-col. railway, 1871-3; ch. engrnr. of govt. railways in operation, 1873-8; also gen. man., 1878-80; also engrnr.-in-chief, Canadian Pacific Rly., 1889; engrnr.-in-chief, govt. rlys. and dep. min. of rlys. and canals, 1892; gen. consulting engrnr., 1905.

SCHREINER, THE HON. W. P., C.M.G. (1890), K.C.—B. 1857; ed. S. A. Coll., Cape Town, Camb. Univ., and London; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape, 1882; legal adviser to high comsrr., 1887-93; atty.-gen. in Mr. Rhodes's ministry, 1893; mem. of Jameson Raid Comte.; premier and col. sec., 1898-1900; M.L.A., Queenstown, 1908.

SCORTINO, J. C.—B. 1875; ed. at Lyceum Univ., Malta; in service of telegraph companies, 1891-1903; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1903; title altered to asst. res., 1904; 3rd cls. res., 29th Apr., 1907; 2nd cls. res., 15th June, 1910.
SCOFFHAM, W. H.—B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to census office, 20th Feb., 1901; transfd. to C.O., 17th Dec., 1902.

SCONCE, HERBERT W.—Ed. at Hurst Coll., Sussex; Gram. Schl.; Bristol; Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. math. tripos, 1886; M.A. 1897; called to the bar, Inner Temple, July, 1908; asst. master, Faversham Gram. Schl.; Godolphin Schl., Hammersmith, and Grocers' Co.'s Schl., Hackney, 1887 to 1893; asst. mr., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. of schl., July, 1895; ag. inspr., May to Nov., 1898; June to Nov., 1900; July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; inspr. of schl., and offl. visitor of Onderneeming Indust. Schl., Apr., 1903; mem. bd. of agric. for educat. matters; examr. for prelim. exam. for govt. clerical serv.

SCOTT, D'ARCY—B. 1872; ed. Ottawa schls. and univ., and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; barr.-at-law; practised profession in Ottawa for several years; mayor of Ottawa, 1907-08; asst. chief rly. comsrr., Canada, 1908.
SCOTT, HON. DAVID LYNCH, K.C.—B. 1845; ed. Gram. Schl., Brampton, Ont.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1870; K.C., 1885; judge of supreme ct., N.W.T., 1894, and of supreme ct. of Alberta, 1907.

SCOTT, DUNCAN CAMPBELL, F.R.S.C.—B. 1862; apptd. to Indian comsrr.'s off., Canada, 1879; jun. 2nd cl. clk., dept. of Indian affairs, 1880; promoted to sen. 2nd cl., 1883; 1st cl., 1889; ch. clkship., 1893; ch. acctnt. and super. of Indian educn., 1908.

SCOTT, JOHN.—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admng. the govt., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Mannar, Apr., 1906; pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1907; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1910.

SCOTT, J. H.—Mag., Tembuland, Aug., 1876; capt. in Tembul levies, Sept., 1877; ag. ch. mag., Tembuland, Apr. to July, 1878; ch. clk., native affairs dept., Oct., 1878; sec. to atty.-gen., or special duty to N. Border during war there, Feb. to Apr., 1879; ag. special comsrr., N. Border, May to July, 1879; special comsrr. and special mag. of N. Border, July, 1879; res. comsrr., Pondele, July, 1888.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B. 1874; B.A. Lond. Univ.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1898; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to Ca IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. dist., July, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1906; ag. comsrr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1906; to Feb., 1907; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1906; collr. of land rev., Penang, Feb., 1907; ag. comsrr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Dec., 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1908; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, Oct., 1908; in charge of stintt., Feb. to May, 1909, and from Feb. to July, 1910.

SCOTT, HON. SIR RICHARD WILLIAM KT. BACH (1909), K.C., LL.D.—Called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1884; created a Q.C., 1867; sat in the legis. assem. of Canada from 1867 to 1873, and while there carried the Separate School Act of Upper Canada; returned to the Ontario assem. at confedn., 1867; ret. on his entrance into the Dominion Govt., Nov., 1873; called to the senate, Mar., 1874; was speaker of the Ontario assem. from 7th to 21st Dec., 1871; mem. of exec. coun. and comsrr. of crown lands for same prov., from later date until Nov., 1873, when sworn of the Queen's privy coun.; S. of S. of Canada, Jan., 1874; ret. on resignation of Mackenzie gov't., Oct., 1878; is now a senator; was the framer of the Canada Temperance Act, known as the Scott act; joined Sir W. Laurier's gov't as S. of S., June, 1896; ret. from cabinet, 1908.
SCOTT, ROBERT.—B. 1873; acctnt. to director, army telegraphs, O.R.C., July, 1900; acctnt., dept. of posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1902.

SCOTT, HON. WALTER.—B. 1867; ed. pub. schls., Middlesex Co., Ontario; journalist; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1900 and 1904; resig. 1905, and sworn first pres. of coun. for Sask. and min. pub. wks.; elec. to Sask. legis. assem. for Lumsden dist., 13th Dec., 1905; and for Swift Current dist., 14th Aug., 1909.

SCOTT, WALTER.—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., 2nd Jan., 1883; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., 3rd Nov., 1896; staff clk., first grade, 1911.
COTT, WALTER DARE.—B. 1870; ch. clk., 1 Selangor, F.M.S., 1891; ast. dist. offr., ala Lumpur, 1893; ast. dist. offr., Krian, 3; dist. offr., Raub, 1906; British agt., mggrau, 1910.

COTT, WM. ALEXANDER.—B. 1871; olk. to y.-gen., Fiji, 15th Oct., 1888; ag. stip. mag., vua and Nadroga, tax inspr., Oct., 1897; stip. g. and ast. comsrr., Colo West, 1898; chief nat., native dept., and stip. mag., Kadavu, 1899; ov. inspr., Kadavu and Lomalivi, 1901; ag. p. mag., Rewa, and comsrr., Naitasiri, 1902; stip. mag., Navua, and comsrr., Namoi, 1903; ast. nat., native comsrr., 1904; despatched to christchurch exhibition in charge of body of iijian natives, 1906-7; ag. chief pol. mag. and fl. recvr. in bankruptcy, 1907; dep. nat., comsrr., 1908.
SCOTT, WILLIAM DUNCAN.—B. 1861; comsrr. or Canada to the Paris Exhibn., 1900; supt. of migr., 1903.
SCOTT, WM. ERNEST.—Ed. at Bradford and Jiggleswick gram. schls., England; dep. min. agric. and supt. of farmers' institutions, B. Columbia, 1909.
SCROGGS, LIEUT. HAROLD CHARLES, R.N.—Harbour mast. and supt. of merc. marine, Mauritius, 23rd June, 1903.
SEALY, A.—Pol. mag. of dist. B, Barbados, sept., 1873.
SEALY, FRANK DEAN.—Supernum. clk., immigr. dept. Br. Guiana, Jan., 1884; 4th class clk., Apr., 1891; 3rd class, Jan., 1894; 2nd class, Apr., 1896; immigr. agt., Oct., 1898.

SEALY, GORDON COURTLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confidl. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., sury-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treasy. dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to comsrrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasy., June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; clk. to sub-recvr., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treasy., Tobago, Feb., 1904.

SEARCY, ARTHUR.—Boarding offr., cust. S. Aust., 1873; clk. cust., 1874; clk. audit off., 1875; clk., cust., 1876; tide inspr. and measurer of ships, cust., 1889; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comsrr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; clk. ass't and sergeant-at-arms, H. of A., 1894; dep. comsrr. of taxes and stamps, Jan., 1896, to Mch., 1897; comsrr., ditto, Mch., to Oct., 1897; dep. comsrr., ditto, Oct., 1897, to Feb., 1911; also pres., marine bd., 1902; also inspr. of kerosine, 1902; comsrr. of trade marks and regstr. n copyrights, 1904-7; also controller, outer harbour, Port Adelaide, Nov., 1907; also controller, ocean steamers wharf, Apl., 1909; also chief nspr. under Inflammable Oils Act, June, 1909; also supt. of life-saving service, Aug., 1909; also controller of harbours, July, 1911.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SEELY, HON. COL. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (deeps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; reed. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life at sea, 1891; partly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comtee. to inquire into organisation of crown agents office, 1908; under-secretary of state for war, 1911.

SEIGNORET, CLARENCE AUGUSTUS.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yaws comsrr., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., registr.'s office and clk. pub. market, 1892; ast. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treasy., 1895; sec., bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1905; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harbmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907; ag. treasr., Sept., 1910, to Sept., 1911.

SELBORNE, 2ND EARL OF (crest, 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1906), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1869; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Colm., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comtce., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtce., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high comsrr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comd-r-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907.

SELLS, H. C., B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S. Sttlmta., Oct., 1893; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1893, to Nov., 1894; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907; ag. postmr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1909.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regist., Leeward Is., and regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., regist. of titles, regist. of friendly societies, and regist.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regist. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and regist. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.F., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911.
SENEVIRATNE, ALEXANDER DE ALWIS.—Mem. of legis. coun., Ceylon; advoc. of the sup. ct.; mem. mun. coun., Colombo, and coun. of legal educn.; chmn. Ceylon national assoc.

SENIOR, BERNARD, I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1865; clk. to the bdry. comsrr. (Redistribution of Seats Act), 1884; attached to Scotch educn. dept., 1885, and again 1888; priv. sec. to Sir F. R. Sandford, under-sec. for Scotland, 1887; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1888; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ditto, G. Coast, Dec., 1889; ditto, Br. Bechuanaland, Feb., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1895; aud.-gen., O.R.C., Sept., 1902; is also chmn. of tender bd.; mem. of comsn. to inquire into law relating to mining for diamonds and precious stones in O.R.C., 1903; ag. col. treas., 12th Aug., 1903, to 15th Feb., 1904; M.L.C., May, 1904; deleg. for O.R.C. at S. African freight confce., 1904-5; mem. of O.R.C. civ. ser. comsn., 1905; chmn. of govt. tender bd.; dir. of National Bank of O.R.C.; col. auditor, Ceylon, 1st Mar., 1907; ag. treas. from 16th Nov., 1908, to 5th Feb., 1909; treas. and comanr. of stamps, Ceylon, 6th Feb., 1909; is also ch. comanr. of loan bd., and mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; author of "A Guide to the examination of Government Accounts in Cyprus."
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief acct., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SETH, G. G.—B.A., Calcutta; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1901; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. registrar, sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. registrar, sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906; astt. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907.

SEVERN, CLAUD.—B. 1869; B.A. Cantab.; temporary clk. in librarian's dept., foreign office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; astt. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; astt. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; astt. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high comanr., F.M.S., and priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., May, 1907; passed in Malay and law, 1898.

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schl. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. astt. to agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apr., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; astt. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; 2nd lieut., Ceylon Planters Rifle Corps.

SHARE, HAMNET HOLDITCH.—B. 1864; entd. R. Navy, 1880; served in H.M.S. "Tourmaline" and numerous other ships (Egypt, medal, 1882, and Khedive's Bronze Star); sec. to comdr.-in-chief, Pacific station, 1899-1900; ditto, Australia station, 1901-1903; priv. sec. to gov. of N.S. Wales, May, 1903; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 22nd Nov., 1904.

SHARPE, Sir ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyassaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. comanr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy comanr., 1st Apr., 1896; and again acted as comanr. and consul-gen., 16th Apr., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; comanr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infil. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; comanr. commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyassaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prot. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

SHARPE, W. S., C.M.G. (1901).—Capt. 4th batt. R.I. Rifles, 1893; major, 1903; inspr., Sierra Leone frontier force, 1894; 3rd comanr. to Anglo-French comanr. and delimited frontier from Atlantic to Little Molle River, 1896; dist. comanr., dist. Jan., 1897; served in the S. Leone 1898-9 (despatches and medal); F.R.O.; J.P. for the col.; transfd. to N. Nigeria res., Dec., 1900; apptd. 2nd class res. to Accra Prov., Oct., 1901; in Dakkakari exp., Nig., 1904 (medal); in Darengo exp., 1906 (dangerously wounded); in Dar expedition, N. Nig., 1908.

SHAW, BERNARD VIVIAL.—Capt., 1st Batt. Rifle Reg. (Reserve); instr. of mus. cls., certif., Hythe); served unofficially number of years under Sir Eyre Shaw; Metropolitan Fire Brigade; priv. sec. to sec. of crim. investig., Scotland Yard; sec. pol., Cape Town, 1882 to 1888; received of govt. for services rendered during M. of 1886; visiting mac., Breakwater station; transfd. to Brit. Guiana, H.M. penal stltlnt., Massaruni, Brit. Gov.; author of "Epitome of the Cape Crimes."

SHAW, CHAS. COURTENAY.—B. 1875; off. sec. to Transvaal adminstr., priv. ag. priv. sec. to ditto, Mar. to June, 1907; sec. to col. sec., Transvaal, July, 1907; Transvaal imigr. off., May, 1904; to J.P., Witwatersrand, May, 1906; priv. lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1st July, 1906; priv. gov., O.R.C., July, 1907.

SHAWE, HENRY BENJAMIN.—Ch. William, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sec. accts., 14th Apr., 1890; chief ditto, 1894; accts., 10th July, 1895; sec., 31st Mar., 1899; ch. clk. and accts., Mar., 1901; astt. under col. sec., 1st July, 1902; mem. of Rhodesian pub. serv. bd., Apr. to Sept., 1909; ag. under sec. for Union of S. Africa, May, 1910.
SHEA, THE HON. SIR E. D., K.T.—Col. sec. and clk. of coun., Newfoundland, 1874; cashier of savings bank, and pres. coun., Feb., 1886.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H. land (Newfield,), 1889-93; again in exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; treasy. bd., 1898; mayor of St. John's mem. exec. coun., 1904.

SHEPHERD, FREDERICK RENT.—Supt. of Skerratt's training schl., Ant. ward Is., 1891-1905; ag. sup. experiments, Antigua, 1899-1904; various agric. confces. held by Impl. supt. of agric., Leeward Is., July to Nov., 1899.

SHEPPARD, WALTER STONE, B. Ch. (Cantab.).—B. 1871; civil sup. of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; sup. S. Sttlmts., 1st Mar., 1901; spec. Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov., Aug., 1905.

SHEPSTONE, ARTHUR JESSE, J.P. (Natal).—Ast. comanr. and Lower Umfolosi dist., Natal, 21st mag., Nkandla dist., 28th Feb., 1901; janeni dist., 1st Dec., 1894; Vryheid, 1903; Newcastle, 21st Nov., 1904; Oct., 1907; sec. for native affairs, and sec., Natal native trust, 1st July, 1909.

SHEPSTONE, H. C., C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1840; priv. sec. to the lieut.-gov., Natal, 1857; elk. to rec. coun., 1863; Indian immigr. agt. and J.P., 1864; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Alfred co., 1870; ditto, Durban co., 1878; acted as sec. to Mr. Justice Phillips, C.M.G., on special service to Barbados, 1876; served on the staff of Sir Theophilus Shepstone, K.C.M.G., H.M.'s special comsrr. in S. Africa, 1877; sec. for native affairs, Transvaal, July, 1877; attended Cetwayo on his visit to England, 1882; sec. for native affairs, Natal, 1884; ret., 1893.

SHEPSTONE, JOHN WESLEY, C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1827; July, 1846, govt. interp., Natal; July, 1847, accompanied Capt. Kyle on special mission to Zulu king; Dec., 1850, accompanied Capt. Gordon on special mission to Pondos; Oct., 1851, interp. to recorder and mag.'s offices; Dec., 1851, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; Aug., 1852, R.M., Lower Umvoti, and govt. agt., Noniti country, and J.P.; 1857, commanded native forces against Chief Usidoi and tribe, and against Chief Matyana and tribe; Nov., 1858, supt. of the cultivation of cotton; Apr., 1861, ag. sec. for native affairs and judicial assessor; July, 1861, commanded a native force in the Fort Buckingham exped.; May, 1862, ag. R.M., Inanda div.; Aug., 1863, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; ag. R.M., Umvoti co., Sept., 1867; May, 1874, comsrr., Klip River and Weenen counties; July, 1874, ag. sec. for native affairs and judicial assessor; Oct., 1874, J.P. for the col.; Sept., 1876, sec. for native affairs and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; Mar., 1878, mem. of the bdny. comen.; Dec., 1878, delivered ultimatum to Zulu deleg. at Lower Drift, Tugela; Aug., 1879, accompanied Sir Garnet Wolseley to arrange the settlement of Zululand; mentioned in despatches; Dec., 1882, Br. comsrr., Zululand; Jan., 1884, judge, native high ct.; ret., 1896.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1867; judal. elk., Nyasaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911.

SHERIFF, PERCY MUSGRAVE CRESSWELL.—B. 1869; called to the bar, Mid. Temple, June, 1892; admitted to the bar of B. Hond., 1893; of Jamaica, 1895; ag. dist. comsrr., Belize, for six months in 1898; apptd. mag. and coroner, So. dist., Grenada, 1900; visiting just. of prisons, 1900; chairman of St. George's Road Board, 1901; ag. mem. legis. coun., June, 1902; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, Dec., 1902, to Dec., 1903, when confirmed; admtd. govt., and ag. col. sec. and treas., Aug., 1903; mem. bd. of edcuon.; prov. mem. of exec. coun., and legal adviser to admstr., and draughtsman of govt. bills, Dec., 1902, to Apr., 1906; M.L.C., 1904; ch. just., St. Lucia, 1906; compiled "Comparative Index of Laws of Grenada and St. Vincent," Dec., 1905; and "Index of Laws of St. Lucia," Dec., 1907.
SHERWOOD, ARTHUR PERCY, C.M.G., M.V.O., A.D.C.—B. 1864; supt. of Dominion pol., Canada, 1882; comsrr., Dominion pol., 1885.
SHERWOOD, M. E.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, Jan. to Nov., 1909.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. elk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1880; minor staff elk., 1911.

SHOLL, LIONEL HENRY, C.M.G. (1911), I.S.O. (1903)—B. 1844; elk., P.W. office, S. Australia, July, 1858; elk., waterwks. dept., Jan., 1859; elk., audit office, Sept., 1863; ch. elk., Jan., 1872; ch. elk. and acctnt., treasy., July, 1874; acctnt. and receiver of rev., treasy., July, 1876; cashier and acctnt., treasy., July, 1879; under-treas., July, 1883; under-sec. and govt. statist, May, 1890; also elk. of exec. coun., July, 1894; also sec. to min. of industry, July, 1908, to June, 1911.

SHORT, EDWARD MORRISON DE COUCY.—B. 1857; Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw, 1884; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1886; ditto, Mannar, Jan., 1889; ditto, Hambantota, Sept., 1889; ditto, Kegalla, 1890; ditto, Varuniya and Mullaitivu, 1890; ditto, Kalutara, 1893; ditto, Matara, 1894; ditto, Chilaw, 1895; ditto, Varuniya, 1896; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Oct., 1896; ditto, Matara, 1897; ditto, Matale, 1900; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 1900; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, 1903; ditto, N.W. Prov., 1904; chmn. of mun. coun. and mayor of Colombo, 1905; lieut., Ceylon volunteer reserve.

SHORITT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1859; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1885) and Glasgow and Edin., Univ.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; apptd. to civ. serv. comen., Canada, 1908; writer on history, banking and economics.
SHOWERS, EDWARD MELIAN.—Served in Bengal police, Feb., 1867, to Sept., 1901; comsrr. of pol., Transvaal, 8th Nov., 1901.

SIDNEY, T. S. M.A.—Ed. Harrow, and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Mid. Term., June, 1885; enrolled adv. to high ct. of Madras, 1894; apptd. dist. comsrr., Lagos, Jan., 1901; ag. trav. comsrr., Ilesha, res., Ibadan, and P.M., Lagos, 1901; dist. comsrr., Epe and Badagry, 1901-02; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1909; comsrr., for revision of laws of Colony, 1910; chmn. of trustees of Antigua pub. library, July, 1910.

SIERRA LEONE, BISHOP OF, Rt. REV. EDMUND HENRY ELWIN, M.A., Merton Coll., Oxon.; cur. of St. Peter-le-Bailey, Oxford, 1894-6; chaplain at Fourah Bay Coll., S. Leone, 1896-8; principal, 1898-1901; chapln. to Bp. of S. Leone, 1898-1901; Bishop of Sierra Leone, 1901.

SIFTON, HON. ARTHUR LEWIS, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1859; ed. Wesley Coll., Winnipeg and Victoria Univ., Cobourg; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1883; elec. to North-West Assem., 1901; comsrr. pub.wks., 1901; K.C., 1903; ch. just. of N.W.T., 1903; ch. just. of Alberta, 1905; resig. May, 1910, to become premier of Alberta.
SILBERRAD, HUBERT.—Ed. at Wren's and Finishing Tech. Coll. (engineering); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 8th May, 1903; transf. to Nyasaland Prot. as 2nd grade res., July, 1909.

SIMMONS, WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct. Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMPSON, ARCHIBALD HENRY, M.A.—B. 1843; chf. judge in equity, N.S. Wales, 1896.
SIMPSON, EVERARD DOWNES.—B.1876; M.A., Oxon.; asst. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 26th June, 1901; dist. comsrr., 1st Apr., 1905; ag. pol. mag., E. Prov., Feb. to Mar., and July to Sept., 1907.
SIMPSON, GEORGE.—Inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1880; also inspr. of dockyard schls.
SIMPSON, Hon. Sir George Bowen, Kt., B.A. (1838) — B. 1838; judge of sup. ct., N.S.Wales, since 1894; judge of divorce and matrimonial jurisdiction since 1896.
SIMSON, Colin Coape — Chief med. offr., Papua, 19th Oct., 1908.
SIMSON, J. A. — Ed. in England, Germany and Switzerland; act-gen. in India for the British Protectorate in Africa.

SINCKLER, Edward Goulburn — B. 1856; entered col. sec's office, Barbados, Nov., 1874; acted as clk. to lieut.-gov., 1878; 2nd clk. corresp. branch, col. sec's office, 1879; ag. ast. clk. to Gov. W. Robinson, 1880; 1st clk. record branch, col. sec's office, and clk., courts of ordinary and error, Jan., 1883; ag. ch. clk., 1886; commr. of census, 1891; ch. clk. to judges, ast. ct. of appeal, 1892; J.P., Oct., 1892; ag. reg. friendly socs., June, 1893; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, and dist. A, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1894; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. E, July, 1896; to Jan., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, Mar. to Apr., 1898; ag. coroner, St. Michael, 1898 (at time of hurricane), and has repeatedly acted as such; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, Feb., 1900; ag. commr. of probate, May to Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, July, 1900, to Sept., 1901; pol. mag. and judge, dist. E, Oct., 1901; discharged duties of pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, in addition to own duties, Sept., 1905; and from Aug. to Oct., 1907; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, 18th July to 23rd Nov., 1900; coroner, dist. E, 27th May, 1907; ag. commr. of probates, May to Aug., 1903, and May to Oct., 1908; ag. judge, petty debt, ct., Bridgetown, July to Oct., 1909; ag. pol. mag. and judge, Dist. "D," in addition to own duties, Aug. and Oct., 1910, and May to Aug., 1911; commr. of census, 1911; mem. and hon. sec. of historic sites comte, 1910; suggested Nelson centenary postage stamp, 1904; took active part in promotion of celebration of tercentenary of Barbados, 1905; compiler of first "Barbados Government Handbook," 1911.

SINCLAIR, Wm. — B. 1844; clk., stores dept., L. and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1875; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rly., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; mem. of tender bd., July, 1901; ch. rly. storekeeper, C.S.A.R., Dec., 1903; sup't. of stores and shipping branch, Transvaal agt.-gen.'s office, London, July, 1907.
SINGAPORE, Lord Bishop of, Right Rev.

CHARLES JAMES FERGUSON-DAVIE, B.A., M.A., D.D. — Formerly curate of St. Paul, Preston, 1896-99; dom. chap. to Bishop of Lahore, 1899-1902; S.P.G. mission at Rewari, 1902-1907; S.P.G. mission at Rawal Pindi, 1907-09; consecrated Lord Bishop of Singapore, 24th Aug., 1909.

SISNETT, Herbert Kortright McDonnell — Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov'r., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, as res. mag., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, as res. mag., Manchester, Apr. to June, 1904; St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1904; half months in 1905, and from Jan. to June, 1906; regar.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen., Br. Hond., Apr. to Dec., 1907, Mar. to Sept., 1908, Mar. to Aug., 1909; J.P. for Br. Hond., commr. of sup. ct., Br. Hond.

SIVERTHIGHT, The Hon. Sir James (1892), C.M.G. (1880). — B. 1848; ag. telegraphy, C. of Good Hope, Apr., 1885; M.L.A., 1889; mem. of Rhodesia without portfolio, July, 1900, and sub-commr. of crown lands and pub. works, 1893; resumed office, Jan., 1896.
SKENE, Ralph Rangabe Felix — Ed. at St. Paul's schl., London, and at Geneva; ast. collr., E. Africa Provinces, 1897; collr., 1st Apr., 1903.

SKINNER, Wm. — Ed. Bedford Coll.; open scholar, St. Catherine's Coll., Oxf. (math. tripos), June, 1882, and burs. sen. optime; M.A., Oct., 1886; hon. schl., Dominica, 1st Jan., 1883; J.P. just., Roseau prison, Dec., 1885; exec. Roseau town bd., 1899; sub-insp. Dominica, 12th June, 1901; insp. of prison measures, Dominica, 1st Feb., 1902; schls., Leeward Islands, June to Nov., treas., Dominica, Dec., 1903; chmn. town bd., May to Dec., 1904; ag. insp. Leeward Islands, Sept., 1905; offr. prison discipline, Nov., 1905.

SKIRVING, John Mackenzie — Ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; serjeant-in-arms, 1st Higths.; 1st cls. certif. of I., 1894; certif. of mil. eng., 1895; prisoners of war staff, S. Africa and India, Nov., 1899, to Oct., 1902 (Queen's clasp, ment. in desps.); insp. of pol. mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1901; and drill inst., St. Helena volrs., 1901; insprr., firemaster and insp. of works measures, 1906; also schl. attendance in charge of Zulu prison, and ast. in govr.'s off., 1907-1909; sub-insp. Islands pol., and mil. instr., Antigua force and defence reserve, June, 1909; J.P. for Leeward Is.; ag. insp. of police and defence reserve, offr. in charge of police and mem. of Roseau Town Board, Dominica, 1909; sub-insp. of pol., etc., Antigua, 1910; sub-insp. of pol., insp. of works measures, insp. of works and instr., defence reserve, and mem. of Health, Montserrat, July, 1910; Montserrat; visiting just. of prison, 1911.

SLADEN, Arthur F., C.M.G. — B. 1866; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Royal Coll., Greenwich; went to Canada, to gov.-gen.'s office, 1891; now prov. H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught, acted in similar capacity to three prov. gen.

SLADER, C. H. Yorke — Jun. clk., Jamaica, 1883; sen. ast. clk. ct., 1889; dep. clk. cts., Westmoreland, 1889; dep. clk., sup. ct., 1894; ag. regar. Jamaica militia, 1896; offr. with contingent (medal), 1897; ag. regar., 1899; passed exam. before judge equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of ct., 1900; ag. commr. and judge of Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the soc'y of Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. regar. 1908; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Island, 1909; ag. comsnr., Mar. to Oct., 1909; J.P. for Kingston, Jamaica; stip. mag., Kingston, Oct., 1909; res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910, and in addtn. ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, July, 1910; compiled 3rd edit. "Index to Laws of Jamaica," 1911.

SLATER, ALEXANDER RANSFORD.—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (B.A. 1897); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra off. astt. gov't agent, W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd astt., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war, Dyiatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1906; additional astt. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apr., 1907; 2nd astt. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. astt. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910.
SLINGSBY, W. ECRODY.—B. 1885; ed. at Aysgarth schl., Yorks, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 24th Nov., 1909.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1906).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moiroi (medal with clasp, 1877-89); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-instr. Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; astt. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901.
SLY, HON. R. M., M.A., LL.D.—Judge of sup. ct. New South Wales, 11th Feb., 1908.

SMALL, ROBERT.—Ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., St. Thomas Hosp., King's Coll. Hosp., and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; certif. of Lond. Schol. of Trop. Med.; L.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901; D.P.H. (Lond.), 1906; D.T.C. (Camb.), 1906; civ. surg., S. African field force, 1901-1902; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 16th Feb., 1906.

SMALLWOOD, HENRY ARMSTRONG.—B. 1869; entered navy, 1885; on board H.M.S. "Victoria," at time of disaster, June, 1893; astt. comsnr., Colo West, and stip. mag., Apr., 1894; ag. stip. mag., Ba and Yasava, Apr., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, and priv. sec. to gov. Fiji, Apr., 1896; ag. sec. to W. Pac. high coman., May, 1897, to May, 1898; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Tavuni, Jan., 1899; island treas., Cyprus, Mar., 1900; passed lower standard Greek, Dec., 1900; ag. comsnr., Nicosia, June-Sept., 1904; treas., St. Lucia, Nov., 1907; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; nominated deleg. to Canadian Reciprocity Conference at Barbados, Jan., 1908; ag. col. sec., July 1908, to Jan., 1909; ag. adminstr. and col. sec., Apr., 1909; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1909; treas., E.A.P., Oct., 1911.

SMARTT, MAJOR J. P. (3rd batt. South Lancs. Regt.)—Public wks. and roads dept., G. Coast, 1893-96; local auditor, N. C. Prot. (how S. Nigeria), Oct., 1896; chief astt. treas., Dec., 1901; ag. treas., Dec., 1901, to June, 1902, June to Dec., 1903, Sept., 1904, to Jan., 1905, Feb. to Aug., 1905; apptd. provcl. treas. on amalgtn. with Lagos, 1st May, 1906; ag. financial comsnr., mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1906, to Mar., 1907, and Jan. to Nov., 1908; ag. adjt., G. Coast volrs., May to Nov., 1903; ag. comdt., Oct., 1905, to Mar., 1906; attached for duty with N. C. Prot. force (now S. N. Regt.), May to July, 1897, May and Aug., 1900; served with expedit. force, Benin territories, Apr. and May, 1899 (ment. in despa., medal and clasp); major comdg. So. Nigeria volrs., July, 1907.

SMARTT, HON SIR THOS. WM., K.C.M.G., (1911).—L.R.C.S. Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A. Cape Colony, since 1894; col. sec., 1898; comsnr. of pub. wks., 1900-02, and 1904-1908; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904, 1906 and 1906; attended Imple. confce., 1907; mem. of S. African Nat. Conventn. in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transkei," having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900.

SMEETON, S.P., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1842; extra clk. in office of S. of S. forCols., June, 1862; 1st class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, June, 1870; regis.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, 8th Feb., 1878; suptd. the taking of census of Jamaica, 1881 and 1891; dep. keeper of records (on amalgamation of registration and record depts.), May, 1899; nom. mem. of coman. to inquire into marriage and regisn. laws, May 1903.
SMITH, ALEXANDER.—Treasy. astt., E. Africa Prov., 19th Sept., 1904.

SMITH, ALLAN F., I.S.O. (1906).—Clk. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; astt. col. sec., 1895; col. postmtr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900, to July, 1901; recvr.-gen., 1908.

SMITH, SIR C. ABERCROMBIE, KT. BACH. (1903).—M.A. (Camb. and Glas.), fellow of St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 2nd wrangler and 2nd Smith's prize-man, 1858, &c.; mem. of the house of assem., Cape, 1866-75; mem. of the exec. coun., 1872; comsnr. of crown lands and pub. works., 1872-75; mem. of comte. under Ordinance 97, 1872-3; comsnr. of the sinking fund, 1872-4; mem. of the Univ. coun., 1873; chmn. of the meteorol. coman., 1874; comptroller and audr.-gen., 1875; chmn. of the tender bd., 1875; mem. of the surety bd., 1875-86; vice-chancellor, Cape Univ., 1877-9; comanr., civ. ser. exams. bd., 1886; chairman, 1887; ret., 1904.

SMITH, C. A. JEFFREY.—Apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Jamaica, Aug., 1870; astt. to ch. supt., 1875; 3rd class ditto, 1879; 2nd class ditto, 1881; ag. dist. engnr., W. dist., 1886; ditto, Kingston dist., 1888-9; overseer of wks., prisons dept., 1889; 1st cls. supt., pub. wks., and supt., St. Catherine waterwks., 1896; also acted on several occasions as govt. assessor, and supt. irrigation wks.

SMITH, SIR CECIL CLEMENTI, P.C. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1892), K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1840; ed. at St. Paul's Schl. and Corp. Chr. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1862, M.A. 1868; elected student interp., Hong Kong, Mar., 1862; reger.-gen., Mar., 1865; ag. pol. mag., June, 1865; ag. col. sec., 1867; mem. of exec. coun., 1869; ag. col. sec., Feb., 1872, to Dec., 1873; treas., 1871; acted as col. sec., Oct., 1874, and also from Mar., 1875; col. sec., S. Stiltna, July, 1878; apptd. under the F.O. to be Br. comanr. to settle certain marine claims at Manila, Sept., 1878, and received the thanks of H.M.'s govt.; also for services in connection with the "Niseru" case, 1884; administered govt. S.S., Mar., 1884, to Nov., 1885; lieut.-gov. and col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1885; gov. of S.S., 1887; also consul-gen. and high commr. for Borneo and Sarawak, 1889; ret. 1893; pres. of commn. to inquire into Trinidad riots, 1903.

SMITH, Sir Charles Holled, K.C.M.G., C.B.—B. 1846; ensign, 1863; lieut., 1869; capt., 1877; S. African war, 1879-1881; Zulu campaign; Transvaal cam., battles of Laing's Nek, Ingogo, and Majuba Mountain; despatches, Lon. Gaz., May 3rd, 1881; Egypt expdn., 1882; at Ramleh, Tel-el-Mahuta; action at Kaassassin; battle of Tel-el-Kebir, Lon. Gaz., Nov. 2nd, 1882; major (brevet), Nov., 1882; with Egypt army, Jan., 1883, to Aug., 1892; Soudan expdn., 1884-5; lieut.-col., June, 1885; col., Dec., 1883; gov.-gen., Red Sea littoral, and comdt., Suakin, Sept., 1883, to Aug., 1892; Soudan, 1888-91; action of Gamaizah, Lon. Gaz., Jan. 11th, 1889; occupation of Handoul, Jan., 1891; major-gen. comdg. Victorian mil. forces, Dec., 1894; several medals, clasps to bronze stars, and mentioned in numerous despatches.

SMITH, Edwin Mitchell.—Entered survey, crown lands dept., S. Australia, as asst., June, 1862; cadet and jun. surv.; surv., Jan., 1869; draftsman, Jan., 1874; steward and surv. of educnl. lands, Feb., 1882; ch. cl., land office, Jan., 1886; dep. surv.-gen., July, 1894; surv.-gen., 1911; mem. of central pastoral and central lands bds.

SMITH, Francis Jago.—M.A. (Oxon); ed. Merchant Taylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. class. moda., 1894; 2nd math. mols., 1894; 2nd Lit. Hum., 1896; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1896; attached to col. sec.'s off., 1896; office ast. to govt. agt., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., Apr., 1898; comnr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1901; landing surv., customs, Colombo, Dec., 1901; 2nd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1905; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Feb., 1908.

SMITH, Frank Braybrooke.—B. 1864; agric. adviser to govt. of Transvaal, Apr., 1902; dir. of agric., Aug., 1902; M.L.C., 1902-7; and mem. land settlmt. bd.; ag. sec. for agric., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SMITH, Frederick Bonham, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1837; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, 1859; in office of govt.-in-chief of the Windward Is., 1860 to 1866; pol. mag. of Christchurch, Barbados, 1866; sen. pol. mag. of Bridgetown and St. Michael, 1873; ag. judge of the ast. ct. of appeal, 1878 to 1880; inspr. of prisons, 1878, held with office of provost-marshal from 1882: ag. col. sec., 1879.

SMITH, F. M. Uring.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910.

SMITH, Frederick Stanley.—B. 1870; ed. at Sherborne Coll.; called to the bar, In Temp., Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. "B," Barbados, 1893; ditto, dist. "D," Apr., 1894, to July, 1895; and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; J.P., 1897; ag. judge, ast., ct. of appeal, Apr. to Sept., 1897; pol. mag., dist. "D," Jan., 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. "A," Nov., 1901, to Dec., 1902; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "A," and Bridgetown, Dec., 1902; ag. judge, ast. ct. of appeal and mast. in chancery in 1906, and again in 1907.

SMITH, George, C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, 10th Apr., 1878; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; asst. to ch. sec., 15th Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, 10th Aug., 1886, to 14th Feb., 1887; clk., legio. coun., 1st Mar., 1886, to 24th Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 15th May, to 10th Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; comnr., Papho, 25th Nov., 1891; regisr.-gen. and offl. mem. of legis. coun., 7th June, 1895; has served also as mem. of comtee. of management, Cyprus museum, 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comtee., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comtee., 1887; mem. of land registn. coman., 1891; pres. mun. coman., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. coman., 1896; mem. of mun. coman., Nicosia, 1895; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. British delegate of Evcaf., under convn. with Turkey, 20th Mar. to 25th Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; British delegate of Evcaf., 20th July, 1905; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1908; col. sec., Mauritius, 16th June, 1910; administered govt., Apl. to Nov., 1911.

SMITH, George. Ed. at Galway House Schl., Leyton, Essex, and at Morden House, Greenwch; ag. dist. comnr., Anamaboe, G. Coast, 1878; dist. comnr., Dixcove, 1878; ditto, Palmwood, Leckie, Lagos, 1880; sub-collr. customs, har. and Leckie, 1883; ch. clk. and warehouse keep. customs, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ag. dist. comnr., E. dist., Lagos, Mar., 1892.

SMITH, George Douglas, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1865; joined Imperial Brit. East Africa Co., 1890; treas., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1894; as chief of cust., from Oct., 1906, and mem. as controller of treasy. savings bank, from 1st Oct., 1907; East and Cent. African medal with clasps, Uganda, 1897-8.

SMITH, Capt. George Echlin.—B. 1871; R. Athmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joind 5th batt. Royal Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1896; hon. capt. in army, 1900; asst. inspr., Harps force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub-inspr., B. Guiana police, Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, K.C.M.G., 1902-1904; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, K.C.M.G., 1904-1906; asst. dist. aspt. of police, E. Afr. Prot., Oct., 1906.

SMITH, Major George Edward, C.M.G. (1909).—Educ. at Winchester and Woolwich; comnr. in R.E., 1888; asst. on Anglo-Germ. boundary, coman., E. Africa, 1891-2; on Mombasa Victoria Nyanza road expdn., 1895-9; come and comdr., B. section, Anglo-German bounds, 1904-6; dir. of surveys, E.A.P., 26th June, 1904; reverted to R.E., 1910.

SMITH, George Whitfield.—F.L.S., Ch. pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1879; 3rd treas., clk., St. Lucia, 1882; sub-collr. of taxes, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Aug., 1882; rev. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Feb., 1885 (resigned); curate, botanic gardens, Grenada, Dec., 1890 (resigned), 16th Dec., 1893; trav. supt., Imperial dept. agric., Dec., 1898; pol. mag. and ast. treas., N. dist. Grenada, and pol. mag., 3rd dist., St. Vincent, Apr., 1904; comnr., Carriacou, Oct., 1904; was engaged as ast. botanist to W. L. exploration comtee. of Royal Soc. during scientific exploration of St. Vincent, 1888-89; is a corresp. mem. of Royal Hort. Soc. of England.

SMITH, Gerald Stanley Wells.—B. 1878; graduate, Ontario business coll., Canada; supr. clk., regisr.'s office, Grenada, Jan., 1890; ast. post office, Mar., 1890; 4th clk., treasy., June, 1890; asst. clk., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1890; 2nd clk., treasy., Nov., 1891; audit clk., Nov., 1897; thanked by gov. for report on condition of paupers, 1905; has held numerous acting appointments, including ag. aud. of Windward Islands, 4th June to 31st Dec., 1906; 1st Jan. to 13th Mar., 1907; and from 18th Mar., 1908, to 24th Nov., 1909.

SMITH, Lieut.-Col. Sir Gerard, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B.1839; late lieut.-col. Roy. Scots Guards; groom-in-waiting to Her Majesty, 1883-5; M.P. for High Wycombe, 1883-5; J.P. for E. Riding of Yorkshire; gov. of W. Australia, 1895 to 1900.
SMITH, Henry J.—B. 1859; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., 12th Nov., 1883; 2nd cl. ditto, 1st June, 1897; Queen's mess., 1st Apr., 1898; asst. office keeper, 22nd May, 1907.

SMITH, Lieut.-Col. Henry Robert, C.M.G. (1911), I.S.C., J.P.—B. 1843; ed. Kingston (Ont.) gram. schl.; entd. civ. ser., Canada, 1859; apptd. deputy-sergeant-at-arms, H. of C., 1872; sergeant-at-arms, 1892; is also hon. A.D.C. to H.R.H. the Gov.-Gen. of Canada.
SMITH, Herbert Francis.—B. 1873; Ed. at Malvern coll. (class. scholar); entd. Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1893; asst. cashier, 1900; head of gen. stores dept., August, 1904.

SMITH, James Alfred.—B.1871; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902; asst. sec. to col. regns. comteee, 1907-8.

SMITH, Jas. Carmichael.—B. 1852; ch. clk. and storekr., Imperial treasy. and commissariat depts., Bahamas, June, 1876, to Aug., 1879; mem. bd. of educ., 1886 to 1892; postmr., 1889 to 1893; J.P., 1890; elected mem. of Bahamas House of Assembly in 1882-89 and 1896; resigned 1897; asst. postmr-gen., Sierra Leone, Dec., 1896; J.P., 1897; ag. postmr-gen., 1897, 1899, and 1900; ag. curator of intestates, S. L. from Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900; postmr-gen. and man. of gov't sav. bank, 1900; ag. collr. of cust., Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. col. treas. in 1906 and 1907; author of "The Distribution of the Produce"; "Inter-Temporary Values, or the Distribution of the Produce in Time"; "The Trust and the Gold Trust."

SMITH, James Landreth.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman, survey off., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1896, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; comsnr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904; survr. of crown lands, 1904; ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, to 16th Jan., 1907.

SMITH, J. Noel.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as ast. res., N. Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910.

SMITH, Sir John Smalman, Kt. Bach. (1896).—M.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; b. 1847; called to the bar, In Tem., 1872; special pleader Oxford circuit; puisne judge, G. Coast Coll., 1883; judge, Lagos, 1886; ch. just., Lagos, 1889; ret., 1896.
SMITH, Laurence.—Ed. Bedford mod. schl.; clk. in acta. dept., Nyassaland Prot., 1899; 3rd ast. treas., Oct., 1902; 2nd ast. treas., Dec., 1908.

SMITH, Lindsay Lea.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comsnr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regis. of shipping) comsnr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904; and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regis. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. ast. comsnr., Dec., 1910, to Jan., 1911.
SMITH, Miles Staniforth.—Comsnr. for land and dir. of agric., Papua, 16th May, 1907; adminstr., comsnr. for lands, and dir. of mfctcs, agric. and pub. wks., 30th Nov., 1908.
SMITH, Norman Lockhart.—B. 1867; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings exhibtnr.), 3rd cls. mods., 3rd Lit. Hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 20th Oct., 1910.

SMITH, Ralph Sydney.—B. 1875; entd. Navy, acctnt. branch, 15th July, 1892; ast. paymaster, 27th June, 1896; paymaster, 13th Jan., 1905; staff paymaster, 18th Jan., 1909; served in H.M.S. "Thetis" during Cretan troubles, 1898-99, and in blockading squadron at Delago Bay during S. African War, 1899-1900 (medal and gratuity); retired from Navy, 8th May, 1910; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1st June, 1907.

SMITH, Richard William, A.M.I.C.E., B.A., B.A.I.—P.W. Dept., Ceylon; b. 1856; ed. at the Grammar Schl., Chard, Somerset, 1874; and Dublin Univ. (Trinity College), 1875-79; employed in engineer's off., D.W.W. Rly., 1879; res. engrn., Wicklow Harb. works, 1880-83; ast. to Mr. J. Lanyon, Belfast (ryls., water-wks., drainage, etc.), 1883-85; res. engrn., Cleggan Harb. Works, 1885-88; ast. res. engrn., St. Heller's Harb. Works (Jersey), 1888-89; supt. P.W. Pahang, S.S., 1889-92; D.E. irrgn. dept., Ceylon, 1892-94; ag. prov. engrn., N.W.P., 1894-97; dist. engrn. and ag. prov. engrn., S.P., 1898; seconded to railway extensions staff, 1898; seconded to "Water Supply Schemes," 1898-1900; charge of Boer Camp Works, 1900; "Water Supply Schemes," 1901-02; seconded as res. engrn., proposed convict prison works, Seychelles, 1902; returned to Ceylon 13th Aug., 1902; engaged in designs of water supplies to the towns of Negombo, Jaffna, Pamban (S. India), Mannar, Galle and Matara; ast. dir. of irrgrtn., Colombo, 1st Jan., 1905; dir. of irrgrtn., 1909.
SMITH, Robert Murray, C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1831; agt.-gen. for Victoria in United Kingdom, 1881-86.
SMITH, Hon. Sydney.—Formerly M.P. for Macquarie, New South Wales in the Federal parlmt.; postmr-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, in Reid-McLean ministry, 1904-05.

SMITH, Sydney Frederick, F.R.G.S.—B. 1870; clk. in treasy., Fiji, 22nd May, 1893; elk., native dept., 1st Jan., 1894; stip. mag., and tax insp., Kadavu (in addition to duties as elk. in native dept.), 1st Jan., 1896; ch. clk., native dept., 4th Feb., 1897; prov. inspnr., 1st Jan., 1899; ag. stip. mag., tax inspnr., and ast. nat. comsnr., Lau Archipelago, 13th Jan., 1902; ditto, Lautoka, 21st Jan., 1903; ast. nat. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1904; sub-collr. of cust. and postmr., Lautoka, 6th May, 1904; on leave from Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1905, and acted as war corre. with Gen. Oku's forces in Russo-Japanese war; ag. stip. mag. and tax inspnr., Lautoka, from 15th Apr., 1907; ag. gov'r's comsnr., Naisaiiri; and stip. mag., Rewa, 1st Feb., 1909; stip. mag., 1st grade, Macuata, 1st Feb., 1910.

SMITH, Thomas Sercombe, B.A., I.M.R. (Lond.).—Hong Kong cadet, 1882; attached to C.O., 1883; passed cadet, 1886; ag. asst. regis.-gen., 1886-90, except for five months as ag. pol. mag.; asst. regis.-gen., 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1893; ag. puisne judge, various dates, 1895-7, and in 1900, 1901, 1902, 1904 and 1905; treas., Dec., 1896; pol. mag., July, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1898-9, for 3 months in 1901, and from Aug., 1905, to Dec., 1906; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., 1907; ag. chief judicial commr., F.M.S., 1911.

SMITH, WM. EDWARD.—Employed on L & N.W. Rly., 1872-82; asst. traffic man., Indian guaranteed rly. (Scinde, Punjab, and Delhi), 1883-86; ag. district traffic man., Lahore and Delhi div.; gen. man., Barbados Rly., 1889; gen. and traffic man., Trinidad Govt. Rly., Feb., 1894; visited Jamaica and reported on Bananas industry, 1904; comsrr. for Trinidad, Colonial and Indian Exhibn., London, 1905; is a J.P.; gen. man. of gvt. rly., G. Coast, 1906; major comdt., rly. volks., May, 1907; offl. mem., Sekondi town council.

SMITH, SIR WILLIAM FREDERICK HAYNES, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1863; solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1865; atty.-gen., Oct., 1874; admtd. the gvt., Apr. to Sept., 1884, and in 1887; gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1888; gov., Bahamas, 1895; high comsrr., Cyprus, 1898-1904.

SMITH, HON. SIR WILLIAM JAMES, KT. BACH. (1896).—M.A., LL.M. Trinity Hall, Camb.; b. 1853; acted as puisne judge of the G. Coast Col. from Dec. 1880, to July, 1881; puisne judge of the sup. ct. of Cyprus, 1882; ch. just., 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1898; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Mar., 1902.
SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—B.Sc., M.B., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn. cent. bd. of health, and coroner, 1903.

SMITHERS, CAPT. JAMES HENRY.—B. 1872; served in Cape Colony med. corps., 29th Oct., 1889, to 8th July, 1892; Cape Town Highlanders, 9th July, 1892, to 2nd Oct., 1897; served as lieut. in Langeberg campaign, for which recd. Cape General Ser. Medal with clasp “Bechuanaland”; capt., Green and Sea Point Town Guard, 8th Jan., 1900, to 30th May, 1901; capt., 1st Batt. Cape Peninsula Regt., 1st June, 1901, to 31st Dec., 1902, when disbanded, temporarily in command, afterwards adjutant, lieut. and qrtmr., Cape Garrison Artillery, 1st Nov., 1904; capt. and qrtmr., 15th Nov., 1905.

SMITH-STEINMETZ, GERARD ARCHIBALD JOHN.—B. 1878; ed. at Stonyhurst coll.; cadet, Straits Settlements, Oct., 1902; in charge of S.S. emigr. depot, S. India, June, 1904; passed final exam. in Tamil, July, 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Feb., 1905; passed cadet, July, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Sept., 1906; ag. asst. supt. of Indian imgrts., Nov., 1907; lent for service in F.M.S., 1909; dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1910.

SMUTS, GEN. THE HON. JAN CHRISTIAN.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Elden scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripos, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comtee. of Het Vlok; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of the Interior, min. of mines, and min. of defenc. Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SMUTS, JOHANNES, L.S.O. (1908).—Cl. treasury, Cape, 1882; acted as priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, 1885-6; to Sir H. A. Smyth, 1889; to Lieut.-Gen. H. E. Cameron and to Sir H. Loch, 1891; sec. to Hon. J. E. Hofmeyr (Swaziland Convention), 1890; as despatch clk., govt. house, 1886; 2nd class clk., 1887; ag. priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gen. Cameron and to Sir H. B. Loch, 1891 to 1895; ch. clk. to hon. comsrr., 1891; acted as clk. of exec. com., 1891 and 1895; Br. consul in Swaziland, Aug., 1895; served on staff of Sir C. Warren during part of S. African war; regis. of deeds, Apr., 1904; chmn. of Swaziland concessions comsrr., 1904.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH. (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub. R.I.L.L.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1st L.L.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; ch. just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., S. Coast, 1911.

SMYTH, C. E. OWEN, I.S.O. (1903).—B. ed. at Erasmus Smith Sch., Dub.; spectac. clk., S. Australia, 1876; profnl. clk., 3rd L.L.D., 1878; 1st class, 1883; supt. of pah. and head of works and bldga. dept., S. Australia, 1886.

SMYTH, HERBERT WASHINGTON.—B. ed. at Westmstr. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; L.L.M., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., barr.-at-law; clk. of sup. ct., Transvaal; unpaid asst. to adviser to office of woods, 1891; sec. to mines, Siam, 1891; director-gen. of mining geological survey, Siam, 1895; sec. to Legation, 1897 (order of White Elephants); Murchison award of R.G.S. for work in Siam, 1898; sec. to mines dept., Transvaal, M.L.C., Transvaal, 1906; mem. exec. com. ag. comsrr. of mines, 1906; sec. for mines, ag. sec. for mines, Union of S. Africa; comsrr. of mines, Natal, 1910; author “Five Years in Siam,” “Mast and Sail in India and Asia,” papers on Indo-China, etc.

SMYTH, JAMES LLOYD.—B. 1871; served R.I.C. from 1889 to 1897; transf. from dept., Belfast, to Jamaica constab., Sept., 1897; ag. supt., M. and S. co. gaol, Sept., 1898; Feb., 1901; ast. supt. of pol., Br. Hond., 1906; J.P. for colony; ag. supt. of pol., Sept., to 26th Oct., 1906; vis. just. to Ceylon and dist. priz., Dec., 1906; ag. supt. of pol., 4th Apr., 1907, to 3rd Mar., 1908; vis. just. to Belize priz., June, 1907; ag. dist. constab., Corozal, Dec., 1908, to Feb., 1909.

SMYTHE, HON. CHAS. JOHN.—B. 1852; Natal, 1887; M.L.A., Natal, 1883; sec. 1897-9; col. sec., 1899-1903; prime min. of Natal prov., 1905, to Nov., 1906; sec. to Natal Prov., Union of S. Africa, 31st May.
SOLOMON, HON. A. E.—Atty.-gen. and of educn., Tasmania.

SOLOMON, JOHN.—2nd class ast. of educn., England, Apr., 1868; 1st class ast., June, 1870; ride offr., June, 1871; resign., Jan., 1876; clk., immigr. dept., Br. Guiana, July, 1876; cashier in rec.-gen.'s office, 1878; comm. taxation, 1879.
SOLOMON, MICHAEL CLAUDE.—Ag. clk., island med. dept., Jamaica, Mar., 3rd cl. clk., Oct., 1888; 2nd cl. clk., 1891.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR RICHARD, K.C.B. (1911), K.C.B. (1905), K.C.M.G. (1901), (1907), C.B. (1903), K.C.—B. 1850; ed. S. African Coll., Capetown; St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 23rd wrangler; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1879; legal adviser to late Lord Rosemead on his mission to Mauritius, 1886; mem. of native law comnr.; chrmn. of mining comnr.; mem. of House of Ass. for division of Tembuland, Cape; atty.-gen., Cape, 1898-1900; legal adviser to Transvaal adminstr., 1901-1902; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 19th June, 1902; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1905; agent-gen. for Transvaal in London, 1907; high comnr. in London of the Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1907).—B.A., Cantab.; admitted to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised in Cape Colony; asst. legal adviser, Cape, 1883; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1887; pres., spec. treason court, 1900; 1st puisne judge, Transvaal, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
SORZANO, TILDEBER.—Draughtsmen, crown lands office, Trinidad, 1874; first ditto, survey dept., Aug., 1881.

SOUTHORN, WILFRID THOMAS.—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1904; N. C. Prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr., customs, Colombo, July, 1909.

SOUZA, SIMON ISIDORO DE.—B. 1863; ed. at the R. Catholic gram. schl., Lagos; 1st despatch clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Lagos, June, 1895; acted as clk. of the legis. coun., 1900; regar. of correspondence, June, 1900; conf. clk. to gov. and clk. of leg. coun., July, 1901; ag. priv. sec. to gov., and clk. to exec. coun., Feb., 1902; in charge of Ibadan residency, Nov., 1903.

SPALDING, COL. WARNER WRIGHT, C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1844; lieut., Royal Marines, 1862-1869; carried colours in operations at Simonosehie, Japan, 1864; N. S. Wales artill., 1871-1896; 2nd in cmd. of N.S.W. Soudan conting., 1880; ch. mag. Norfolk Is., 1896-1898.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNET, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripos, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripos, Part I, 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comnr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just. N. Nigeria, 1908.

SPENCE, FRANK.—B. 1864; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., Fiji, 1880; clk. to comnrs. of wks., Oct., 1884; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 1884, 1885, and 1887 to 1896; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific, 1889 to 1896; stip. mag., Cakandrove and Bua, 1896 to 1898; provincial instr. and stip. mag., Cakandrove and Bua, 1899; ditto, Cakandrove, 1901; gov.'s comnr. or Namose and stip. mag., Navua, Jan., 1904.
SPENCE, J. B.—Med. supt. of Colombo lun. asyl., Ceylon, 1886.

SPENCE, R. O. H.—Cler. asst., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 16th Aug., 1886; ag. 2nd clk., P.W. dept., Aug., 1889; ag. 3rd cl. clk., treasy., Jan., 1890; gov. offr., govt. id. dept., Apr., 1890; clk., govt. id. dept., May, 1890; sec., Bartika comnrs., June, 1891, to May, 1893; clk., dept. mines, Oct., 1892; ag. 3rd cl. clk., dept. mines, Apr., 1893; govt. offr., dept. mines, July, 1893; J.P., May, 1894; comsnr. to admr. oaths, June, 1894; dist. govt. offr., dept. mines, Aug., 1896; warden, dept. mines, Nov., 1896; obtid. certif. as sworn land surv., June, 1898; ag. ch. clk., dept. mines, Feb., 1900; obtained 1st cls. certifa. in assaying and mine surg., Camborne schl. of mines, 1902; elected assoc., Inst. of M. and M., Lond., Apr., 1902; 1st cls. offr., dept. of lands and mines, Apr., 1903; ag. asst. comsnr. of lands and mines, Apr. to Dec., 1904; 1st cls. clk., dept. of lands and mines, July, 1905; ch. clk., ditto, Oct., 1905; ag. comsnr., ditto, June to Aug., 1906.

SPENCER, CYRIL EDWARD.—B. 1873; entd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1898; also clk. to legis. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comnr. on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspr., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comdt., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in comd. of diva. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov. of pris. and asst. to dist. comnr., and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comsnr., Apr. to Dec., 1905; passed in mod. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transf. to B. East Africa, as ast. dist. comnr., Nov., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. comnr., Mombasa, from July, 1907.

SPRIGG, THE RT. HON. SIR JOHN GORDON, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; represented E. London in Cape assem. 1869-1904; col. sec. and premier, Feb., 1878, to May, 1881; col. treas., May, 1884, to Nov., 1886; premier, Nov., 1886; apptd. comdr. legion of honour for services in Paris Exhib., 1889; resig. office, July, 1890; again treas., 1893, and also premier, 1896; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; resig. premiership, Oct., 1898; again premier 1900-04; M.L.A. for E. London, 1908.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid. Temp.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. asst. regist., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros., May, 1905; ag. sol. gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908.

STANFORD, WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1850; mag.'s clk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Goaleka war, 1877-8 (medal); mem. native laws and customs comnr., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1; on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Grigualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs comnr., Sept., 1903, to Jan., 1905; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembuland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909.
STANLEY, GEOFFREY ARMSTRONG.—Ed. at Bath and City of London Schl.; G.W. rly., 1897 to 1901; asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., 23rd May, 1901.

STANLEY, HERBERT JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (B.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910.

STANLEY, W. B.—Served with 1st Border regt., occupation of Crete 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (medal and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjut., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901, (medal and clasp); ag. trav. commr., Gambia, May and June, 1901; trav. comsnr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. engineering, Chatham, 1905; resig. coman., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1910.
STANNUS, HUGH STANNUS.—M.B. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1901; med. offr., Nyasaland Prot., May, 1905.

STANTON, CAMERON.—B. 1861; 3rd cls. clk., dept. of finance, Canada, 1879; promoted to 2nd cls., 1883; transf'd. to dept. of marine and fisheries, 1888; promoted to 1st cls., 1892; ch. clerkship, 1901; asst. dep. min., marine and fisheries, 1908.

STANTON, LIONEL WM.—B. 1843; inspr. of schls., S. Aust., 1876; asst. inspr.-gen., 1892; clrn. of bd. of inspr., 1896; inspr.-gen. of schls., 1902; sec. to min. of educn. and sec. of educn. dept., 1906.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS.—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbour. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; is also supt. of port, recvr. of wreck and regiar. of shipping; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910.

STEDMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY.—Ed. at Weymouth Coll.; articled to borough engnr. and surv. of Dorchester, 1895 to 1898; asst. to same, 1898 to 1903; on staff of superintending civil engnr., H.M. Breakwater, Portland, 1903 to July, 1906; asst. engnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 27th July, 1905.

STEELE, THOMAS MELLEFONTE.—B. 1875; B.A. Roy. Univ., Dublin; ed. at Wesley Coll., Dublin; 2nd div. clk., nat. debt. off., Dec., 1894; transf'd. to P.W.D., Ireland, 1896-1904; examr. of accts. audit dept., O.R.C., Sept., 1904; dist. examr. of accts., July, 1906.

STEELE, WALTER MATTHEW.—B. 1868; clerical asst., treasy., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1884; 5th class clk., Feb., 1886; 4th class, July, 1888; 3rd class, Apr., 1890; ag. clk.in-charge, sub-treasy. and savings bk., Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; 2nd class clk. (acctnt.), govt. land dept., Apr., 1896; asst. treas., So. Nigeria, July, 1904; ag. treas., Cent. or Niger Prov., 1st May, 1906; ditto, Calabar Prov., 23rd July, 1907; ditto, Lagos or W. Prov., 7th Dec., 1908; ditto, Cent. or Niger Prov., 16th June, 1909.

STEERE, CHAS. JAS. LEE.—B. 1868; probtn. clk., col. sec.'s off., W. Aust., Feb., 1885; clk., Aug., 1885; ag. clk., gov.'s off., 1889-1890; clk. of legis. coun. and clk. of parlts., Dec., 1890 to June, 1901; clk. of leg. assem., June, 1901.

STEIN, JOHN.—Ed. at Christ's Coll., Finzels, and at Neuwind, Germany; sec. to consulates in Germany, Sweden and Norway, and Denmark in Mauritius, May, 1889, to Dec., 1896; apptd. S. of S., F.O., an ast. dist. comsnr. in H.R.Y. Niger Coast Protectorate, Apr., 1897; ag. dist. comsnr., Opopo, June, 1897, to June, 1898; Beart Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900; promoted dist. comsnr. Jan., 1900; ast. sec. to govt., Southern Niger June, 1900; ag. sec. to govt., Sept., 1900; to 1901, and from June to Nov., 1901; West Afr. medal with clasp, 1899; ment. in despatches in connection with Aro expedition, 1901-2; ret., 1904.

STEPHEN, CHELVUM.—Ind. interp. to res. mag., Durban, Natal, Jan., 1875; dist. Ind. interp. to res. mag., Umlazi dist. of Durban, Mar., 1875; Tamil interp., sup. ct., Stephen, Guy Neville.—Ed. Paris, Marseilles, London; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1881; licentiate of medicine, France, 1882; ag. house surg., co. hosp.; ditto, ast. med. offr., Middlesex asylum (Colney Hatch); surg. to the consuls in Norway and Sweden, Marseilles, 1881; to the Br. Consulate and Seameh's Home, Mars., 1882; dist. med. offr., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1883; offr., central prison, gen. and ophthalmic med. offr. of health, Nicosia; pres. of the municipality, Nicosia, 1889; ag. ch. med. offr. and 1890; surg. to Smyrna Hosp., 1894.
STEPHEN, HON. SIR MATTHEW HENRY BACH.—B. 1828; mem. legis. bd., N.S.W., 1869-71; judge, sup. ct., Mat., ret. from bench, 1903.
STEPHEN, WILLIAM WILBERFORCE.—Chief secretariat, N.S. Wales, 1852; priv. sec. to premiers, 1856-58; clk., land dept., 1858; sec. for lands, 1870; sec., atty.-gen., 1880.

STEPHENS, THOMAS NOAKES.—Jun. in Australia; customs, 1864; clk., marine bd., boarding offr., customs, 1866; ast. landings, 1867; confid. clk., 1870; ch. clk., treasy. sec. marine bd., 1877; sec. of customs, 1889; treasy., 1890; collr. of customs, reg. of ships, ch. inspr. of distilleries and excise, pres. of bd., 1894; is a J.P.; transf'd. with cust. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st July, 1901; collr. of cust., S. Aust., 1907.

STEPHENSON, ALBERT EDWARD.—Ed. clk. E. and A. dept., Feb., 1884; local aud., Lagos, Aug., 1888, also ag. local auditor, G. Dept., and apptd. to col. audit branch, Jan., clk. in charge of accts., May, 1893; ast. clk., Aug., 1897; apptd. sen. clk. on amalgamated col. audit branch with E. and A. dept., July, 1906; dir. of col. audit, C.O.; assisted in preparation of colonial instructions, 1889, for which recd. S. of S.

STEVENS, PERCIVAL, Assoc. M.I.C.E. 1857; ast. engnr. govt. rly., Trinida; dist. engnr., Couva extension rly., 1879; engnr. in charge of construction, San Fernando and Guaiacara rly., 1879; 1st ast. engnr. dept., Jan., 1885; has acted as ast. dir., 1885, 6, 8, 9, and 90; engnr., N. div., P.
STILES, ALFRED.—B. 1850; apptd., after exam., messenger C.O., Dec., Queen's mess., Aug., 1882; asst. off. keeper, 17th Feb., 1900; recd. coronation medal, 1902; off. keeper, 22nd May, 1907.
STIRLING, SIR JOHN LANCELOT, K.C.M.G. (1909); KT. BACH. (1902), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. legis. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres. legis. coun., 1901.
STIRLING, RIGHT REV. W. H., D.D.—Bishop of the Falklands.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, M.A. (1911), B.A., F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalen Coll., Camb.; Holmes exhibn., Magdalen Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (lst cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Imp. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, British Guiana, Aug., 1908; is dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., B.G.; comsrr. for Br. Guiana at internat. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; author of several papers and articles relating to fungus diseases of W. Indian crops, agric., and the breeding and selection of sugar cane seedlings.

STOCKER, JAMES EDWARD.—Cape Mounted Rifles, S. Africa, 1897-1902; S. African war, 1899-1902; medals (King's and Queen's), clapsa, Transvaal, Free State, and Cape Col.; astl. collr., E. Africa Prot., 27th May, 1904.

STOKER, WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temp.; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Mar., 1898; mem. fed. and island ex. and leg. couns.; ag. 1st puisne judge conjointly with atty.-gen., Nov. to Dec., 1901; atty.-gen., Barbados, July, 1902; chmn., Barbados quarantine comm., 1902-3; K.C., Barbados, 3rd Apr., 1903; M.L.C., Apr. to Oct., 1903; ag. pres. of educn. bd., May to June, 1903; ag. ch. just., 1st Aug. to 30th Oct., 1903; deleg. for Barbados, and elec. pres. at Brit. W. Ind. conf. on quarantine, Apr. to May, 1904; M.L.A. for St. Michael's, 4th July, 1905; re-elected, 23rd July, 1906; chmn. of spec. comttee. on liquor licensing system, and as to tobacco industry, 1906-7; pres. of W. Indian cent. quarantine authority, May, 1907; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, 31st Aug., 1907; ag. chief just., E. and C. Provs., Sept., 1907, to 31st Mar., 1908; ag. atty.-gen., 1st June to 14th Sept., 1908, and in Nov., 1909; ag. ch. just., 1st Dec., 1909.

STONE, SIR EDWARD ALBERT, K.C.M.G. (1912), KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1844; clk. to atty.-gen., W. Australia, 1860; called to bar, 1865; clk. to legis. coun., 1870; nominee mem. of ditto, 1880-2; crown solr., 1882; puisne judge, 1884; acted as ch. just., 1880, 1881, 1887, and 1889; ch. just., 1901; admstr., 1901-2; retired from bench, 1906; lieut.-gov. of W. Aust., 7th May, 1906.

STONE, E. C. M.—Clk. to registr., Trinidad, 1875; ch. clk., 1878; clk. of complaint ct., 1878; ch. clk. to registr., sup. ct., 1880; registr., ct. of survey, 1883; ag. registr. sup. ct., 1880, 1881, 1883, and Apr., 1884, to July, 1885, and June to Sept., 1886; ch. clk. to registr.-gen., Sept., 1887; dep. registr.-gen., Oct., 1889; ag. registr.-gen., 1892-93; is a comsrr. of affidavits, and supt. registr. of births and deaths for Port of Spain.
STONE, ROBERT GEORGE.—Ast. paymaster, 1st King's African rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906.
STORDY, ROBERT JOHN.—Uganda transport service, 1st Jan., 1898; chief veterinary offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1st Apr., 1901.
STORR, IRA WILLIAM.—B. 1847; entd. ser. of the old govt. of Canada at Quebec, 1864; 3rd cls. clk., dept. of the S. of S., Ottawa, 1878; 1st cls. clk., 1891; ch. clk., 1905.
STORRS, FRANCIS JOHN TOWNSEND.—Apptd. astt. collr., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1899; 2nd cls. dist. res., Apr., 1906.

STOUT, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. solr., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1875; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for immigr., 1878; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educ., 1884-87; fellow and chancellor of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1899; mem. of coun. of Victoria Coll.

STRACHAN, W. HENRY W., C.M.G. (1902), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), F.L.S.—Jun. res. med. offr., pub. hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, 1882; sen. res. med. offr., 1885; sen. med. offr., 1892; mem. of bd. of govrs., Inst. of Jamaica, 1892; chief med. offr. of Lagos, Dec., 1897; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1899, to Mar., 1900; P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906.

STRACHEY, CHARLES.—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtee., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; principal clk., 1st Jan., 1907.

STRANGE, WM. LUMSDEN, M.I.C.E.—B. 1867; served in P.W.D., Bombay, 1879 to 1901; under-sec., P.W.D., Govt. of India, July, 1901; supting. engr., 2nd cl., Dec., 1906; seconded as dir. of irrigtn. and water supply, Transvaal, 25th Apr., 1903; author of "Indian Storage Reservoirs with Earthen Dams"; recd. Telford premium, Inst. C.E., 1897.
STRATHAIRN, GEORGE CECIL, M.B. Ch. (Edin.).—Med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prota., 20th July, 1903.

STRATHCONA AND MOUNT ROYAL, LORD, INT BARON (U.K. creat. 1897), SIR DONALD A. SMITH, G.C.M.G. (1896), G.C.V.O. (1908); K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1820; Gov. of Hudson's Bay Territory; special comsrr., Red River, 1869; mem. of Canadian parlmt., 1871, dir. of Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; high comsrr. for Canada, since 1896; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtees., 1896 and 1899; Lord Rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1899; chancellor of Aberdeen Univ., 1903.
STREET, PHILIP WHISTLER.—Puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907.

STRICKLAND, SIR GERALD, COUNT DELLA CATENA, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripods; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn Temp., and pres. cholera comn., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for negotiations on Imp. veto of appts. to See of Malta; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. astt. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres., comtee. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comtee. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comm. in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factors for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gen. Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; assumed govt., 31st May, 1909.
STRICKLAND, R. B., M.A. (Dublin)—Inst. of schools, Jamaica, 1st Mar., 1894, to 28th Aug., 1902; ditto, Ceylon, 29th Aug., 1902.
STRONACH, F. L.—Land purchase off. P.W.D., W. Aust., 1896; under sec. for pts. wks., May, 1905.

STUART, ALEXR.—B. 1861; ed. St. Andrews Univ.; regist. of impts. and expts., Singapore, Sept., 1890; temporarily attached to H.M. customs, and trade marks branch of the patent off., London, 1897-8; comm'l. correspondent for S. and F.M.S. to intell. branch of B. of T., 1901.

STUART, HON. CHARLES ALLAN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1864; ed. High schl., Strathroy, Ont.; and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ont., 1896; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1898; elect. first prov. legis., Alberta, 1905; judge of supre. ct., N.W.T., 1906; judge of supreme ct., Alta., 1907; chancellor of univ. of Alberta, 1908.

STUART, JAMES.—Ed. St. John's Col., Hurstpierpoint; matric. Lond. and Cape; medal, Natal, Eng. lit. exam., 1891; clk. of Pietermaritzburg, Feb., 1888; clk. and int. R.M., Eshowe, May, 1888; 1st clk. and int. res. comsrr. and ch. mag., Zululand, Feb., 1889; acted on various occasions as sec., res. comsrr. ch. mag., sct. Zululand; interp. to Brit. Swaziland, 1894 to 1896; and in charge of deputations to Capetown and England; acted as Br. consul, Swaziland, 1895; R.M. vuma dist., Zululand, 1896; mag., Lower Tdiv., 1899; astt. mag., Durban div., 1901; sec. for colony, 1902; astt. sec. for native affairs, sec. to the coun. for native affairs, 1909.

STUBBS, REGINALD EDWARD.—B. 1857; at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical moda., 1878; cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; apptd. compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1901; 1st cls. clk., May, 1901; 1st cls. clk., Dec., visited S. Stilimta., F.M.S. and Hong Kei, inquire into question of revision of ev. salaries, 1910-11; edited "Lucas' History of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd ed., 1906; joint editor of C.O. List since 1910.

SUETER, EDWARD BEAUMONT FRASER.—B. 1880; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; ed. Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to astt. agt., Colombo, Apr., 1905; astt. astt. govt., Kegalla, Nov., 1907; dist. judge, Kegalla, 1908; pol. mag., Kandy, Jan., 1910.

SULLIVAN, WILLIAM WILFRED, K.C.—in P.E.I., 1843; called to the bar 1867; elected legislature in 1872, and has continuously represented the same constituency; created a govt. of P.E. Is., 1876, and by the govt. Canada 1878; is a dep. judge in the admin. was a mem. of the exec. coun., holding the of solr.-gen., Apr., 1873, till Sept., 1878; of the Opposition in the legislature is prime min. and atty. and advoc.-gen.
SUMMERS, WALTER LLOYD.—R. 1878; crown lands off., S. Aust., July, 1887; clk. off., May, 1892; chief clk., July, 1902;
SURMON, WILLIAM BOWKER.—Sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1897; asst. res. mag. in Western Kalahari, 1907-1908; inspr. Bech. Prot. police, 1909.

SUTER, WM. CHAS.—Asst. master, govt. English schls., Singapore, 22nd Nov., 1884; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1st July, 1888; shorthand reporter to leg. coun. in addition to other duties, 1st July, 1897.
