SUTHERLAND, A. R. —M.A., Aberdeen Univ.; Science mast., Glen Urquhart H.G. schl., 1903-5; asst. mast., Campbelltown gram. schl., 1906-6; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-1910; ag. asst. master, pol. schl., 1909.
SUTHERLAND, GEORGE.—B. 1877; asst. man., Barbuda, Leeward Is., 1st Oct., 1908; ag. man. from 15th June, 1910.

SUTHERLAND, HON. ROBERT FRANKLIN, K.C., B.A.—B. 1859; ed. pub. and high schls., Newmarket and Windsor, Ontario, and Toronto and Western Unive.; called to the Bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1900; re-elected 1904 and 1908; speaker of H. of C., 1905-1908; judge, High Court of Just., Ontario, 1909.

SUTHERLAND, WM.—Apptd. to Fiji civ. ser., Jan., 1880; clk. recr. gen.'s office, Sept., 1880; clk. regisr.-gen., Jan., 1882; stip. mag. of the col., Apr., 1883; ag. collr. of cust., Sept., 1897; ag. recr. gen., Mar., 1898; native comsnr., 1898; ag. col. sec. and recr. gen., Jan., 1903.

SUTTOR, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS B., KT. BACH. (1903).—B. 1839; postmr.-gen., N.S. Wales, Aug., 1890, to Nov., 1881; min. of pub. instruction, 1881 to 1883, and again, 1891-4; representative of col. at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; pres. of legis. coun. of N.S. Wales, June, 1903.

SWAIN, ALFRED WILLIAM.—Clerical asst., regisr.'s off., Barbados, 1879; jun. clk., G.P.O., Br. Guiana, June, 1881; 3rd cls. clk., Jan., 1883; 2nd cls., Dec., 1889; 1st cls., Apr., 1900; ch. clk., Sept., 1900; ag. postmr.-gen., May, 1901, to May, 1902; and Aug. to Dec., 1904; postmr.-gen., 18th May, 1907; 2nd lieut. Br. Guiana volunteers, 1889, ditto militia, 1891; lieut., 1892; capt., 1895; maj., 1900; ag. comdt. of local forces, July, 1901, to Feb., 1902; Hythe certif., 1893; Colonial and Aux. forces long service medal; reed. thanks of govt. on retiring in 1902, and retains rank of Major.

SWAIN, GEORGE LLEWELLYN DOUGLAS.—B. 1858; 1st clk. and bookkeeper, pol. dept., British Guiana, Apr., 1882; inspr. of pol., May, 1884; ch. inspr., Mar., 1892; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July to Sept., 1893; on special service with Mr. McTurk, Feb., 1895, to take over Urnau from Venezuelans, remaining in command there till July, 1895; volr. in Ashanti expdn., Nov., 1895, to Mar., 1896; mentioned in despatches (star); two months' training with R.I.C. at Dublin, 1890; Hythe, P.S., and 1st cl. ambulance certif.; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1898, to Jan., 1899; promoted ch. county inspr., and 2nd in command, 18th Nov., 1899; ag. insp.-gen., 10th May to 21st Aug., 1900; servd. on comsn. apptd. to inquire into admtn. of poor law relief, 1900; ag. inspr.-gen. of police, and col. comdt. of militia, 8th May to 3rd Sept., 1902; deputy inspr.-gen. of police, Trinidad, 30th September, 1903; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab., and comdt. of local forces with local rank of lieut.-col. and seat in exec. and legis. couns., 7th May to 10th Nov., 1905; ditto, with seat in legis. coun., 15th Apr., to 16th Sept., 1907; inspr.-gen. of constab. and comdt. local forces, 17th Sept., 1907 (with seat in legis. coun.); mem. of exec. coun., June, 1910; mem. of medical coman.

SWAN, ROBERT ABRAHAM.—B. 1849; matric. at Madras Univ., Dec., 1868; passed special law test prescribed for unconvenanted supts. and ast. supts. of pol.; 2nd class inspr. of pol. in the Cuddapah dist., Madras, May, 1870; in Aug., 1871, 1st class; May, 1874, passed in Telugu; Oct., 1876, special ast. to sub-comr. and joint mag. of Cuddapah for famine relief duty, and in same month apptd. mag. with 1st class powers, and placed in charge of the Kadri and Madnapully Taluqs of Cuddapah dist.; in Feb., 1877, stip. mag. Mauritius; Jan., 1878, stip. mag. of Flocq.; and in Aug., 1879, Grand Port and Savanne; mem. of comsn. to frame regulsns. under "labour ordinance of 1878"; member of Lincoln's Inn, 1882; called to the bar, 1887; stip. mag. Br. Guiana, Nov., 1883; in July, 1884, sent on special duty to Madras to organise an emigrn. agency for Br. Guiana; sheriff, Essequibo co., Dec., 1887; chmn. lands titles inquiry comsn., Feb., 1890; a revising barrister under Br. Guiana Constitn. Order, 1891; sheriff of Berbice and mag. New Amsterdam, 1892; acted as pol. mag., Georgetown, Feb., 1890, and Oct., 1895, to Apr., 1895; mem. of comsn. on poor-relief, 6th Apr., 1900; chairman of ditto, 7th Sept., 1900; apptd. as pol. mag., Georgetown, confirmed by S. of S., 1st Oct., 1900; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1st Oct., 1900; puisne judge, Trinidad, 3rd Aug., 1903; ag. ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 7th July to 12th Oct., 1904, and 26th June to 30th Oct., 1906; 1st puisne judge, Oct., 1906.

SWAYNE, CHARLES RICHARD, C.M.G.(1906).—Stip. mag., Fiji; inspr. of native taxes, 1876; stip. mag. and comsnr. of sup. ct., Lau prov., 1878; sent to Kadava with special authority in native matters, 1879 and 1880; inspr. of Indian and Polynesian labourers, Lau prov., 1883; stip. mag., regisr. and comsnr. of sup. ct. at Levuka, 1885; returned to Lau, 1886; asst. native comsnr., Lau, 1888; ag. Br. res., Gilbert and Ellice Is., and special judicial comsnr. for trial of certain cases, Oct., 1893, to Nov., 1895; stip. mag., Rewa, and comsnr., Naitasiri, 1898; ag. native comsnr., and mem. exec. coun., 29th July, 1901, to 11th Sept., 1902; comsnr. Naitasiri, Sept., 1902; ag. comsnr., Rewa, 27th May to 31st Dec., 1903; offl. mem. legis. coun., 7th Apr., 1905; ret. 1906.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.B.(mil.) (1904); grand offr. of order of the Crown of Italy (1905).—B. 1863; ed. at naval coll., Gosport, and abroad; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1882; entd. Welsh regt., 1883; Bengal staff corps, 1884; adjut., 1888; capt., 1894; local lieut.-col., 1900; brevet lieut.-col., 1901; brevet-col., 1903; temp. brig.-gen., 1903-1906; retired from army, 1906; served with Burma expdn., 1885-86 (medal with clasp); intell. offr., Hukong Valley reconnaissance; in command of advanced base exploration, Turong River and Sing Pho country, Nov., 1895, to Mch., 1896 (McGregor medal); exploration to Somaliland, Feb. to July, 1891 (thanked by resolution of Bombay govt., and appreciation by govt. of India); Gildessa affair on Abyssinian frontier, 1892 (commendation by resolution of Bombay govt.); engaged on delimitation of internat. frontier between French and British Somaliland, 1892; served for five years on intell. staff, India, as staff-capt. and D.A.Q.M.G.; apptd. 2nd-in-command, Uganda Rifles, on active serv. in Jubaland, E. Africa (medal with clasp); on active serv. in Uganda, 1899; selected to command Somaliland police force, 1900; raised and organised levies, and commanded expeditionary force in Somaliland, Nov., 1900; defeated and routed Mullah (ment. in desps., medal with clasp, brevet lt.-col., thanked by gov't.); organised 6th Batt. Uganda Rifles, second expedit., 1901; again defeated Mullah (C.B., ment. in desps.); apptd. commnr., consul-gen. and commr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., and judge of its high court, Apr., 1902; on special duty in Egypt, 1904; arranged evacuation of Somaliland, and organised control of tribes; British deleg. to conference with Italian deleg. at Lyons in connection with agreement with Italy respecting Somaliland; gov. and commr.-in-chief, British Honduras, 21st Moh., 1906; assumed gov't. 13th Aug., 1906; spec. duty to Canada, with reference to Indian labour (thanked by gov't. of India, 1908); spec. duty to Mexico, 1909.

SWEENEY, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Asst. mast. Boys' Model Primary Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, Feb., 1887; house mast., Maritzburg Coll., 1st Aug., 1888; resig. 30th Sept., 1895; clerical asst., col. office, 30th Oct., 1896; 3rd cl. clk., crown sol'r's office, 1st Feb., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., atty-gen.'s office, 1st July, 1898; acctng. offr., atty-gen.'s dept., 1st July, 1898; apptd. ch. clk. of the legis. assem., 1st Feb., 1901; sec. to parly. reprsentn. comm., Dec., 1903; law lecturer to Natal law soc., June, 1904; has acted as sec., law dept., and as asst. under sec.
SWEENIE, J. W.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., Aug., 1898.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E.B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon; exhibitioner, Worces. Coll., Oxon, June, 1876; 2nd class classical moda., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admstr. the gov't. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admstr. gov't., Apr. to Nov., 1895, again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed gov't., 20th Nov., 1899; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Oct. 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, 15th Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., 30th Jan., 1906; assumed gov't., 26th Moh., 1906.

SWETTENHAM, SIR F. ATHELSTANE, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Sltltns., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and commnr., ct. of requesta, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted to act temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. commnr., Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; commnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhib., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. commnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhib., 1886; res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malay States, 1896; admstr. gov't. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov't. ret., 1904; mem. of royal comm. on Maori, 1909.

SWETTENHAM, SIR J. A., K.C.M.G., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service; ag. pol. mag., Harrispattu, Feb., 1870; pol. Kayta, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrispattu; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; landing and tide survr., Galle, June, 1872; collr. of customs, Jaffna, Oct., 1872; to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, 1873; 2nd asst. col. sec., and clk. of the coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; ag. to Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S. S., 1895; admstr., June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1895; from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov't., Br., 1901; gov't., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904; rec.-gen.

SWINBURNE, HON. GEORGE C.E.—M.L.A. for Hawthorn, Victoria, since 1902; mem. of pub. accts. comtes., 1898-1904; mayor, 1902; of water supply, Victoria, since Apr., 1898; also min. of agric. since Nov., 1904.
SWINDELL, REV. FRANK GUTHRIE (Oxon.).—Chap., Selangor, May, 1902; chap., Malacca, June, 1906; ag. col. Singapore, Apr., 1907, to Jan., 1908.

SYDNEY, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF YORK, JOHN CHARLES WRIGHT, B.A., M.A. Formerly curate of Kilworth-Beauchamp, 1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1896; St. John's, Leeds, 1895-1904; can. res. of Minster Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Chester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop Sydney, 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Anglican Life and Work.”

SYER, WM. CHEVALLIER.—B. 1873; Eastbourne; qualified at schl. of musk., Feb., 1901; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1901; ag. dist. commnr., Degema, Jan., 1902; asst. transport offr., Aro field force column, 1901-1902 (medal with clasp); dist. commnr., Degema, July, 1903, to Jan., polit. offr., Ekpaffia field force, No. 2 Det., Dec., 1904; promoted dist. commnr., 1905; dist. commnr., Akpiko, Aug. to Oct., polit. offr., Abakaliki, Oct., 1905, to Jan., dist. commnr., Degema, Jan. to May, 1907; commnr., Abeokuta, May to Sept., 1907; commnr., Sapele, Feb. to June, 1907; ag. sec. and ag. asst. prov. commnr., Cent. June to Oct., 1907; dist. commnr., Sapele, Dec., 1907; ag. prov. sec. and ag. asst. commnr., Cent. Prov., Dec., 1907; ag. prov. and ag. asst. prov. commnr., E. Prov., Jan., 1908; ag. sen. asst. col. sec., 7th to 31st, 1908; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1st Jan. to 31st, 1909; ag. res., Ibadan, 1st May to 15th, 1909; ag. prov. treasur., W. Prov., 19th to 27th, 1909; dist. commnr., Warri, 12th June, 1910; ag. chief asst. sec., prov. commnr., W. Prov., 14th June to 15th, 1910; dist. commnr., Onitsha, 3rd Oct. to 15th May, 1911; polit. offr., Onitsha, Orlu patrol, 22nd Nov. to 31st Dec., chief asst. sec., 28th Oct., 1911.
SYMONT, SIR JOSEPH HENRY, K.C.M.G., K.C.—B. 1846; mem. of H. of Assem.

TATE, HARRY RUSSELL.—Ed. at Sherborne schl.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 16th Dec., 1897; offr. in charge, E. Africa Transport, Ashanti Field Force, Aug., 1900; transport offr., Ogaden Punitive Force, Jan., 1901; collr., 8th May, 1901; Ashanti medal, 1900; Jubaland medal, 1901.
TAUBMAN-GOLDIE, P.C. (1898), THE RT. HON. SIR GEORGE DASHWOOD, K.C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1846; founder and dep. chmn. of the Royal Niger Co.; chmn., 1895.

TAVERNER, HON. SIR JOHN WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1909).—B. 1834; mem. of Swan Hill Shire coun., Victoria, 1879; twice pres. and resig. in 1889; M.L.A. for Donald and Swan Hill, 1898 to 1904; mem. of first rlyw. standing comtce., min. of agric., comnr. of pub. wks., and vice-pres. of bd. of lands and wks., 1894-1899; min. of lands and agric., and pres. of bd. of lands and wks., 1902 to 1904; agent-gen. for Victoria in London, Feb., 1904; re-apptd. ditto, Feb., 1907.
TAYLOR, ARTHUR WM. FORBES.—B. 1857; 4th clk., treasy., Natal, June, 1878; 3rd clk., Apr., 1879; 2nd clk., July, 1881; 1st clk., audit office, Mar., 1884; inspr., audit dept., 1897; ch. inspr., 1903.

TAYLOR, BASIL REGINALD HAMILTON, F.R.A.S.—B. 1865; entd. R.N. 1878; served in Egyptian war, 1882 (medal and clasp, Khedive's bronze star); lieut., 1888; served on Mediterranean, N. America, and W. India, China, and home stations; res. coman., 1898; rejoined R.N. as comdr. (emergency list), 1906; asst. harbmr., Hong Kong, July, 1899; ag. harbmr., Mar., 1900, to June, 1901, Sept., 1903, to Feb., 1904, and Aug. to Nov., 1905; M.L.C., June, 1900, to June, 1901, Sept., 1903, to Feb., 1904, and Aug. to Nov., 1905; ag. A.S.P. in addition, Oct., 1904, to Nov., 1905; harbmr., marine mag., emigrn. and cust. offr., supt. of imports and exports, regir. of shipping, supt., mercantile marine off., agent of C.I.B., B. of T., and fiscal authority under Brussels Sugar Convention, Mar., 1907; M.L.C., Sept., 1907, to Sept., 1908.

TAYLOR, EDWIN.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; clk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treasy. asst., Uganda, Jan., 1909.
TAYLOR, FRANCIS BRYANT OLDELE.—Ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. of Preceptors, 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Lagos, Feb., 1907.

TAYLOR, FRANK.—Architectural asst., P.W.D., Cape Town, 3rd Nov., 1900, to 8th Jan., 1901; on active serv., lieut. in irregular corps, 8th Jan., 1901, to 7th Apr., 1902; chief draughtsman, P.W.D., O.R.C., 18th Apr., 1902; ch. architect, 1st July, 1905.

TAYLOR, JOHN.—B. 1863; entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Soudan war, 1884-5 (medal and Khedive's bronze star); impl. prison serv., Lond., Dec., 1887, to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize pris., B. Hond., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898; ag. dist. comnr., Toledo, 1st Apr. to 5th Sept., 1905; ditto, Stann Creek, 12th Jan. to 29th May, 1906; ditto, El Cayo, 6th July, 1906, to 15th Apr., 1907; ditto, Stann Creek, 19th June to 17th July, 1907; ditto, Orange Walk, 18th July to 4th Sept., 1907; ditto, Stann Creek, from 6th Sept. to 30th Dec., 1907; ditto, Orange Walk, 7th Jan. to 13th Oct., 1908; ditto, Belize, offl. recr. in bank, and offl. adminstr., 23rd Oct., 1908, to 22nd May, 1909; J.P. for the colony, 1908; ag. dist. comnr., Stann Creek, 12th July to 31st Aug., 1909; ditto, Toledo, 3rd to 13th Sept., 1909; ditto, El Cavo, 1st Oct., 1909, to 9th Jan., 1910; ditto, Stann Creek, 20th Jan., to 4th Mch., 1910; ditto, Orange Walk, 10th Mch., 1910, to 12th July, 1911; ditto, Stann Creek, 19th July, 1911.

TAYLOR, CAPT. STUART CAMPBELL.—Joined King's Own Yorkshire Light Infantry in India, 1892; served with Tirah expndy. force, 1897 (medal with two clasps); A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir Chas. Bruce, G.C.M.G., gov. of Mauritius, 1898; served throughout S. African war, A.D.C. to Gen. Featherstonhaugh, commdg. 9th brig., 1899-1902 (Queen's medal with four clasps, King's medal with two clasps, twice ment. in desps.); served with 2nd batt. K.O. Yorks. Light Infantry in Malta, Crete and England, 1903-1907; seconded from army and apptd. asst. res., N. Nigeria, 20th July, 1907; ret. from army, 1911.

TAYLOR, Sir W. T., K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. recvr.-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. comnr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; ag. recvr.-gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr.-gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1895; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apr., 1900, to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Setttmts., June, 1901; admntd. govt. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apr., 1904, and Feb. to June, 1906; ag. res. gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1904; confirmed, Jan., 1905; ret., 1910; in charge of F.M.S. development agency, London, Oct., 1910.

TEETZEL, HON. JAMES, LL.D.—B. 1853; ed. Woodstock and Galt (Ontario) collegiate insts.; called to the bar, 1877; K.C., 1890; elec. bencher, law soc., Upper Canada, 1891, 1896 and 1900; mayor of Hamilton, Ontario, 1889-1900; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., 1900; justice, high ct., Ontario, 1903; LL.D., MacMaster Univ., 1907; chrmn., Ontario parole bd., 1910; comnr. for revision of Ontario statutes, 1908-09-10.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTINE, B.Sc.(Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; asst. agric. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. govt. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909; govt. chemist and supt. of agric., Leeward Is., Nov., 1909.

TEMPLEMAN, HON. WILLIAM.—B. 1844; apptd. a senator of Canada, 1897; sworn of the priv. coun. and min. without portfolio, 1902; min. of inland rev. and mem. for Victoria, B.C., 1906; min. of inland rev. and of mines, 1907; mem. for Comox-Atlin, B.C., 1909, defeated at g. e., 1911.

TENNANT, HERCULES, C.M.G. (1906).—Barrister-at-law, Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Col., 1873; sec. of law comnr., 1879; represented Caledon in house of assem., 1879-81; lieut., D.E.O.V.R., 1878-81; extra A.D.C. to gov. and comdr.-in-chief, July, 1879; served in Basuto war, 1880-81 (medal), with rank of capt., as asst. staff offr. to Gen. Clarke, C.B., and afterwards to Col. Carrington, C.M.G.; sec. to ch. just., 1882; librarian of the sup. ct., 1882; asst. registr., sup. ct., and official reviser of authorised edition of Cape statutes, 1884; asst. registr., ct. of appeal, Mar., 1896; J.P. for Cape-town and dist., and Capt. dist., July, 1896; ag. taxing offr., sup. ct., Sept., 1884, and Aug., Dec., 1885; Apr. and Oct., 1886; Mar. and Aug., 1887; and Oct., 1887, to Jan., 1889; taxing offr., Feb., 1889; high sheriffl. Offr., 1889; regt. sup. ct., 1894; sec. to law dept., Transvaal, June, 1901; J.P., 1901; advocate of sup. court, 1902; examiner of candidates for admission as conveyancers, 1902; M.L.C., 1906; editor and compiler of "The Notary's Manual"; "Justice of the Peace's Manual"; "Rules of Court"; "Chronological Table and Index of the Same"; "Law of the Colony, 1714-1883"; "Master Servants Laws of the Colony"; joint editor (with authority) of a revised edition of "The Statutes, 1652-1886, and of the Griquas' Statutes"; also compiled (under authority) "The Index of Government Proclamation Notices, 1803-1881."

TENNYSON, Rt. Hon. Lord B. (U.K., creat., 1884), G.C.M.G. (1903), K.C. (1899).—HALLAM TENNYSON, succeeded by the death of his father), Alfred Lord Tennyson poet laureate, 1850-92.—B. 1852; ed. Marlborough Coll., Camb., and Inner Temple; M.A., Hants; mem. of Marlbg. Coll. exec. compared his father's memoir, published in 1878; S. Australia, 1899; gov.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, 1902-1904.

TENNYSON, CHARLES BRUCE LOCKETT (1902).—B. 1879; ed. at Eton, and King's Camb. (scholar); 1st div. 1st cls. class, 1902; Whewell scholar (Internat. Law), Univ., 1903; Arden scholar of Gray's Inn, called to the bar, 1905; legal asst., C.O., 1911.
TEW, GEORGE McLEOD.—Indian pol., 1893-7; Uganda rly. pol., 1899-1903; asst. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1903.

THAINE, ROBERT NIEMANN.—B. 1875; Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1898; asst. to govt. agt. S. Prov., Apr., 1899; Prov., Aug., 1900; comnr. of requests agt., Balapitiya, June, 1901; off. ass't. agt., Ratnapura, Dec., 1901; asst. govt. Kalutara, Mar., 1902; dist. judge, Badulla, 1902; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1904; landr. cust., Colombo, Sept., 1904; asst. govt. Trincomalee, June, 1906; Puttalam, dist. judge, Jaffna, July, 1908.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Grad. examr. of loan accts., audit dept., O.R., Jan., 1904; examr. of accts., ditto, Ist Jul., 1904.
THOMAS, E. J.—Entered Victoria in 1852; receiver of revenue, etc., 1853; sec. to numerous boards and commis.; pres. to Sir B. O'Loughlen, Bart., 1881; sec. pres. dept., 1883.
THOMAS, HON. JOSIAH.—B. 1863; New South Wales, 1894-1901; elected H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected in 1906; P.M.G., C. of A., Apr., 1910; external affairs, 1911.
THOMAS, T. S. W.—Asst. dist. clk., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd asst. sec., Ap., 1910; clk. to legis. coun.

THOMLINE, ROBT.—Intermediate Lond. (economics and polit. science), scholar of Lond. Schl. of Economics and Science; 2nd div. elk. nat. educn. offr., Apr., 1898; ditto, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1899; examr. of postal accts., audit dept., Oct., 1903; sen. examr., audit dept., acctnt., P.W.D., 1905; ch. clk., treas., 1907.

THOMPSON, AUGUSTUS WM.—Asst.clk.of ct., G. Coast, 1873; clk. to Queen's advco., 1874-5; dep. regisr., cent. prov., 1877; ch. regisr., 1880; postmr., Cape Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1884; regisr. and interp., W. Prov., 1884; regisr. of deeds and taxing master, W. Prov.; dist. comsrr., G. C. col., May, 1889.

THOMPSON, Jas., M.I.C.E.—B. 1863; entd. Queen's Coll., Cork, 1879; B.E., Roy. Univ. of Ireland, 1882; pupil at pub. wks. and docks bd., Dublin, 1882-3; asst.engnr., Southport and Cheshire lines extension rly., 1883-84; asst. engnr., survey, design, etc., Cavan, Leitrim and Roscommon light rly., 1884-85; res. engnr., construction of rlys. and harb. wks., Ireland, 1885-89; draftsman and asst. survr., Victorian rly., 1889-91; asst. engnr., P.W.D., W. Australia, 1891-93; res. engnr., Yilgarn rly., May, 1893; dist. engnr., Nov., 1895; engrnr.-in-charge of rly. constn., June, 1896; engrnr.-in-charge of harbs. and rivers (in addition to rly. construction), Aug., 1898; inspctg. engrnr., July, 1902; ag. engrnr.-in-chief, Mar., 1904; engrnr.-in-chief and consulting engrnr. to the Fremantle Harb. Trust, Sept., 1904.

THOMPSON, J.V., B.A.—B. 1872; ed. Dulwich Coll., Blair Lodge, and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1895; English and math. master, Idadi schl., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1899; headmaster, Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1906; inspr. of schls., 1907; J.P. for the Colony, 1909; represented Fiji at Imp. conf. on educn., 1911.

THOMPSON, JOHN.—Ent. Royal Irish constab., Belfast, 1864 to 1870; obtained apptmt. in the convict ser., Woking, England; asst. in reforming prison ser. in Jamaica, Oct., 1883; prin. warder and storekr., gen. penitentiary, to July, 1888, then dep. supt. of same; ag. supt. to Dec., 1894; supt. of St. Catherine dist. prison, Spanish Town, Jamaica, Nov., 1897.
THOMPSON, PERCY G.—Called to the bar, Mid.Tem., 1891; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1904; has acted on several occasions as Crown prosecutor and as pol. mag.

THOMPSON, THOMAS AUGUSTUS.—Called to bar, Oct., 1872; in Easter term, 1874, received comsrr. to act as public prosecutor in the crim.ct., Bahamas; acted as judge of court of common pleas from June to Dec., 1875; pol. mag., Bahamas (being still allowed priv. practice), Jan., 1876; chmn. of man. comtee. of prisons, and presides over investigations into cases of wreck and other marine casualty; mem. of the Bahamas legislature, and trustee of the Nassau Museum and Library, 1881; stip. and circuit mag., 1886; ag. atty.-gen., 1887; ag. ch. just., July, 1890, June to Oct., 1891, and June to Nov., 1892; ag. col. sec. and chmn. of bd. of educn., Oct., 1890; judge and col. sec., Falklands, 1893; admtd. govt., July to Nov., 1894; registr. and marshal, sup.ct., Trinid., 1897.

THOMPSON, WALTER.—Supt.'s asst., Skerritt's training schl., Leeward Is., June, 1892, to Apr., 1894; 4th outdoor offr., treasy. dept., Apr., 1894, to Jan., 1895; 3rd ditto, Jan., 1895, to Dec., 1902; 2nd ditto, Dec., 1902, to Feb., 1903; 1st ditto, Feb., 1903; admeasurer of ships., Nov., 1903; ag. 1st indoor offr., treasy. dept., Dec., 1903; ag. harbmr., Feb., 1904; 1st outdoor offr. and ag. harbmr., May, 1904; appt. 1st outdoor offr. and harbmr. on the amalgamation of offices, June, 1904.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM AUSTIN.—2nd cl. supervsr. G. Coast, Apr., 1894; ag. asst. treas., Cape Coast Castle, Mar., 1895; cashr., Accra, Mar., 1896; ag. asst. treas., Apr., 1896; 1st cl. supervsr., Sept., 1897; sent on serv.in hinterland, Feb., 1898, invalided and retd. from West Afr., Feb., 1900; treas., etc., Falklands, July, 1901; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., in addition to duties as treas., May to Nov., 1902, Apr., 1903, to Jan., 1904, July to Sept., 1905, June to Nov., 1909, and from Jan. to June, 1911.

THOMPSTONE, SYDNEY WILSON, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1863; F.R.C.P. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Camb.); form. house surg., Roy. Infir., Glasgow; appttl. dist. med. offr., Oil Riv. Protec. (now S. Niger.), Mar. 22nd, 1893; asst. prin. med. offr., 1897; ag. prin. med. offr., Aug.-Nov., 1898; Nov., 1899, to June, 1900, and June, 1901, to Oct., 1902; prin. med. offr., N. Nigeria, May, 1903.

THOMSON, ALEX. MCDONALD.—Ed. Abdn. Univ., M.A.; 1st class hon. math., 1883; asst. prof. of math., Aberdeen, 1886-7; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1888; cadet, Hong Kong, 1887; attached to C.O., 1888; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. supt., Victoria gaol, Apr., 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. clk. of couns., Jan., 1891, to Oct., 1892; ag. asst. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1892; ag. regisr.-gen. and provisional mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1893; ag. asst. col. sec., 1894; ag. col. treas., Mar., 1895; and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. governing body of Queen's Coll., 1894; postmr.-gen., 1897; col. treas., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1898; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1902, Nov., 1903, to July, 1904, and in 1907, 1909 and 1910; has served on various comtees. and comsrs.
THOMSON, HON. DUGALD.—B. 1848; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894, 1895, and 1898; mem. of Commonw. parl., Mar., 1901, Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1906; min. for home affairs, ditto, Oct., 1904.

THOMSON, JOHN HASTINGS.—B. 1873; ed. Wallace Hall Academy, Dumfries-shire, Scotland; appttd. asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, May, 1901; ag. consul, Berbers, Sept., 1901, to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Bulhar, Jan., 1904-5; polit. offr. with Gen. Swayne in Abyssinia, Mar. to Apr., 1905; polit. offr. with troops on Abyssinian border, May to July, 1905; ag. sub-comsrr., Zeyla, 2nd June, 1905; Somaliland medal with clasp, 1902-4; dist. offr., Makhir Coast, Aug., 1906; ag. sub-comsrr., Zeyla, 5th June, 1907.

THORBURN, J. JAMIESON, C.M.G. (1907).—Writer, Ceylon, Oct., 1886; ag. govt. agt., N. Cent. Prov., Mar. to Aug., 1889; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Dec., 1893; ag. off. asst. to govt. agts. in various dists. from Oct., 1889, to 1895; ag. 2nd asst col. sec., Jan., 1896; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan. and Dec., 1898, and June, 1899; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Jan., 1899; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1899; ag. sec. central irrigation board, Jan., 1900; principal asst. col. sec. and joint sec., loan board, May, 1900; senior prov. comsrr. (in anticipation of proposed amalgamation of the admstns. of Lagos and S. Nigeria), performed duties of sec. to admstn. of S. Nigeria, dep. high comsrr., S. Nigeria, Mar. to June, 1905; ag. gov., Lagos, and ag. high comsrr., S. Nigeria, July, 1905; ag. col. sec., Lagos, 12th Feb. to 30th Apr., 1906; lieut.-gov. and col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; dep. gov., 24th Dec., 1906, to 4th Jan., 1907, 6th-14th Jan., 1907, and 27th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1907; ag. gov., 14th May to 27th Oct., 1907; govrr., Gold Coast, 24th Oct., 1910; assumed govt., 21st Nov., 1910.

THORNE, WM. HOBART HOUGHTON.—B. 1875; ed. at St. Paul's Schl. and King's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1900; Licencié en Droit de la Faculté de Paris, Nov., 1903: British representative on Internat. Claims Com., at Casa Blanca, 7th May, 1908; employed at F.O. in investigation of Anglo-American pecuniary claims, 15th Sept., 1910, to 11th July, 1911; pres. of dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 20th July, 1911.

THORNHILL, Ponsonby.—Supernumerary off. of cust., B. Guiana, Aug.-Sept., 1887; asst. record clk., P.W.D., Oct., 1887; asst. to clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.) P.W.D., New Amsterdam, May, 1888; 5th cls. offr., P.W.D., Apr., 1892; ag. clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.) Berbice and distcts., Feb. to Aug., 1893; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1895; clk., P.O. savings bank, Jan., 1899; 4th cls. clk., G.P.O., Jan., 1900; 3rd cls. clk., treasy., Georgetown, Jan., 1901; ag. cashier, June, 1904, to July, 1905, and May to Nov., 1908.

THORNTON, George.—M.D. (Edin.); M.R.C.P.(Lond.); D.P.H.(Oxford); house physcn., Edin. Royal Infir., 1890; house surg., Metropolitan hosp., Lond.; house surg., children's hosp., Hull; asst. med. offr., Tooting Fever hosp., 1893-99; civil surg. to H.M. forces in S. Africa; med. supt., Pretoria hosp., 1900-1908; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Nov., 1908.

THORNTON, Swinfords Leslie, B.A.—B.1853; Lincoln Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; admitted to the bar, S. Sttlmtns., Nov., 1886; regis., sup. ct.; comnrs., ct. of requests, and comr. of stamps, Malacca, June, 1887; ag. sen. mag., Singapore, July, 1888; regis., sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, July, 1892; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1894; ag. admistr., Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1895; regis., mag., Jamaica, 1896; mem. comttee. to revise rules and form rea. mag. cts., 1897; mem. parochial bds. comsn.; and chrmn. house and land tax comttee., 1899; ag. puisne judge, 1901; puisne judge, S. Sttlmtns., 22nd Mar., 1904; sen. puisne judge, Aug., 1906.

THORPE, Wm. Edward.—Offr., cls. III., Ceylon civ. ser.; ed. St. Edmund's schl., Canterbury; cadet, Ceylon, 1891; office asst., Anuradhapura, 1892; ditto, Jaffna, 1894; ditto, Ratnapura, 1896; ditto, Colombo, 1896, with additional duties as asst. supt. pol.; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1898; ditto, Chilaw, 1899; dist. judge, Matara, 1900; pol. mag., Colombo, 1903; dep. coll. cust., Colombo, 1905; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1908; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1908.

TRELFAUL, Wm. Herbert.—B. 1875; apptd., after open compet., exam., to controller's off., London postal service, Apr., 1888; supt. of registn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1905; ag. supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1905; supt. of registn., ditto, Sept., 1906; supt. of mails, Penang, Sept., 1907.

TIBBITS, Arthur Charles Kent.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmr. in 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905; and from June, 1906, to Mar., 1907; postmr. of Antigua, and federal postmr. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910.

TIBBITS, Elwood D'Arcy.—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1899 to Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., 1st Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 8th July, 1901; ag. 3rd clk., 29th Aug., 1901, to 1st June, 1902, and 5th June, 1902, to 5th Mar., 1903; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 17th Oct., 1903, to 5th May, 1904; ag. 3rd clk., 24th Mar. to 1st Aug., 1905; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk., to exec. coun., Dominica, 2nd Aug., 1905, to 5th Apr., 1906; ag. 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, and clk. to exec., Antigua, from 6th Apr., 1906; 3rd clk., col. off., Antigua, 23rd Sept., 1906, to 25th 1907; ag. 2nd clk., ditto, and clk. to exec. and supt. of govt. printing off., from 5th 1907, to 8th Mar., 1908; ag. ast. col. sec., 13th Nov. to 11th Dec., 1907, and from 22nd Jan., 1908; ag. 2nd clk., 20th Apr., 19th Oct., 1908; 19th April, to 24th Nov., 7th Feb. to 29th July, 1911; and from 7th July to 20th Aug., 1911; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov. from 2nd to 11th Jan., 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 18th Sept., 1911, to Jan., 1912.

TIBBITS, Richard White Longmuir—1846; ed. at high schl., Quebec and Lennoxville, formerly coroner, J.P., and sec. to Victoria, New Brunswick; elec. to legis. N.B., 1882; dep. prov. sec., N.B., 1883; printer, 1895.
TICE, W. G.—B. 1867; apptd., after 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 19th Feb., 1900; 2nd Feb., 1902.
TINNEY, Louis Herbert John, B.B. 1873; apptd. dep. harbrmr., Georgetown, Guiana, Apr., 1904; ag. harbrmr., June, 1905, and May to Nov., 1907; R.H.S. silver medal for saving life at sea.
TOBIAS, M.—Ck., stattn. branch, Cape Town, Dec., 1901; 2nd clk., res. off., O.R.C., May, 1902; examr. of acct., O.R.C., Aug., 1906.

TODD, John Spencer Brydges, C.M.G.—B. 1840; jun. clk., col. sec.'s office, Cap., 1860; 2nd clk. to C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, Dec., 1862; 2nd cl. clk. to C.C., R.M., Nov., 1864; 1st clk. to C.C., Swellendam, May, 1867, to Aug., 1874, but was R.M., Swellendam, June, 1870, to Jan., 1874; again during Sept. and Oct., 1874; corresp. clk. to the rly. engrnr. of the com., 1874, to Mar., 1875; clk. in charge of mints and stamps, G.P.O., Capetown, Apr., 1875; employed on special service as sec. to govt. comsn. investigating the accounts of the col. treasy-chest, (Cap.) May to Dec., 1875; was exec. comsn. the col. at the Paris Exhibition of 1878; col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1876; in June, served with Sir H. White and Mr. Lawson, and Sir W. B. Gordon (Treasury), on comsn. to investigate the expenditure incurred by the Impl. and col. govts. in the Transkei, 1878; proceeded in June, 1881, on spec. to Kimberley, with a view to adjusting the prov. of Griqualand W.; acted as regis. of deeds at Kimberley from Dec., 1881; and as ast. comsnr. of crown and pub. wks. in Capetown from Jan., 1882; sec. and acct. to the agt-gen. col. in London, Oct., 1882; ag. 1895-6; ret., 1906.

TOLMIE, Roderick Finlayson.—B. 1854; coll. schl., Victoria, British Columbia; bank of B. Columbia (Cariboo agency) B.C.; govt. serv., 1877; gen. sec. B.C. owners' assoc., 1899; re-entd. govt. serv., min. of mines, 1902.

TOMALIN, Herbert Frederic.—B. 1854; M.I.C.E., F.R.I. Br. Architects, and F.C.S., Northampton Grm. schl.; astt. to C.E. L. and N.W. rlyw. wks., 1880-6; dist. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886-90; reported on lighthouse sites, southern coast of Ceylon; dist. engrnr. Colombo harb., foreman. drainage wks., 1887-8 and 1890-2; 2nd finan. and office astt. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1890-6; designed and constructed post and telegr. bldgns., Colombo, 1892-5; dist. engnr., Colombio, 1893-6; dist. engnr., scheme for water supply to the town of Jaffna, Nov., 1896, to Apr., 1897; engnr., N.W. Prov. and E. Prov., Aug., 1899, and scheme for a water supply to the town of Trincomalee and to the naval and military cantonment; prov. engnr., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1903; ditto, W. Prov., Mar., 1904; astt. D.P.W., Jan., 1906; prov. engnr., Cent. Prov., Sept., 1905; ditto, S. Prov., 31st Mar., 1908.

TOMKINS, STANLEY C., C.M.G. (1900).—Apptd. by F.O. as astt. dist. offr., Uganda, Feb., 1896, and ch. offr., Uganda rides, 26th Apr., 1897; served during mutiny in Uganda, 1897-8 (ment. in desp., medal with two clasps); dist. offr. in charge of Kavirondo, Aug., 1897; ag. sub-comsrr., in charge of Uganda Kingdom, May, 1900; jud. offr., Uganda Port, 21st Mar., 1900; ag. sub-comsrr., W. Prov., May, 1902; sess. judge, May, 1903; sub-comsrr., Sept., 1904; in charge of Uganda Kingdom, Dec., 1904; ag. dep. comsrr., 19th May, 1906, to 20th Feb., 1907; in charge of Uganda Kingdom, 4th Dec., 1907, to 28th Sept., 1908; ag. dep. comsrr., 29th Sept., 1908; ag. gov., 29th Sept. to 19th Oct., 1908; ag. chief sec. to gov., 1st Nov., 1908; ag. gov., Uganda, 1909; retired, 1911.
TONNET, LOUIS ARTHUR.—Joined Mauritius police, 16th Feb., 1885; transf'd. to Seychelles, 1893; sub-inspr., 1st Mar., 1902; ag. inspr. since 8th May, 1907; a visiting magistrate for outlying islands.

TOOKER, H. P.—Ed. at Queen's Coll., Cork, Irel.; grad. bach. of engnr. at Queen's Univ. (now Royal Univ. of Irel.), 1879; artic. to borough engnr., Salford, 1880; apptd. astt. engnr. to munl. coun., Birkenhead, 1883; apptd. exec. engnr., p.w. dept., H. Kong, 1890; ag. dir. p.w., May to Aug., 1894, and again in 1900; ag. astt. D.P.W., 1900; 2nd astt. D.P.W., Apr., 1911; apptd. assoc. mem. inst. civ. engnrs., Dec., 1892; mem. of soc. arts, Feb., 1901.

TOPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Diocesan schl., Waterford, Ireland; served as book keeper and acctnt. with W. Australian land co. (Great Southern rly.), 1887 to 1897; on taking over of line entd. gov't. ser. as sub-acctnt., rly. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; astt. acctnt., 1st July, 1900; ag. ch. acctnt., 1st July, 1903; aud.-gen. for State of W. Aust., Feb., 1904; also dep. aud.-gen. for Commonwealth, Feb., 1904, to Jan., 1906.

TORONTO, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. J. F. SWEENEY, D.D.—B. 1857; ed. at McGill Coll., Montreal, and Trin. Coll., Toronto; ordained, 1880; rector of St. Luke's, Montreal, 1880-82; hon. Canon, Toronto, 1889-1905; archdeacon of Simcoe, 1905; rector of St. Philip's, Toronto, 1882-1909; archdeacon of York, Toronto, 1906; bishop of Toronto, 1909.

TOTHILL, FRANCIS JAMES.—Extra astt. to postmtr.-gen., Ceylon, 28th Oct., 1898; astt. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; astt. sec., cent. irrigtn. bd., 15th Jan., 1900; sec., ditto, and office astt. to dir. of irrigtn., 15th May, 1900; astt. acctnt., gen. treasry., Jan., 1907.

TOUGH, JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin; served 11 years in 42nd Highrs.; 1st cl. cert. of educn., 1896; cert. of mil. eng., 1898; Hythe cert. of musk. (distinguished) 1904; mounted infantry cert., 1905; served in South African War, 1899-1902; Queen's medal and 3 clasps; King's medal and 2 clasps; severely wounded at Magersfontein; sub.-inspr. of pol. and drill instr., St. Kitts, 1907; J.P., St. Kitts, 1907; ag. inspr. of pol. and adjt. of local forces, 1908; chmn. of tel. bd., 1908; estab. helio. commun. between St. Kitts and Nevis, 1909; sub.-inspr. of pol., mil. instr., inspr. of roads and works, inspr. of weights and meas., Montserrat, 1909; J.P., Montserrat, 1909; mem. bd. of health, 1910; sub.-inspr. of pol. and mil. instr., Antigua, 1910; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Is.

OWNER, H. V.—Mem. San. Inst., Assoc. mem. C.E.A.; astt. supt. of wks., S. Stilimts., Mar., 1901; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1904; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, Apr. to Sept., 1902, and from Nov., 1904, to Mar., 1908.
TOWNSEND, ALFRED ERNEST.—Ed. at Brisbane gram. schl.; astt. survr., G. Coast survey, 1902-1905; survr., E. Africa Prot., 24th Aug., 1905.
TOWNSEND, W. H. M.—Ch. offr., Uganda cly. lake steamers, June, 1903; comdr., July, 1905.

TOWNSEND, WM. RICHARD.—B.A., Dublin Univ., 1894; called to the bar, Ireland, 1894; atty.-gen., Gambia, 1st May, 1902; inspr. of schls., col. regiar. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns. during tenure of atty.-generalship; ch. mag. and M.L.C., Gambia, 6th Nov., 1906.

TOWNSHEND, SIR CHARLES JAMES, Kt.-Bach. (1911).—B. 1844; ed. Collegiate sch. and King's Coll., Windsor, Nova Scotia; B.A., B.C.L., D.C.L.; called to bar, 1866; Q.C., 1881; elec. to legis. assem., 1878 and 1882; H. of C., 1884; just. of the sup. ct. of N.S., 1887; ch. just. of N.S., 1907.
TOWNSON, HARRY WALLS, M.R.C.V.S.—Ch. inspr. of stock, Falkland Is., May, 1910.
TRAILL, FRANCIS STUART FORBES.—Astt. collr., E. Africa Prot., 10th Oct., 1898; collr., 1st Apr., 1903.

TRATMAN, DAVID WM.—Ed. at Clifton Coll., achlr. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet., Hong Kong, Dec., 1904; passed cadet., 1907; ag. astt. land offr. in New Territories, and regiar. of land etc., Mar., 1907; mem. of land etc., June, 1907; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, July, 1907; ag. astt. dist. offr., May, 1909; 2nd astt. regiar.-gen., Sept., 1909; ag. supt. of imports and exports, May, 1910; ag. dist. offr., N.T., July, 1911.

TRAVERS, A. K.—3rd elk., col. sec.'s office, Hong Kong, Oct., 1874; priv. sec. to Admstr. Austin, Mar. to Apr., 1877, and to Gov. Hennessey, Apr. to July, 1877; ag. 2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, July to Nov., 1877; 2nd elk., Dec., 1881, to May, 1883; priv. sec. to Admstr. Marsh, Apr., 1882, to Apr., 1883; astt. P.M.G., May, 1883; J.P., Dec., 1883; ag. P.M.G. and collr. of stamp rev., June, 1888.

TRAVERS, BENJAMIN.—B. 1849; dist. mag., Br. Honduras, June, 1882; excise offr., judge of civil juris., also coroner, ag. priv. sec. to gov'r., and elk. to exec. and legis. couns., 1886; undertook a journey over unexplored territory to bring into the colony Indian refugees from Guatemala, 1884; conferred with dissatisfied Indian chief, Gen. Tamay, on question of border delimit. and established amicable relations, 1887; comanrr., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1892; acted as local comanrr. of pol. in conjunction with own duties, July, 1898, to Oct., 1899, and at various other times.

TREACHER, SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1890), M.A. (Oxford).—B. 1849; ag. pol. ag., Labuan, Nov., 1871, to June, 1872; ag. col. sec. and audr., Oct., 1873; col. sec., audr., and pol. mag., Apr., 1877; admstd. the govt., Dec., 1876, to Feb., 1877; and Oct., 1877, to Apr., 1880; mem. of the legis coun., Dec., 1874. During the periods he admstd. the govt. he acted as consul-gen. in Borneo, and visited Sulu and N. Borneo in connection with Spanish claims; apptd. (1881) the first gov. of Br. N. Borneo, to Dec., 1887; again acted as admstr. of Labuan and consul-gen. in Borneo, from Feb., 1884, to Nov., 1885, and was instrumental in saving Brunei from an attack of the Limbang rebels; sec. to Perak, June, 1888; ag. res., Sept., 1888; res. Selangor, July, 1892; res., Perak, July, 1896; res.-gen., Fed. Malay States, Sept., 1901; ret., 1905.
TREFFLE, HON. J. L.—M.L.A., N.S. Wales, 1907; hon. min. and asst. min. of agricul., Oct., 1910.

TRENHOLME, HON. NORMAN W.—B. 1837; ed. McGill univ., B.A. and Chapman medal, 1863; B.C.L. and gold medal, 1865; D.C.L., 1887; prof., faculty of law, McGill univ., 1883-1888; dean, 1888-1896; K.C. and bottomer of the bar, Montreal, 1888-1889; counsel for Quebec in arbitn. between Ontario and Quebec and the Dominion, 1897-1901; judge, super. ct., Montreal, 1901-1904, and of K.B. and appeal ct., since June, 1904.
TRESIDDER, CAPT. TOLMIE JOHN, R.E., C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1850; was engnr. for Malta drainage wks.
TRICKETT, W. J., M.L.C.—Postmr.-gen., N.S. Wales, May, 1883; min. of instruction, May, 1884, to Nov., 1885; chmn. of comtees. and dep. pres. of legis. coun., June, 1900.
TRINIDAD, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. JOHN FRANCIS WELSH.—Ed. at Christ Church, Oxford, B.A. (1881), M.A. (1886); D.D., Oxford Univ., 1904; consec. Bishop of Trinidad of 28th Oct., 1904.

TROTTER, J. F.—Asst. surv., great trigonometrical survey of India, for seven years; ensign W.I. Regt., 1870; lieut., 1871; 2nd sub-imigr. agt. of Br. Guiana, Feb., 1872; sen. sub-imigr. agt., Nov., 1872; in charge of imigrn. dept., Feb. to Sept., 1873; and ag. imigrn. agt.-gen. on several occasions; prot. of imigrn., Mauritius, Apr., 1881; special imigrn. commr. to India, May, 1883, to Apr., 1884, and May to Dec., 1889.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schls., Toronto, Ontario; asst. clk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coalcoms., 1907; sec., Alberta pork comsn., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and regist. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

TROWELL, WM. JOHN.—B. 1864; M.I.N.A.; engnr. and shipwright survr. and examnr. of engns., Liverpool, Feb., 1898; inspr. under bd. of agric., Dec., 1898; inspr. of marine surveys, S. Sttlmts., 16th Jan., 1903; on special ser. in England in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., July to Nov., 1905.

TRUMP, JOHN, M.I.C.E.—B. 1858; entd. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1878; in charge of convicts on Colombo breakwater construction, 1880 to 1883; services lent to native states, S. Sttlmts., 1883 to 1886; transf. to P.W.D., Perak, 1886; dep. state engrn., Perak, Feb., 1887; state engrn., May, 1901; ag. dir. of P.W., F.M.S., Apr., 1904, to Jan., 1906.

TUBOKU-METZGER, ALBERT EMERIC, B.A.—Ed. in C.M.S. Gram. schl. and Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone; gained Fourah Bay Coll. foundation scholarship, Jan., 1877; grad. in Arts, June, 1880; first cls. hons in theology, Durham Univ., Dec., 1881; jun. cls. tutor, Fourah Bay Coll., 1882; entd. S. Leone civ. serv. as extra clk., secret. and treasry., 1st Mar., 1885; govt.'s office, 1886; col.-treasry., 1887; served as finan. clk. in Yonnii expdn. under Sir Samuel Rowe, 1887; clk., regar.-gen.'s dept., 1890; regis. births and deaths, Freetown dist., 1890; pub. clk. and clk. of ct. of requesta, Freetown, Apr., 1895; ag. regar.-gen., 1904 to 1905, and asst. dist. commr., 1st May, 1908; apptd. crown prosecutor in the Sept. sessions of the sup. ct., Sherbro, Oct., 1908; is a J.P. for the col.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool College, Insttn. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; Ist cls. 3 years certif. exam.; parchment certif.; head master, govt. boys' schl., St. Helena, 1905; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; sec., govt. Lace schl., 1908; supervisor of schla., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911.

TUCKETT, GEORGE HENRY.—Imperial service, 1891; postmaster, B.C.A. Prot., asst. agt. and P.M.R., Chinde, 1898; ag. service-consul, Chinde, 1899; ag. P.M.G., B.C.A. Prot., 1900-1; ditto, Nyassaland, 1910; master and acctnt. to armed forces, Nyassaland, 1910; deputy P.M.G., Nyassaland, 1911.

TUDOR, DANIEL THOMAS, K.C.—B. called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1890; dr.; also practised on West. Circ.; atty. Grenada and St. Vincent, July, 1903; both colonies, May, 1908; acted as chief also as col. sec. of Grenada on several occasions; admstd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1907, to 1908; and govt. of Grenada, May and June, and Mar. to May, 1910; commr. to consolidate revise the Laws of Grenada, 1911; ag. legal C.O., May to Oct., 1911; chief justice, same Nov., 1911.
TUDOR, HON. FRANK GWINNE.—B. M.P. for Yarra, Victoria; pres. of Melb. trades hall coun., 1900-1901; elec. to H. of R., C. of A., 1901; min. of trade and C. of A., Apr., 1910.

TUPPER, THE RIGHT HON. SIR C. (creat. 1888), P.C. (1907), G.C.M.G., K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B. (1867), M.D., L.R. Edin. (1843).—Born 2nd July, 1821; is an D.C.L. of Acadia Coll., Nova Scotia; gov. of Dalhousie Coll., Halifax (appd. of Parlmt., 1862); pres. of the Canadian assoc. from its formation, 1867, until mem. of exec. coun., and prov. sec. N.S., 1860; and from 1863 to June, 1867; prime min. prov. from 1864 until he ret. from his govt. on the Union Act coming into force July, 1867; deleg. to England on public from N.S. govt., 1858 and 1865; from the De- govt., Mar., 1868; leader of the deleg. from the union confce. at Charlottetown, 1864; in Quebec in same year, and to final col. in London, to complete terms of union, 1867; patent of rank and precedence from Her Majesty Queen Victoria as an exec. coun., N.S.; the P.C., June, 1870, and pres. of that body 1st July, 1872, when apptd. min. of int. rel. of customs, Feb., 1873; resig. office with Sir Macdonald, 5th Nov., 1873; min. of pub. wks., 1878; min. of rly. and canals, 1879; repre. Cumberland in parlmt. for 29 years in N.S. from 1856 until confederation in 1867, and Commons from that year until he resig. his cabinet and was apptd. high commr. for Canada in London, May 24th, 1884; acted as exec. or Dominion at the Antwerp Exhibn., 1885; and Col. and Ind. Exhibn., London, 1886; resumed office in Canadian ministry as finance min., 1887; ne of H.M.'s plenipotentiaries at fisheries conferece at Washington, 1887; re-apptd. high commnr., May, 1888; joint plenip., with the Marquis of Dufferin and Sir J. A. Crowe, for negotiating a commercial arrangement between Canada and France, 1892; of S. for Canada, Jan., 1896; premier, Mar. to June, 1896; leader of opposition in H. C. of, 1896-1900.

TUPPER, HON. SIR CHARLES HIBBERT, K.C.M.G. (1833).—B. 1855; son of Sir C. Tupper, mem. of S. bar; grad. Harvard law schl.; mem., house of commons, Canada, 1882-1900; min. of marine and fisheries, 1888-1890; deleg. to Washington in connection with Behring Sea difficulty, 1890; Br. gt. for the arbitration with the U.S. on the ehring Sea question, 1892-3; min. of just., 1895; sig. 1896; mem. of priv. coun. for Canada.
TURGEON, WILHELM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and avul univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of uk., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; elec., 1908.
TURLAY, HON. HENRY.—B. 1859; M.L.A., Queensland, 1893-1902; elected to the Senate, C. A., 1903; pres. of Senate, July, 1910.

TURNER, THE RT. HON. SIR GEORGE, P.C., C.M.G. (1897), LL.D.—B. 1851; premier and eas. of Victoria, 1894-9 and 1900-1; represented col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee 1897; pres. of conf. of premiers at Melbourne, 1899; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as assessor, Jan., 1901; treas., in 2nd (Deakin) ministry; treas. in 4th (Reid-Turner) coalition ministry, 1904; retired from politics, 1906.

TURNER, GEORGE.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., D.P.H. (Cantab.); lecturer on hygiene, Guy's hosp., 1882-1895; examnr. for P.H., roy. coll. of phys. and surgs., Lond., 190-1895; employed by L.G.B. to report upon phthisis, 1884; and by L.C.C. to inquire into idemies of enteric fever and the sanitation of ver Lea, 1894-95; med. offr. of health, Cape, 95-1900; mem. of Cape med. coun., 1898-1900; of govt. rinderpest experimental statn., Kimberley, 1897-98; med. offr. of health, Transvaal, 1st Aug., 1900; M.L.C.; census commnr., annual, 1904; del. for Transvaal and O.R.C., ternat. Leprosy. cont., Bergen, 1909.

TURNER, JAMES POPSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regist.'s off., St. tte, 1897; comnr. to administer oaths, 1898, 95-1900; mem. of Cape med. coun., 1898-1900; of govt. rinderpest experimental statn., Kimberley, 1897-98; med. offr. of health, Transvaal, 1st Aug., 1900; M.L.C.; census commnr., annual, 1904; del. for Transvaal and O.R.C., ternat. Leprosy. cont., Bergen, 1909.

TURNER, WILLIAM.—Ed. at Abdn. and Edin. wra.; M.A. (Abdn.), 1876; M.B. Edin., 1879; p., 1881; res. phys., roy. hosp. for sick chldrn., 1879; non-res. phys., roy. infirmy., Edin.; res. accoucheur, Glasgow maternity hosp.; mem. roy. med. soc., Edin., fell obstetrical Edin.; ast. surg. civil hosp., Gibraltar; also surg. civ. prison, med. offr. lunatic, and dist. med offr., 1882; pub. vaccinator, urg., 1888; surg. to col. hosp., Oct., 1889.

TUTCHER, WM. JAMES, F.L.S.—B. 1867; at Merchant Venturers' schl., Bristol; ed Royal Gardens, Kew, 1888; ast. supt., botan. and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1891; astg. supt. in 1893, 1894, 1897, 1900 to 1901, 1902 to 1903, 1905, 1906, 1907, 1908 and 1909; supt., 1910; J.P., 1911; late hon. lecturer on botany, coll. of med. for Chinese author of "Gardening for Hong Kong."

TWEEDIE, HON. LEMUEL J., K.C., LL.D.—B. 1849; ed. at Presbyterian Coll.; admitted to bar, New Brunswick, 1871; mem. of legis., 1874-1878, 1886-1907; has represented Northumberland ever since, except during 1878 to 1882; surr. gen., 1890; prov. sec., 1896; premier, 1900; represented N.B. at the King's Coronation, 1902; holds Canadian Militia long service medal; lieut.-gov. of New Brunswick, 2nd Mch., 1907.
TYLER, JAMES ERNEST.—Govt. printer, S. Stltmtns., 1st July, 1905.

TYRELL, FRANCIS GRAEME.—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admtrg. the gov't., Apr., 1902; off. ast. to gov't. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; ag. ast. gov't. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1904; seconded supt. of pol., N.W. prov., Aug., 1905, cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; ast. gov't. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909.

TYSER, SIR C. R., KT. BACH. (1909).—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In Tem., 1873; ast. ag. judic. commnr. and leg. advsr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leeawards, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1895; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 1906; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evkaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejelle, 1901.

TYSEN, FRANCIS DASHWOOD.—Ed. at Melbourne Church of England Gram. schl., and Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut. 2nd Scottish Horse, S. African War; inspr. of pol., E. Africa Prot., May, 1905; ast. dist. supt. of pol., 1st July, 1906; supt. of pol., 1910.

UNIACKE, CAPT. ANDREW GORE, D.S.O.—Served with N.W. mtd. pol., Canada (including Yukon expdn.), 1893-1898; served in S. African war, with Canadian forces and M.I., 1899-1902 (2 medals, 8 clasps, D.S.O.); dist. supt., N. Nigeria police, 1903; served with expdn. against Okpotos, 1903-4 (W. African medal and clasp); commnr. and ag. dep. inspr.-gen., N. Nigeria police, 1911.

UNIACKE, G. L.—Served as trooper in mtd. pol., W. Australia, 1895 to 1899; private in 2nd contingent W. Austn. M.I., S. African war, 1899 to 1901 (Queen's medal and 5 clasps); reed. commnr. in 4th Batt. King's Own (R. Lancaster Regt.), 1902; seconded for serv. with N. Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 6th Sept., 1902; served in the Kano-Sokoto campaign (medal with clasp), 1903; company commdr., N. Nigeria regt., 1908; noted for brevet-majority, 1909; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1910, with astp. dated 6th Sept., 1910; still holds commnr. as lieut. in 3rd Batt. King's Own (R. Lancs Regt.), special reserve.

URICH, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.M.Z.S., F.E.S.—Ed. in Trinidad, Real Gymnasium, Coblenz, and Geneva Univ.; ledger keeper, govt. printing dept., Trinidad, Jan., 1895; acctnt., rly. extensions, Aug., 1895; 2nd clk., educ. dept., Jan., 1899; store-keeper, P.W.D., Mar., 1901; acctnt., educ. dept., Sept., 1903; also clk. to coll. coun., Oct., 1906; ch. clk., educ. dept., and ast. inspr. of schls., Dec., 1906; mem. of bd. of management and hon. sec. to Victoria Inst., 1894 to 1895, and 1899 to 1901; ag. sec. to agric. soc'y., May to June, 1894; ag. sec. to bd. of educn., Feb. to Apr., 1900; ag. statistical clk. and librarian, col. secretariat, Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. forest offr., Apr. to Oct., 1908, holds a comm. as lieut. in local forces, Trinidad, since 1897; capt. and adjt., T.L.I.V., 1908; seconded to dept. of agric. as entomologist, 1909.
URQUHART, CHARLES FRDERIC ROBERT HILDYARD.—B. 1867; entd. Crown Agent's off., Jan., 1885; supt. of stock transfer off., 1895; reg. of inscribed stocks and head of stock dept., 1905.

USHER, HENRY CHAS., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1838; clk., col. sec'y's office, Honduras, June, 1859; in public treas., July, 1865; provost-marshal, July, 1869; pol. mag., now called dist. mag., Belize dist., Nov., 1878; in charge of the public treas., June, 1886, to July, 1887, and Apr. to June, 1889; and of Belize gaol, May to Aug., 1872; has acted as regsr. of shipping, surv. of shipping, inquisitor of wreck and casualty, and inspir. of pol.; was marshal of the V.A. ct., is ex-officio judge of the summary ct., and also coroner of the col.; commr. of currency under Ord. No. 32 of 1894; ag. col. treas., collr. of cust. and postmr., harbr., and suptd. of mercantile marine on many occasions from 1883 to 1900; cr. officio regsr. of births and deaths, 1885 to 1892; suptd. fire brigade, 1886 to 1895; commr. on special duty to the Cays, Aug. to Sept., 1889; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1890; ret., June, 1903.

UTIDJIAN, H. A. S.—English translator to Turkish Adm'y., and translator and priv. sec. to Admiral Augustus Hobart Pasha, 1874-76; joint treas. and acctnt., T. Squadron, Cretan waters, 1876; dragoman and transr. under Mr. Walter Baring, 2nd sec. to H.B.M.'s Embassy, Constantinople, during sittings of special comm., apptd. to try the authors of the Bulgarian atrocities in 1876-77; 2nd in charge and res. Turkish professor, student dragomans establishment under H.B.M.'s embassy, Constantinople, Jan. to Aug., 1878; translator of state documents, Cyprus, since 21st Aug., 1878; acted as attache to commr., Nicosia, and as asst. collr. of tithes in 1878-1880; Turkish examiner, 1879-1907; Turkish translator to leg. coun., Cyprus, 1883; transr., land registn. comm., 1901; sec. to Moslem secondary educ. bd., 1902-1904; apptd. to examine and report on general state of Turkish instruction in Idadi schl., 1905; 4th cls. Mejidieh and Osmanlieh orders.

VALANTIN, W. ADOLPHE.—Asst. clk. and messenger, Queen's advoc.'s chambers, S. Leone, Jan., 1878; acted as asst. sec. to Br. consuls. on the N.W. Liberian Bdry. Comsn. in 1879; clk. to crown solr., June, 1880; acted as clk. to ch. just., 1882; clk. to Queen's advoc., 1883; dep. sheriff, Mar., 1890; clk. to ch. just., Aug., 1891; dep. coroner, July, 1896; clk. atty.-gen.'s chambers, Dec., 1897; curator, intestate estates, Jan., 1902.

VANDER BEEK, J. N., I.S.O. (1903).—Extra clk., col. sec'y's office, Singapore, Sept., 1871; 8th clk., Jan., 1873, and attached to govt. house; 6th clk., Dec., 1875; guardian of govt. house, May, 1877; 5th clk., col. sec'y's office, Apr., 1879; 4th clk., June, 1879; 3rd clk., June, 1880; clk., govt. house, Dec., 1883; financial asst., pol., 1905.

VAN DER MERWE, JOHANNES LOEWEN.—Served under govt. of late S. A.R. as mining comm. from 1885 till British occupation, 1st Sept., 1894; mining comm'r., Barberton, 1885; ag. spec. lic. drost, Barberton; chrmn., diggers' comm., Barberton; chrmn., Barberton hosp. bd.; mun. comm'r., Johannesburg, 1893; chrmn., Johannesburg hosp. bd.; chrmn., Jameson Raid comm., 1895-6; mem. of comm. to inquire into cause of dynamite explosion at Johannesburg, 1896; mem. of dynamite relief comm.; mem. of Johannesburg liquor comm.; pres., Witwatersrand agr. soc'y.; hon. mem. Witwatersrand chamber of mines; served during Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; prin. clk., townships (mines dept.), Transvaal, 1903; ag. deputy asst. regsr. of mining rights, 1st district regsr. of mining rights, Johannesburg, 1906; mem. of trading bd., Witwatersrand, 1907.

VAN DER MEULEN, FREDERICK A.—B. 1857; ed. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Keble Col., Oxford; B.A. (hons. in mod. hist.), 1896; M.A., 1900; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1900; West circuit; asst. dist. comm'r., Sierra Leone, 1901; solr.-gen., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. and also ag. police and circuit judge on various occasions, 1908-1910.

VAN DE VELDE, MARCEL ARTHUR MAURICE.—Ed. at Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; audit branch of E. and A. dept., 29th May, 1900; asst. audr., E. Africa Prot., 9th Mar., 1903; audr., Uganda Prot., June, 1903; E. Africa June, 1904.

VANE, HENRY G. B.—B. 1861; asst. audr., Perak, Dec., 1884; ag. asst. audr.-gen., S. Ap., 1887; audr., Sungei Ujong and Negri Sembilan, May, 1888; treas., pol. mag., etc., Sungei Ujong, Oct., 1890; audr., Selangor, Dec., 1892; state audr., Perak, Apr., 1895; rev. audr., Perak, Jan., 1900; ag. fin. comm'r., Oct., 1903, to Oct., 1904; ag. fin. audr., cent. audit off., Jan., 1904; ag. finance comm'r., Oct., 1906, to 31st Jan., 1907; treas. F.M.S., 26th Nov., 1906.

VAN EEDEN, WALTER CLARENCE.—Entd. cust. dept., B. Guiana, 1891; lieut., B.G. militia, 1897-1900; Hythe schl. of musk. certif. for musketry and maxim guns, 1899; attached to batt. Dorsetshire regt., and gained certif. of proficiency, 1899; asst. inspr. of constab., G. Coast, 30th June, 1900; Ashanti war, medal and gratuity 1900; offr. condg. dist. of Bole, 1901; lieut., 6 batt. Dorsetshire regt., Aug., 1901; lieut., G. Coast regt., W.A.F.F., 1901; certif. E. 511, sch. of instr., Chelsea barracks, Dec., 1901; on expdt. against the Fra Fras, 1902; offr. commdg., Black Volta dist., 1902; transf. to cust. dept., G. Coast, 3rd Aug., 1903; supervisor in charge, Accra cust. dist., 1904; supervisor in charge, S. dist., W Frontier preventive service, 1906-7; supervisor in charge, Axim cust. dist., 1907, 1908 and 1909; harbrmr., Axim, 1909; attached to Impl. customs, London, during May, 1909; 1st cls. supervisor, 24th Feb., 1909.

VAN SOMEREN, ROBERT ABRAHAM LOGAN.—Ed. at Edin. Univ. and Royal Coll. of Surg. Edin.; M.B., Ch.B.; spec. univ. certif., Edin. trop. diseases, and certif. of London Schl. of Trop. Med.; med. off., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 10th Oct., 1905.
VAN ZIJL, JAMES STRATEN.—Controller, P.O. savings bank, late O.F.S., Sept., 1897; ditto O.R.C., Mar., 1900.

VARDON, JOSEPH.—M.L.C., S. Aust., 1900; comm'r. of pub. wks. and min. of industry, 1894; ch. sec. and min. of industry, 1st Mar. to 28th July, 1905; res. seat in coun., 11th Oct., 1905; returned in Dec., 1906, to Commonwealth

VAUDIN, WILLIAM MARSHALL.—B. 1866; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (M.A.); admt. Ceylon cir. ser., Oct., 1889; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Aug., 1891; fiscal, Cent. Prov., and off. asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, Jan., 1896; asst. collr. of cust., Trincomalie, May, 1898; asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Sept., 1898; dist. judge, Batticaloa, June, 1900; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalie, Feb., 1902; April, 1906; Aug., 1906; Kegalla, May, 1907; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., June, 1906; Oct., 1907; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1909; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1909; ditto, Cent. Prov., 1911.

VAUGHAN, CHARLES STEWART.—B. 1866; ed. Harrow and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (M.A.); admt. Ceylon cir. ser., Oct., 1889; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Aug., 1891; fiscal, Cent. Prov., and off. asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, Jan., 1896; asst. collr. of cust., Trincomalie, May, 1898; asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Sept., 1898; dist. judge, Batticaloa, June, 1900; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalie, Feb., 1902; April, 1906; Aug., 1906; Kegalla, May, 1907; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., June, 1906; Oct., 1907; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1909; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1909; ditto, Cent. Prov., 1911.
VAUGHAN, CRAWFORD.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1905; treas., and comsnr. of crown lands and immigration, 3rd June, 1910.

VAUTIER, JOHN FRANCIS.—B. 1863; clk., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 30th Dec., 1902; acctnt., govt. relief dept., 1st Nov., 1903; examr. of acc'ts, treas., 1st July, 1904; book-keeper to treas., 4th Apr., 1906.
VECCHIO, THOMAS JOSEPH.—Marshal, sup. ct., bankruptcy, etc., and V.-A. ct., Gibraltar 1866; also interp., 1866-76.

VELLA, TOMMASO.—Entd. Malta service, Dec., 1865; clk., May, 1896; dep. collr. of cust., 1892; dep. postmr., 1896; collr. of cust. and aspt. of ports, Sept., 1902; postmr.-gen., May, 1903; mem. of ex. coun. and coun. of govt., 1903.

VILLACOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOTT, M.B. Lond. (hons. in for. and obst. med.) 1895; B.S., 1896; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; F.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901; house surg., res. obstetric and clin. med., Guy's hosp.; asst. curator of museum, St. George's hosp.; civ. surg., S. African field force, 1901 to 1902; capt., S.A. Constab., 6th June, 1902; transfd. to O.R.C. govt. serv. as res. med. offr., National hosp., Bloemfontein, 1st Dec., 1905; fellow of R. Inst. of pub. health; deleg. from O.R.C. to 2nd Internat. Congress on School Hygiene.

VENNING, G.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transfd. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903.
VERNON, J.—Employed in rly. dept., N.S. Wales; chief acctnt. in treas.; auditor gen., N.S.W., and deputy auditor gen. in N.S.W. for Commonwealth of Australia, Nov., 1902.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES.—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oxford; scholar of Balliol, 1895-97; 1st cls. rhods., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schrl.; Craven schrl., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s dept. inland revenue, Nov., 1899; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug. 13th, 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot coman., 1903; mem. of managing comitee of E. I. O.; assist. priv. sec. to Lord Elgin, Jan., 1908; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 16th Apr., 1908; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Denman, gov.-gen. of Australia, 1911.
VERRAN, HON. JOHN.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1901; premier and comsnr. of pub. wks., 3rd June, 1910; also min. of mines, 13th Oct., 1910.
VERTUE, GEORGE ELFORD BANNERMAN.—Apptd. 3rd asst., Nyassaland Prot., Jan., 1901; 2nd grade res., Oct., 1906.

VICKERS, EDWARD.—B. 1842; Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1863; called to the bar, In. Tem., Jan., 1868; advoc. in the high ct., Bombay, Dec., 1868; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1883; acted on several occasions as puisne judge, 1894 to 1904; jun. puisne judge, 1st Apr., 1904.

VICTORIA, RIGHT REV. GERARD HEATH LANDER, Lord Bishop of Victoria, Hong Kong.—B. 1861; ed. at Trin. Coll. and Ridley Hall, Camb., B.A., 1884; M.A., 1888; D.D., 1907; consec. at Lambeth Parish Church, 29th June, 1907; curate of St. Bride's, Liverpool, 1884 to 1888; vicar of St. Benedict's, Liverpool, 1888 to 1896; of Litherland, 1896 to 1905; of St. Cyprian's, Liverpool, 1905 to 1907; chrmn., Litherland schl. bd., 1898 to 1903; chaplain to Liverpool dist. lay readers, 1892 to 1905.

VIGORS, CHARLES THOMAS DOYNE.—Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; asst. collr. of customs, Trincomalee, 1884; ditto, Galle, 1886; ag. off. asst. to govt. agt., S. prov., from Sept., 1886, to Mar., 1888; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Apr., 1888; Tangalla, Dec., 1889; landing survr. cust., Colombo, Nov., 1888, resumed duties, Feb., 1890; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1894; asst. govt. agt., Matare, May, 1894, resumed same, Jan., 1896; Kalutara, May, 1898; govt. agt., Uva, Aug., 1900; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 13th May, 1902; ag. chmn., mun. coun. and mayor of Colombo, May, 1903; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Nov., 1903; govt. agt., N. Cent. Prov., May, 1904; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., June, 1906; govt. agt., N. Cent. Prov., Aug., 1906; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., Dec., 1906; dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1908; prin. collr. of cust., Jan., 1909; ag. postmr.-gen., Sept., 1909; ag. govt. agt. and fiscal, W. Prov., 1909; prin. collr. of customs, Jan., 1911.

VILLIERS, FRANCIS JOHN, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1851; entered the C.O., Sept., 1869; priv. sec. to the admstr. of Griqualand W., Oct., 1875, and clk. of the coun.; ag. col. sec., May, 1877, to Oct., 1880; mem. of legis. coun., 1879; engaged on special service in connection with the annexation of the prov. to the Cape Col. from Nov., 1880, to May, 1881; audr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882; ag. govt. sec., at various times between 1883 and 1896; ret., 1898.

VIRET, ALFRED PERCHVAL.—B. 1865; rev. offr., Dominica, May, 1882; sec. bds. of health and quarantine, Aug., 1884; sec. to local comtee., Ind. and Col. Exhib., 1886; confill. clk. to pres., and clk. exec. coun., May, 1886; govt. offr., treas. and quarantine offr., June, 1891; 1st cl. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1897; asst. col. treas., S. Leone, July, 1899; acted as col. treas., S. Leone, from July to Oct., 1899, and from Jan. to Sept., 1901; acted as col. P.M.G., from June to July, 1900, and from Oct. to Mar., 1902; apptd. collector of customs, 3rd Nov., 1902; ag. col. treas., Nov., 1902, to Apr., 1903, and from May to Aug., 1904; selected in May, 1905, to carry out transfer of the Isles-le-Los to France, under Anglo-French convention of 1904.
VITALIS, NICHOLAS.—Ch. interp. and clk. to coman., Larnaka, Cyprus, July, 1878; ditto, Nicosia, Mar., 1879; clk. and interp. to Queen's advoc., Feb., 1882; regisr., supreme ct., Cyprus, 1905.
WADDELL, HON. THOMAS.—B. 1854; M.L.A. for various districts in N.S.W. since 1887 (with only short interval); col. treas., 1901-4; premier, 1904; col. treas., 1907.

WADE, HON. CHAS. GREGORY, K.C.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1886; Crown proscr., New S. Wales, 1891; proscr. for West. circuit and cent. crim. ct., 1894, and resigned, 1902; ag. dist. ct. judge and chmn. of sess. on various occasions; M.L.A. for Willoughby, New S. Wales, 1903; M.L.A. for Gordon, 1904; atty.-gen. and min. for just., Aug., 1904; premier of N.S.W., 1907-1910.

WAGNER, WILLIAM QUINN.—B. 1849; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Town; sec. to the Krigsraad under pres. Burgess, in the first Secooceni war, 1876; filled various ag. appts. in Landdrost's off., Rustenburg, subsequently becoming postmaster and town clk. of that place; served in various capacities under O.F.S. govt., 1881 to 1900; mine inspr., Jagersfontein, 1891 to 1900; supt., refugee camp, Heilbron, O.R.C., May, 1901, to Mar. 1902; joined mines dept., Transvaal, May, 1902.

WAIT, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; addl. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and adml. comsrr. of requests and pol. mag., Mannar and Puttalum during pearl fishery, Feb., 1905; asst. land stltnt. offr., May, 1905; lieut., Ceylon Planters' Rifle Corps.

WAKEMAN, GEORGE HERBERT.—Ed. Bradfield Coll., Berks, and Camb.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, July, 1891; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, June, 1900; J.P., Dec., 1900; ag. dep. land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Apr. to Dec., 1901; ag. land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Jan. to Nov., 1902, and Jan. to Aug., 1905; comsrr. for oaths, sup. ct., Hong Kong, and comsrr. for taking ackmnts. of married women, Aug., 1905; land offr. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Aug., 1905; ag. regisr. of trade marks and patents, Aug., 1910; ag. regisr. of sup. ct., offr. admstr., offr. trustee and regisr. of companies, Sept., 1910, to Aug., 1911.

WALCOTT, HENRY BARCLAY.—B. 1866 excise and customs offr., St. Lucia, 1884; treasy. acctnt., 1890; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1891; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., St. Vincent, 1892; ag. treasy. acctnt., Grenada, 1892; ch. clk., audit office, Dec., 1893; ag. postmtr., 1895-6; ag. ch. of pol. and excise, 1897; 2nd audit clk., col. sec.'s off., Br. Honduras, 1897; auditor, Feb., 1898; ch. asst. treaser., Gold Coast, Jan., 1900; ag. treasy. and mem. ex. and legia. couns., dir., pub. offr.'s guarantee fund, mem. bd. of educn., July, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and May to Oct., 1902; ag. comsrr. of police and supt. of prisons, 1901-1902; pres. Accra town coun., 1901-1902; treas. and collr. of cust., regisr. of shipping, pres., quarantine bd., chmn. poor relief comtee, mem. of bd. of educn., mem. ex. and leg. couns., Br. Hond., Mar., 1903; J.P., Apr., 1903; ag. col. sec., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905, 10th Nov., 1905, to 12th Aug., 1906, and from 8th May, 1908; collr. of cust. and detaining offr., M.S. Act, Trinidad, 1909.

WALKER, HENRY.—2nd asst. land dept., Br. N. Borneo, Jan., 1883; asst. supt., lands and pub. wks., Kudat, July, 1883; comsrr., land, and recorder of lands, May, 1884; mem. coun., Oct., 1883; ag. supl., P.W. dept., Jan., 1887.
WALKER, HOPSON PUCKNEY, B.A.—Jesus Coll., Camb., 1883; LL.B. (Lond.), M.A. (Melb.); barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.), 1888; ch. clk., sup. ct., Melbourne, Dec., 1883.

WALKER, JAMES HUTCHINSON.—Ed. A. Univ., M.A. (nat. sci. hon.), 1876; M.B. (highest hon.), Murray scholar, and gold med. 1878; M.D. 1884; asst. prof., botany, Abdn. prin. med. offr., N. Borneo, Aug., 1881.

WALKER, JOHN C.—4th batt. Yorkshire regt., Coorga Nagpore rifles; served with Yorks. regt., S. Africa (two medals with 5 clasps); asst. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1905; mentions despatches, 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1907.

WALKER, LT.-COL. R.S. FROWD, C.M.G.—B. 1850; entered Sandhurst, 1869; ensign, foot, 1871; capt., June, 1881; hon. lieut.-col., A.D.C. to gov., S.S., 1878-9; attached to armed pol., Mar., 1879; dep. commr. of the force, Feb., 1880, and in command from Nov., 1880; on various occasions as pol. mag., asst. res. and sec. to gov.; has received the thanks of several times for his action in dealing with cain and coolie disturbances; in command of expednry force, 1892 and 1894; ag. sec. to Perak, Oct., 1894, to Aug., 1895; organised States Guides, 1896; and since commr. regt.; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1896; Jan., 1900; ditto, Perak, Apr., 1900, to May, 1901; ret. 1910.
WALKER, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A.—Appd. 3rd class clk., govt. genl.'s office, 1887; promoted to 2nd cls., 1892; 1st cls. ch. clk., 1908; chf. clk., dept. of external affairs, 1909.

WALL, JAMES HORNE DARRELL.—R. clk. and storekeeper, Holberton Inst., 1878; Nov., 1891; shortld. reporter to W. Ind. Coms., 1897; aud. def. force sects.; ag. aud. gen., Leeward and fedrl. accnt., 1899; also clk. to trade and income tax rec., Sept., 1899; ch. clk. to aud. gen. and fedrl. accnt., May, 1900; ag. treas. of Antigua and fedrl. May-Oct., 1909, and Aug., 1910.
WALLACE, LAWRENCE AUBREY.—1910.—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, May, 1907; ag. admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909.

WALLACE, SIR WM., K.C.M.G., C.M.G. (1897).—Senior ex. offr., Roy. Navy, 1886; later as agt.-gen., until 1900, when territs. were handed over to H.M. govt.; numerous treaties with native potentates with the Sultan of Sokoto, 1894; commr. served with over 30 expdtions.; deputy high and senior res., N. Nigeria, 1900; admstr. May to Dec., 1901, May to Nov., 1903, Dec., 1905, and July, 1906, to Apr., 1907; African medals, 5 clasps, coronation; received Murchison medal from R.G.S.; important services rendered to geography; many years' residence in Nigeria; ret. 1910.
WALLACE, WILLIAM HENRY.—Edser., 1882; sec. of mines, Tasmania, 1888.

WALLACE, WILLIAM THOMAS EWART.—4th batt., The King's regt.; S. Africa, Feb., 1900, to Sept., 1902; supt. of telegr. S. Nigeria, 1903-5; asst. supt. of telegr. Africa and Uganda Prots., 21st Dec., 1905.
WALLER, DESMOND DALMULPY.—Asst., E. Africa Prot., 12th May, 1895; asst., 23rd Sept., 1899; offr.-in-charge migtn., Nov., 1902; prot. of imigtn., 1904.
WALLIS, CAPT. C. BRAITHWAITE, F.R.G.S.—Ed. privately and at M. Oxford; 2nd lieut., 2nd Manchester regt.; lieut., 1895; capt., 1897; appd. to frontier force, Jan., 1898; ag. dist.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898; treas.-gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald.

WALTON, GEORGE LAIRD, M.I.M.E., M.I.N.A.—B. 1868; ed. Liverpool coll.; B. of T. Ist cl. certif.; engnr. marine dept., Lagos, 1899; supt. engrnr., 1902; senr. engrnr., S. Nigeria, 1907; supt. engrnr., 1907.

WALTON, GEORGE O'DONNELL.—Matriculated a student of Lond. Univ., June, 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 17th Nov., 1893; ag. on several occasions as pol. mag., Barbados; judge of the petty debt court of Bridgetown; judge of the asst. ct. of appeal; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. “A” from 21st July, 1902, to 14th Feb., 1903; pol. mag., dist. “F,” 9th Dec., 1902; ag. chief just., St. Lucia, 18th May to 29th Oct., 1908.
WALTON, J. E. LAWSON.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 1910.
WARD, C. E.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 7th Aug., 1909.

WARD, DANIEL, K.C.—Sen. mod. and gold medallist in law, hist. and polit. sci., Trin. Coll., Dublin, 1881; M.A., Dublin, 1887; LL.D., Cape of Good Hope, 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1881; crown prosecutor, Br. Bechuana-land, 1884; asst. legal adviser, atty-gen.'s off., Cape Colony, 1898; asst. legal adviser, atty-gen.'s off., Transvaal, 1902; legal adviser, 1903; mem. of legis. coun. of the Transvaal, 1903; 2nd puisne judge, O.R.O., 23rd Mar., 1904; author of “Practice at Parliamentary Elections,” “The Marriage Laws of the Cape Colony,” “Digest of the Criminal Law,” etc.
WARD, JOHN CORBET.—I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps; King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; dep. regar., E. Africa Prot., 1906; regar. of documents, 1906.

WARD, RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH GEORGE, Bart. (1911), P.C. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1901).—Prime min., col. treas., postmtr.-gen., comsrr. of electric telegraphs, and min. of defence, New Zealand; rep. New Zealand at Impl. confce., 1907 and 1911; mem. of Imp. confce. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; rep. New Zealand at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

WARD, W. F.—Assoc. Roy. Schl. of Mines, Lond. (1876).—Analyst, etc., to the govt. of Tasmania, 1882; asst. to Dr. T. Percy and Prof. W. C. Roberta-Austen, etc., in chemical and metallurgical laboratories of Roy. Schl. of Mines, Royal Mint, and Normal Schl. of Science, S. Kensington (1876-80); special apptmt. to Potosi Gold Mining Co., Venezuela (1881); in charge of chemical testing of smoke-consuming appliances at exhibition, S. Kensington (1881).

WARLORD, ALEXANDER TURNER.—B. 1866; served with S. Stiltsn. pol., 1889-1890; joined B.N. Borneo constab. as storekeeper and orderly-room elk., 1st July, 1890; whilst connected with constab. performed the following duties on various occasions; ag. chief gaoler, sub-editor and in charge of P.W.D., 16th June, 1894; in charge of govt. printing office, 1st Sept., 1894, and of P.W.D., 5th Dec., 1894; second in command of Dyak Jubilee contingent, 1897 (medal); resigned, 30th April, 1898; second in command of Dyak Coronation contingent, 1902 (medal); rejoined the ser. as capt.-supt. of civ. pol., and inspr. of sanitary bd., Sandakan, 2nd Oct., 1902; served in S. African War, wounded and shot four times (medal with five clasps); bronze medal for saving life; B.N. Borneo Company's medal, Mat Sallah expedition (with clasp "Punitive"); apptd. U.S.A. consul agent, Sandakan, 1st July, 1904; J.P. for B.N. Borneo; gen. pub. prosecutor for N. Borneo, 1907.

WARING, EDWARD LENNON.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Crystal Pal. Engnr. Compy. Schl.; astt. engrnr., Uganda rly., 1900-03; astt. ch. survr., E. Africa Prot., 27th July, 1903; dep. dir. of surveys, cadastral branch, 1st Apr., 1906.

WARNER, GEORGE BRUNTON.—Extra clerical astt., G.F.O., P.M. Burg., from Sept. 1st, 1881, to Feb. 16th, 1892; 3rd class clk. civ. ser., and filled the post of 7th clk., G.F.O., Feb. 16th, 1882; as 6th clk., Jan. 18th, 1883; clk., col. engineer's dept., Jan. 13th, 1886; border customs offr., De Jager's Drift, Jan. 8th, 1887; issuer of native passes, Dundee div., Jan. 16th, 1888; sheep inspr., De Jager's Drift, May 7th, 1888; J.P., Dundee div., Dec. 13th, 1892; chief examining offr., Customs Point, Durban, Nov. 21st, 1896; ag. clk. of the court, Newcastle, June 16th, 1900; clk. of the court, Newcastle, Aug. 9th, 1900; J.P., Newcastle div., Aug. 16th, 1900; deputy registrar special court, Newcastle div., Aug. 23rd, 1900, in terms of sec. 25 of Act No. XIV., 1900; has acted as astt. and ag. mag. of Newcastle on many occasions; also for the following periods:—Ag. astt. mag. from Nov. 19th, 1900, to March 24th, 1901; ag. mag. from June 6th, 1901, to Sept. 8th, 1901; ag. astt. mag., Sept. 9th, 1901.
WARREN, A.—Apptd. astt., treasy., O.R.C., 23rd July, 1900; astt. book-keeper, funds dept., 1st July, 1903, acctnt., funds dept., 1st July, 1906.

WARREN, Lieut.-Gen. Sir CHARLES, R.E., K.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1840; ed. Chelt.
